news_summary: Oman signed a mineral exploration agreement with the UK’s Knights Bay to explore for and extract nickel, described as the first deal of its kind with a foreign partner. Initial investment for exploration and evaluation is estimated at $25–$30 million over the first three years, with no near-term output details. | EV Nickel Inc. announces upcoming presentations at PDAC and other events and says it is awaiting assay results from its 2023 W4 drilling later in March. It also discusses clean nickel strategy and a previously released CarLang A Zone maiden resource estimate. No new production or pricing data is provided. | Oman’s Ministry of Energy and Minerals signed a mining concession with Knights Bay for Block 21, giving the UK firm rights to extract high-purity nickel with zero carbon emissions. The project includes exploration and appraisal and plans to process battery minerals (nickel and cobalt) for EV industry demand. | Sky Gold Corp. says the TSX Venture Exchange accepted its 100% acquisition of three nickel-copper-cobalt-platinum group element properties in Ontario. The deal terms include share issuance, cash, and a net smelter/royalty. No production or development timeline is provided, implying limited short-term supply impact for nickel and copper. | Sky Gold Corp. reports that TSX-V has accepted filing for Sky’s 100% acquisition of three nickel-copper-cobalt-PGE properties in northwestern Ontario. The deal terms involve share issuance and cash payments, plus potential additional consideration if a large, viable ore body is identified—no near-term production impact is stated.

news_summary: London copper was set for a weekly gain as stronger-than-expected China manufacturing and services data boosted demand sentiment, while declining SHFE inventories suggested improving tightness. Markets also weighed a firmer dollar and potential additional U.S. sanctions on China. Aluminium rose slightly; nickel fell on the day. | SMM says bonded zone nickel inventory fell 1,000 mt to 5,600 mt week-on-week as of March 3. Nickel plate stock dropped to 4,130 mt, while briquettes were flat at 1,470 mt due to low stainless steel demand. Spot trading picked up slightly amid lower nickel prices. | The UK Financial Conduct Authority launched an enforcement investigation into the London Metal Exchange’s nickel trading halt decision and its systems and controls around Jan. 1 to March 8, 2022. The regulator appears focused on stopping trade, not on canceling trades. Bank of England reviews also flagged LME Clear shortcomings. | Three Chinese migrant workers filed a human-rights complaint alleging abuse and unsafe conditions at a nickel processing estate in Indonesia, including alleged forced overtime, lack of protective equipment, and withholding passports. The company denied involvement with the IMIP Morowali complex. Allegations could heighten scrutiny and disrupt nickel operations.

news_summary: SMM reports Ni 1.5% ore CIF prices at about $72/wmt, steady week-on-week, with low Philippines shipments. Supply tightness is pushing mines to seek higher ore quotes, but NPI demand looks weak: traders restocked earlier, hedgers may lower prices, and NPI plants may reject higher ore costs. SMM expects further NPI declines. | SMM says Chinese nickel ore inventories fell 172,000 wmt week-on-week to 7.0 million wmt (55,000 mt Ni content) by March 3, citing low recent arrivals and rigid NPI purchasing. It expects inventories to keep trending lower, potentially around 6 million before the end of the Philippines rainy season. | Nickel ore arrivals at Chinese ports totaled 498,000 wmt as of Feb 24, up 27% week-on-week, with shipments landing at several major ports. The ore is mainly for delivery to NPI plants in Shandong, Jiangsu and Liaoning. Overall imports remain low and downstream demand is rigid, limiting stock build-up. | SMM reports pricing pressure on NORNICKEL nickel affecting nickel sulphate spreads and near-zero electrowinning profits. Ternary demand is weak and firms prefer cheaper liquid nickel sulphate, while nickel sulphate supply is tight due to delayed Indonesia raw-material line commissioning and late February restarts. Costs support prices; expected range is 39,000–40,000 yuan/mt this week. | SMM reports nickel sulphate enterprises’ margins fell further as demand rose but new MHP capacity commissioning was delayed. Nickel salt plants faced high-priced raw materials and tighter high-grade nickel matte supply (some matte lines switched to NPI). Lower downstream purchase prices pushed firms toward reduced profits or losses. | The UK FCA has opened an enforcement investigation into the LME’s 2022 suspension of nickel trading, examining conduct and controls around 1 Jan to 8 Mar 2022 after a surge in nickel prices linked to Russia-Ukraine supply concerns. The Bank of England also flagged governance and risk-management shortcomings at LME Clear. | Electric Royalties announced it will acquire a 0.5% gross revenue royalty (with options for additional royalty) on the Kenbridge nickel project in Ontario. The project is described as nickel-copper mineralized, with a preliminary economic assessment using assumed Ni and Cu prices, but no near-term production changes are stated. | The UK FCA has opened an investigation into the LME’s March 2022 suspension of nickel futures, reviewing trading activity from early 2022 until the March 8 halt. The suspension followed a brief nickel surge above $100,000/t. FCA highlights transparency and risk management as LME may adjust market structure. | The UK FCA announced an enforcement investigation into some of the LME’s conduct and controls between Jan. 1, 2022 and the nickel contract suspension on March 8, 2022. The article notes the probe relates to the period of disorderly nickel trading and adds ongoing uncertainty for LME nickel confidence. | EV Nickel Inc. announced it received CAD 500,000 from the Ontario government for its “Clean Nickel” strategy, including R&D on bioleaching and carbon capture to produce lower-carbon nickel from its Shaw Dome project near Timmins. The non-dilutive funding supports innovation for EV battery supply. | Canada Nickel reported assay results from the first 12 holes at its Texmont nickel project, finding near-surface, thick, high-grade mineralization with multiple intervals above 1% nickel and remaining open at depth and to the north. Management highlighted potential near-term, smaller open-pit production, supporting bullish sentiment for nickel supply prospects.

news_summary: Hedge funds are suing the London Metal Exchange over the cancellation of billions of dollars of nickel trades after the exchange suspended nickel in March following a violent price spike. The FCA is investigating possible misconduct, while other lawsuits are ongoing, increasing nickel market uncertainty. | Goldman Sachs Commodities Research said lithium prices are expected to fall further and will move lower alongside cobalt and nickel. The note projects a downtrend over the next nine to 12 months, implying bearish sentiment for nickel prices, though the timing extends beyond the immediate weeks focus. | Copper prices edged higher as the dollar weakened and traders anticipated improving China demand from March. LME three-month copper rose slightly, and Shanghai April copper also increased. Aluminium eased on the day and nickel declined, indicating the bullish impulse was strongest for copper rather than industrial metals broadly. | PT Aneka Tambang reported improved unaudited January 2023 performance for its nickel business. Ferronickel production rose to 2,113 tons (+28% YoY) and sales to 2,013 TNi. Nickel ore production reached 1.06 million wmt (+16% YoY) with sales at 1.08 million wmt (+80% YoY). | LME data shows nickel inventories dropping to a multi-year low around 43,884 mt before a slight rebound, then easing again to 44,628 mt. SHFE inventories also widened but rose 14.72% last week to 2,884 mt, indicating tighter LME visibility while domestic stocks recovered.

news_summary: FPX Nickel reportedly received funding from the Government of Canada aimed at scaling up production of nickel and cobalt for EV battery supply chains. Additional support for output expansion can shift expectations for nickel availability and demand dynamics, potentially supporting nickel prices over the next few weeks. | Harita Nickel begins a $600m investor roadshow ahead of a public listing, in a broader Indonesian push to retain nickel processing after the 2019 raw nickel export ban. The country’s EV-battery investments and growing nickel export value support nickel demand/supply expectations, with some peripheral copper IPO plans mentioned. | Aurubis is constructing the Bleed Treatment Olen Beerse (BOB) hydrometallurgical facility to recover copper and nickel from electrolytes/“bleed” streams at its recycling sites, boosting recycling capacity. Production is scheduled for late 2024 with full operations in 2025/26, so near-term price impact is limited despite involvement of both metals. | Aurubis is building the Bleed treatment Olen Beerse (BOB) hydrometallurgical plant to process copper electrolysis bleed and recover metals including copper and nickel. news_summary: SMM reported bonded zone nickel inventory fell 500 mt to 5,100 mt as of March 10. Nickel plate stock declined more amid weak downstream demand, reflecting dropping nickel prices. Nickel briquette demand from stainless steel stayed low, leaving briquette inventories broadly flat. Overall, the inventory move aligns with modest expectations. | Canada announced a ban on importing Russian steel and aluminum as part of sanctions over the Ukraine invasion. Reported additional curtailed trade is expected to be small because Russian shipments had already declined and imports faced prior tariffs, though the move may add some supply-chain pressure. Copper and nickel are not addressed. | Canada will ban imports of Russian steel and aluminum under its sanctions against Moscow. Foreign Affairs Minister Joly ties the policy to weakening Russia’s ability to attack Ukraine and possible regime change. Industry sources say Russia’s Canadian aluminum/steel volumes are small and the overall commercial impact is likely muted, limiting price effects.

news_summary: TMC entered a non-binding MOU with Japan’s PAMCO to evaluate toll processing 1.3 million tonnes/year of polymetallic nodules from 2025. PAMCO would use existing Hachinohe smelting infrastructure to produce nickel-copper-cobalt alloy and manganese silicate, with further study on upgrading to matte. Near-term effects are uncertain due to study-only terms. | European nickel refined premiums for uncut cathode and briquettes rose sharply, especially in-whs Rotterdam, amid tighter regional availability and higher offers/assessments. LME nickel weakness encouraged some consumer return, but liquidity remained limited at top range levels. Cathode premiums stayed high, while US premiums were mostly flat.

news_summary: The LME cancelled nine nickel contracts after “irregularities” at a Rotterdam warehouse, where JPMorgan was said to own the invalidated nickel warrants. The episode sparked wider inspection of LME nickel on warrant across Europe and Asia, though no further issues were found so far, keeping near-term nickel delivery uncertainty elevated. | JPMorgan owned nine LME nickel contracts that were suspended/canceled after irregularities were found at a Rotterdam warehouse, with the issue backed by stone material rather than metal. The LME and warehouses launched widespread nickel verification. While no broader problems were found, deliverability uncertainty is likely negative for nickel prices.

news_summary: LME cancelled nine nickel warrants (54 tonnes) due to failures to meet contract specification after some delivered nickel was found to be stone. Concerns over additional non-conforming metal have damaged delivery confidence, widening steep discounts/contango for nearby nickel contracts versus longer-dated prices. | The LME said it found no further irregularities after cancelling nine nickel warrants (54 tonnes) for non-conforming bagged nickel containing stones. The exchange postponed nickel trading to allow warehouse inspections. Traders are rolling positions and nearby contracts show steep discounts, reflecting nervousness about LME delivery quality. | Fathom Nickel says it has acquired new mineral claims at Watts Lake in Saskatchewan through a cash-and-share deal with SKRR Exploration. The expanded land package triples coverage and connects the historic Gochager Lake nickel deposit, strengthening its nickel exploration position; the claims may also host VMS-style lead-zinc mineralization. | Rusal plans additional London lawsuits involving Nornickel CEO Vladimir Potanin, extending a long-running shareholder dispute tied to dividend protections and alleged diversion of Nornickel subsidiaries. Rusal says action protects shareholders, while sanctions apply to Potanin but not directly to Nornickel. No operational impact is indicated. | LME said that, after completing inspections of bagged nickel warrants in licensed warehouses for irregularities in stocks, Asian-hours nickel trading will resume on Monday (March 27). Trading had been postponed by about a week after the LME found nickel failing to meet contract specifications at a warehouse. | LME said Asian-hours nickel trading will resume on March 27 after a full inspection of bagged nickel warrants in LME-licensed warehouses. Trading had been postponed by a week after nickel failing to meet contract specifications was found earlier. The decision follows irregularities in warehouse stocks and LME contract compliance checks. | The LME said a 100% inspection of all bagged nickel warrants in licensed warehouses found no further irregularities. It expects to resume Asian-hours nickel trading on March 27. The update follows last week’s cancellation of some nickel warrants amid concerns about contaminated or incorrect contents. | Wood Mackenzie says China’s renewed growth would most benefit steel, aluminium and copper, with housing/property recovery lifting construction-linked demand. In a high-growth scenario it estimates additional copper and aluminium consumption and suggests that higher energy demand could trigger supply curtailments that can support nickel and other metals. | Wood Mackenzie expects China’s reopening and faster growth to raise construction-driven demand for copper and aluminium, with additional tonnes forecast for both. In a higher-growth scenario, exporters and stronger activity could support metal prices. It also warns higher energy demand may trigger supply curtailments, potentially boosting nickel as well.

news_summary: SMM reported that pure nickel inventories in six China regions fell by 379 mt to 5,670 mt as of March 24. Nickel briquette inventories were down 50 mt to 580 mt, while nickel plate inventories declined 379 mt to 5,090 mt, driven by downstream demand for on-demand purchases. | Nickel prices rose 4.82% last week despite a Fed rate hike that was not initially bullish. The report links the rally to tightening Chinese spot supply as pure nickel imports incurred losses, while demand for pure nickel stayed weak. Stainless steel mill cut news weighed on NPI, but overall nickel support prevailed. | Reuters reports copper holding a narrow range with small gains on both LME and SHFE as investors weigh global economic headwinds and banking-system worries. LME nickel resumes Asian hours after a prior suspension and cancellations, while LME aluminium rises modestly. Overall moves are mild, implying limited net impact. | Copper traded in a tight range as investors weighed global economic headwinds and banking risks, with LME copper up 0.3%. LME nickel resumed Asian hours after a year-long suspension, but fell 0.6%. SHFE nickel rose sharply, while aluminium was up modestly. | Copper prices held in a tight range, with three-month LME copper up 0.3%. Nickel resumed LME Asian trading after a long suspension; LME nickel fell 0.6% while SHFE nickel rose 3.3%. Aluminium also inched higher on both LME and SHFE amid macro and bank-stability worries. | Copper prices were steady-to-slightly higher on the LME and SHFE as investors weighed metal stocks against global economic headwinds and concerns over the banking system. LME nickel resumed Asian hours after a prior suspension, but fell on the day. Aluminium rose modestly on both exchanges. | Copper held a tight range but posted small gains as falling inventories and improving demand outweighed macro worries. LME nickel slipped after resuming Asian-hours trading, with concerns over liquidity, margins and deliverable constraints. Aluminium was also slightly higher on both LME and SHFE, supported by broader metal firmness. | Reuters reports LME/SHFE copper trading slightly higher on lower inventories and improving demand sentiment despite macro concerns. LME nickel resumes Asian-hours trading after a suspension, but the contract slipped, reflecting volatile/liquidity risks. SHFE aluminium also gained modestly alongside broader base-metals moves. | Copper traded in a tight range but was slightly higher, supported by falling inventories, improving demand, and expectations around a possible pause in U.S. rate hikes. LME nickel resumed Asian hours after a suspension, with the contract slightly lower and liquidity concerns highlighted. Aluminium rose marginally alongside broader metal sentiment. | Copper prices were steady to slightly higher on Monday, supported by falling inventories, improving demand, and a potential pause in U.S. interest-rate hikes. LME nickel resumed Asian-hours trading after a long suspension, easing logistics for arbitrage, though liquidity remains limited. Aluminium also edged up, but without clear catalysts. | LS Group completed construction of a nickel sulfate plant in Asan, operated via affiliate Toricom, producing 5,000 tons for EV batteries. The company plans to expand nickel sulfate capacity to 270,000 tons by 2030 using additional feedstocks, supporting LS MnM’s battery materials value chain and potential nickel demand. | Palladium One reported discovery of the Ember Zone, a high-grade nickel–copper target at its Tyko project in Ontario, with earlier soil/VTEM indications and plans for further geophysical work and drilling. The news may support longer-term Ni/Cu sentiment but lacks immediate supply or production impacts. | Palladium One Mining reports discovery of the “Ember Zone,” a new high-grade nickel–copper mineralized zone at its Tyko project in Ontario, adjacent to interpreted feeder dyke/chonolith structures. The company describes initial geophysical/soil anomalies, planned BHEM surveys, and ongoing exploration drilling. | Minnesota’s DNR will hold a hearing on Monday over a key permit for PolyMet’s NorthMet proposed copper-nickel mine. The review focuses on whether a bentonite-based waste-rock dam design will work, amid ongoing environmental and water-quality permitting disputes that could delay supply. | Minnesota’s DNR will hold a hearing on Monday for a key permit for PolyMet’s NorthMet project, intended to be the state’s first copper-nickel mine. The decision hinges on whether bentonite will work for waste-rock containment. Permitting progress could support near-term copper and nickel sentiment. | The LME said that nickel bags stored in a licensed warehouse contained stones rather than actual nickel. The incident follows previous nickel-related troubles, raising concerns about stock integrity and warehouse controls. Such issues can weigh on confidence and increase perceived risk in near-term nickel supply pricing. | Palladium One announced the discovery of new high-grade nickel in a zone that includes copper. While exploration results may not immediately change production, the news can modestly support sentiment for both nickel and copper prices over the coming days as traders reassess potential future supply. | The LME discovered irregularities in nine nickel contracts after inspections found some delivered “nickel” bags filled with stones. Rotterdam warehouse receipts and warrants tied to JPMorgan were suspended/invalidated, raising delivery/security concerns. Nickel for immediate delivery reportedly plunged to the widest discount to futures in 15 years, pressuring prices. | The LME found “irregularities” in nine nickel contracts invalidated after bags labeled as nickel in Rotterdam were found to contain stones. Warehouse staff are inspecting thousands of bags, and traders are reassessing delivery security. Immediate nickel prices reportedly plunged to the widest discount to 3-month futures in 15 years.

Prices rose after a firm began electrodeposition nickel production using nickel sulphate and demand strengthened in February. By late March, tepid NEV demand recovery reduced buying from ternary precursor plants, creating a supply-demand imbalance and pushing prices down. | SMM reports that SHFE base metals mostly closed higher by less than 1% on March 30, but nickel fell sharply, down 2.53%. Ferrous metals were mixed, with iron ore up 1.91%, while stainless steel dropped 2.5%, highlighting stronger downside pressure on nickel. | Copper rose on LME, supported by signs of recovering Chinese demand, supply disruptions, and a weaker dollar as banking fears eased. Chinese smelters agreed lower Q2 treatment/refining charges, with spot TC/RCs also down, implying tighter concentrate availability and potential market tightening amid low inventories. | Reuters reports the LME’s bid to restore its troubled nickel contract after a 2022 trading halt and cancelled trades. It details FCA and Bank of England involvement, regulatory probes, enhanced monitoring, delivery issues in warehouses, lawsuits, and eventual resumption of Asian-hours trading after inspections found no further irregularities. | LME announced measures to revive its nickel contract after last year’s chaotic trading, including a new lower-grade “class 2” nickel contract with Qianhai Mercantile Exchange, fast-track listings, and a fee waiver to boost liquidity. It also made copper and aluminium price caps tighter. The nickel contract disruption followed a mismatch between high- and low-grade supplies. | Flying Nickel Mining reported initial Minago project drill core assay results, highlighting intersections grading about 1.1–1.5% nickel and small copper percentages, alongside Pt/Pd/Au. The company is continuing a 2023 PGM assay program. The update is primarily sentiment-relevant for nickel, with no direct aluminium link. | Indonesia’s president said the government will strengthen monitoring and require mining and smelter operators to follow best environmental practices. New smelter permits will be approved only if powered by renewable energy, and deadlines will push coal-powered facilities to transition. He also warned against overproduction that could cause price declines. | Ford is joining Tesla in locking up supply of nickel as global EV demand accelerates and strains battery-material supply chains. Auto makers are seeking to secure inputs for EV production, which can tighten nickel availability and support higher short-term nickel pricing expectations. | Ford will invest $4.5B in a joint venture Indonesia nickel processing plant producing 120,000 tons/year of mixed hydroxide precipitate for EV batteries made in Kentucky. The deal is meant to secure nickel supply and ESG standards, reinforcing demand expectations for nickel feedstocks. | Flying Nickel reports initial PGM assay results from its Minago nickel project in Manitoba, with multiple drill intersections grading about 1.1–1.5% nickel over 4–23 meter intervals. The news improves sentiment for nickel resources, while copper is mentioned at low levels. Further assay batches are expected in April. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. hired Nordmin Engineering as it moves the Kenbridge Nickel Project into advanced exploration and project development. The company plans permitting/environmental work, a deep geophysical (MT) survey, additional drilling, and a scoping review to guide expenditures and submissions toward feasibility. No near-term output is specified. | LME announced a two-year plan after the 2022 nickel saga, including fast-track listing and fee waivers to boost liquidity in LME Nickel, potential changes to margin methodology, and making daily price limits permanent. It also plans revised calibration methodology for copper and aluminium daily limits and considers broadening nickel delivery. | Stillwater Critical Minerals filed an NI 43-101 technical report updating resources at its Stillwater West nickel-copper-cobalt project, citing a 60% expansion to 1.6 billion pounds of nickel and copper plus cobalt. The company also outlines upcoming 2023 drilling and research catalysts, but no near-term production changes. | LME proposes measures to revive nickel trading, including shortening approval time for new nickel brands, expanding eligible nickel forms (including nickel powder), enhancing reporting of eligible stocks, and considering changes to margins and trading platform rollout. It also makes permanent daily price limits for all metals, tightening caps for copper and aluminium. | NickelSearch’s MD says the Carlingup nickel sulphide project in Western Australia has an upgraded indicated resource estimate of 8.3 million tonnes at 0.52% nickel, following successful infill drilling. The company notes potential for further shallow open-pit expansion and new discoveries, but timing and output are not specified. | Ford partners with Indonesia’s PT Vale and China’s Huayou to invest in a $4.5 billion Indonesian nickel-processing (HPAL) project, producing MHP for EV batteries. While operations are slated for 2026, the deal signals tighter near-term supply expectations as automakers lock in nickel for EV production targets. | Ford partnered with Zhejiang Huayou Cobalt and PT Vale Indonesia to build a nickel processing facility in Sulawesi using the HPAC method. Funded at $4.5 billion, it is under construction and planned for commercial operation in 2026, aimed at supplying processed nickel for Ford’s U.S. battery production.

The article briefly includes nickel in the same “demand surge” theme. | Copper prices fell on Monday as China’s Caixin manufacturing PMI weakened to the 50.0 level, raising demand concerns. A firmer U.S. dollar tied to renewed inflation worries after oil producers’ output cuts further pressured dollar-priced commodities. LME and SHFE aluminium also eased, while nickel was mixed. | Surge Battery Metals announced it can acquire the remaining 20% interest to reach 100% ownership of its HN4 and N100 mineral claims in central BC, a nickel/iron alloy (awaruite) project. The company also closed a private placement. The update is modestly supportive for nickel sentiment, not immediate supply. | Surge Battery Metals announced it can acquire the remaining 20% interest in the HN4 and N100 mineral claims of its Surge Nickel Project in British Columbia, paying consideration in shares and consolidating to 100% ownership. news_summary: SMM says traded NPI prices dropped sharply last week as stainless steel mills demanded lower NPI amid their losses. Intensive Indonesian NPI arrivals led traders to dump inventory, pressuring prices. Nickel ore prices may fall further, and high-grade NPI discounts versus pure nickel are expected to widen this week, weighing on nickel. | Mallee Resources’ bid to relist on the ASX has been delayed because it has not yet received the full ~$15.33 million owed from a Myanmar-related sale. Payment timing may push funding/financing options beyond late September, adding uncertainty around ongoing operations at its Avebury nickel mine near Zeehan. | London copper eased as rising supply and economic pressure offset support from a weaker U.S. dollar ahead of inflation data. Antaike cited a 14% year-on-year jump in China refined output. Aluminium rose modestly amid increasing Russian LME stock share; nickel and other metals were mixed. | Copper prices steadied at a one-week high after China posted stronger-than-expected bank lending and faster credit growth, supported by continued policy measures post-COVID curbs. Rising copper supply expectations tempered gains. The same macro tone lifted LME aluminium and nickel slightly, alongside cautious sentiment ahead of U.S. inflation data. | Copper climbed on Wednesday, helped by a weaker U.S. dollar and stronger-than-expected Chinese credit growth ahead of inflation data. However, gains were tempered by rising refined copper output in China, driven by new capacity and adequate raw-material supply. LME aluminium and nickel also rose alongside copper sentiment. | London copper prices fell from a one-week high as China’s refined output rose 14% year-on-year, increasing supply, while macro-economic pressure weighed on demand expectations. The weaker U.S. dollar offered some support. Aluminium was slightly higher, while nickel traded with a larger swing amid mixed broader base-metals pricing. | Western Copper and Gold said Rio Tinto will exercise its participation right in full, subscribing for 878,809 shares and keeping its ~7.84% stake, alongside Mitsubishi’s larger investment. The company is developing the Casino copper-gold project, implying marginally improved copper project funding visibility near term. | Nickel 28 Capital Corp. adopted a shareholder rights plan to deter “creeping” bids and urged shareholders not to tender to Pelham’s conditional mini-tender, which the board says undervalues the company. Nickel 28 produces nickel-cobalt at Ramu, but the release lacks operational or supply changes impacting nickel prices. | SMM reports Shanghai spot nickel premiums easing: Jinchuan nickel averaged 8,250 yuan/mt (down 500) and NORNICKEL averaged 7,250 yuan/mt (down 250). This decline is attributed to slightly firmer downstream demand but rising nickel futures. Nickel briquette prices rose, while transaction activity stayed slack versus nickel sulphate. | SMM reports nickel ore (Ni 1.5% CIF) prices fell slightly in March, with shipments from the Philippines remaining low after the rainy season. Poor demand in China pushed NPI prices down, discouraging some buyers and leading traders to cut nickel ore quotes to facilitate shipping. | Latin Metals Inc. said mapping and prospecting at its Auquis Project in Peru identified two new mineralized zones, including porphyry-style copper and skarn mineralization. Grab samples reported high-grade copper in multiple areas, with associated silver and some lead/zinc. No aluminium or nickel impact is mentioned. | Advance United reports final drill assay results from its Buck Lake VMS property, highlighting high-grade copper intersections (e.g., 4.59% Cu over 3.2 m) and associated silver and zinc values. This exploration update may slightly influence copper sentiment, but lacks production/inventory implications, so near-term price impact is likely limited. | FinnCobalt’s Hautalampi Ni-Co-Cu project in Finland (about 4.56 Mt at 0.30% Ni and 0.24% Cu) is being developed by Eurobattery Minerals after stake acquisitions. The pre-feasibility study outlines plant design and flotation circuits for copper and nickel. It is project-stage, with limited short-term supply impact. | EV Nickel filed an NI 43-101 technical report for its CarLang “A Zone” maiden resource, estimating 1.25M tonnes indicated and 1.16M tonnes inferred contained nickel (with Co as by-product). The deposit has meaningful nickel and copper reserves and planned flotation processing, but it is still at development stage, limiting short-term supply impact on prices. | Harita Nickel (PT Trimegah Bangun Persada) raised about $673 million in a 2023 Indonesia’s biggest IPO. The move follows Indonesia’s push to expand EV-related nickel capacity. | Premium Nickel Resources filed an NI 43-101 technical report for its Selkirk Ni-Cu project in Botswana, outlining historic resources and metallurgical work. The company plans additional 2023 exploration/resource drilling and aims for a new compliant resource in Q1 2024, with possible future economic evaluation for an open pit.

news_summary: China imported 1.48 million mt of nickel ore in February, up 4.31% month-on-month and 19.26% year-on-year, with higher volumes from the Philippines as rainy-season disruption eased. Port stocks stayed down, but high spot supply and weak demand pushed NPI prices lower, pressuring nickel ore prices, with further growth expected in March. | Mitsubishi’s CEO said the company is considering investments in nickel and lithium to support electrification-driven demand. It already holds a stake in a Canadian nickel venture and is assessing nickel project profitability. Mitsubishi also flagged potential reallocation of coal-mine sale proceeds into growth areas like copper and battery metals. | Canada Nickel reported positive preliminary drill results at its Midlothian property, including near-surface serpentinized dunite with nickel-iron alloy awaruite (up to ~0.49% awaruite in sampled intervals) and high brucite content. The larger target footprint versus Crawford may improve nickel project upside. No assays are finalized. | Fathom Nickel reported assay results from two drill holes at the Gochager Lake Property, intersecting high-grade nickel-bearing mineralization and testing for cobalt. One section graded 0.22% over 4.2 meters. The company plans a larger drill program later this year, reinforcing positive near-term nickel exploration expectations.

news_summary: SMM reports firm premiums for NORNICKEL nickel due to warrant delivery and no cleared spots flowing into China. Nickel demand improves with 300-series stainless and alloy output, supporting prices. However, projected electrowinning output growth amid recession-related macro concerns is expected to add supply and pressure nickel prices. | As of April 7, Chinese ports received 770,000 Wmt of nickel ore, up 15% week-on-week. Arrivals increased at Yangjiang, Jinzhou, Ningde and Lanshan. Nickel ore prices dipped slightly on rising NPI prices, while Philippine mines resumed operations slowly, with further arrivals expected in late April. | SMM reports Apr 17 spot nickel trading was slack. Jinchuan nickel premiums rose to an average of 6,900 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL premiums averaged 6,750 yuan/mt. SHFE nickel prices continued higher and premiums stayed firm, but intraday spot and briquette transactions were weak, keeping price momentum muted. | Nickel ore (Ni 1.5%) CIF prices in China slipped slightly to $52/wmt as Philippines supply remains limited because many mines haven’t started. NPI prices rose to 1,052 yuan/mtu WoW, but NPI plants hesitate due to unpredictable price trends and low purchase offers, keeping ore quotes firm. Short-term ore pricing likely stable. | SMM reports nickel sulphate enterprises shipping at lower levels due to slack demand and low profit margins. April production schedules are planned to dip 15.93% month-on-month, while nickel briquette premiums over nickel sulphate previously widened sharply. Slightly sufficient raw-material supply may push raw-material producers to cut quotes, weighing on nickel-linked prices. | The piece reviews nickel supply and demand for EVs, noting Indonesian production growth and muted near-term demand from a slow Chinese reopening and recession fears, which weigh on nickel prices. It also highlights 2022’s extreme nickel volatility tied to the Ukraine conflict and a longer-term EV demand outlook through 2030.

news_summary: Copper rose on Tuesday after faster-than-expected Chinese GDP growth boosted expectations for near-term demand. While some indicators pointed to softer factory orders later in April/May, the data still supported prices. LME aluminium gained on warehouse inflows and March output, and nickel was also higher as industrial metals moved together. | SMM said April 18 spot nickel trading in Shanghai remained sluggish. Jinchuan nickel premiums averaged 6,150 yuan/mt, down 750 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL averaged 6,250 yuan/mt, down 500. Despite lower premiums, NORNICKEL supply was still tight and premiums remained firm. Nickel briquette transactions were slack.

news_summary: Copper dipped in Asia as traders weighed the Fed’s outlook for further interest rate hikes, supporting a firmer dollar and pressuring dollar-priced industrial metals. LME tin retreated after Myanmar mining suspension concerns eased. LME aluminium and nickel also eased, with SHFE aluminium and nickel showing opposing gains. | Copper fell in Asian trading as traders weighed prospects of additional U.S. Federal Reserve rate hikes, supporting the dollar and pressuring non-ferrous prices. Three-month LME copper declined and Shanghai copper ended lower. LME aluminium dropped and nickel was little changed, reflecting overall market risk sentiment tied to Fed outlook. | SMM’s midday market wrap notes the dollar was slightly higher and oil slightly lower. SHFE base metals broadly rose, with nickel up 4.36% and aluminium also increasing. In contrast, LME base metals fell across the board. The piece is descriptive and lacks specific drivers for nickel and aluminium. | SMM reports that ferronickel prices for small orders fell as stainless-steel mill demand weakened and NPI prices dropped quickly. While higher-Ni ferronickel (≥35%) stayed firm, 20–25% and <20% grades declined, with some trading tied to NPI averages. The sharp fall hindered trader shipments, though supply is expected to remain relatively scarce into Q2. | Palladium One reported wide intercepts of nickel-copper mineralization at its Tyko Project’s West Pickle Zone, including multiple holes with ~0.6% Ni and ~0.4% Cu over ~28 m, plus higher-grade sections. The zone remains open for expansion toward the RJ Zone, supporting potential future Ni and Cu resources. | Palladium One reported final 2022 drill results from the Tyko nickel-copper project in Ontario, including wide intercepts of nickel and copper sulphides at the West Pickle zone and indications the zone remains open for expansion. The update is exploration-focused and does not change near-term production or inventories. | Nickel 28 says it has received confirmation of a US$9.7 million cash distribution and US$18.1 million debt repayment from its Ramu nickel-cobalt joint venture, driven by H2 2022 operating performance. The company plans to resume its normal course issuer bid to repurchase shares. Ramu timing is expected in April 2023. | Lifezone Metals says it has commenced an off-take marketing process for nickel and copper (and cobalt) from the Kabanga nickel project, retaining marketing rights up to 40% of contained production. The firm expects demand from battery and EV supply chains and engaged RBC Capital Markets to run the process. | Surge Copper reported final 2022 reconnaissance drill and sampling results for its Ootsa-Berg projects, highlighting expanded copper anomalies and multiple copper intersections at targets like Bergette and Sibola. It also outlined initial 2023 exploration plans. The news is supportive for long-run copper expectations but unlikely to move near-term prices materially. | Lifezone Metals says it has started an off-take marketing process for nickel (and copper) from the Kabanga nickel project, engaging RBC Capital Markets to monetize up to 40% of contained production. The step supports expectations for future supply contracts and could modestly lift nickel and copper sentiment near term. | China’s Zhejiang Huayou Cobalt is reportedly seeking to build the Philippines’ third nickel ore processing facility with a local miner. The project would expand domestic processing of nickel ore used in stainless steel and EV battery supply chains, potentially improving processing capacity and influencing nickel supply expectations. | Québec Nickel reports long core intervals of serpentinized dunite/peridotite at its Ducros Ni-Cu-PGE project, with results indicating a potentially large nickel-cobalt exploration target. Native copper is mentioned on fracture surfaces and historical drilling grades include nickel and cobalt. It remains exploration with no immediate supply impact, limiting price movement. | Philippines’ Board of Investments says two firms will invest up to $10 billion in nickel processing plants in Leyte. The projects, aimed at supplying nickel for electric-battery precursor production, could boost local processing capacity and value-added exports, improving market sentiment for nickel demand and supply balance in the near term. | Talon Metals reported high-grade nickel assays (up to 6.93%) from six holes at the newly identified “Raptor Zone” of the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt Project in Minnesota. The company says results suggest a new nickel-copper system and plans further drilling and related permitting. | The update round-up includes First Class Metals’ West Pickle Lake drilling results, claiming wide intercepts with ~10% nickel and ~4.5% copper. The news may modestly improve sentiment around future nickel and copper supply prospects, but it does not indicate near-term production, refining, or inventory impacts, limiting price effects. | Palladium One reported drill intercepts at its Tyko Project’s West Pickle Zone showing wide nickel-copper sulphide mineralization, including grades up to 2%+ Ni and multi-percent Cu in selected sections, and extensions of strike length toward the RJ Zone. The update supports potential incremental Ni/Cu resource outlook.

news_summary: The piece discusses copper and nickel outlooks via broker commentary. Copper is supported by declining LME stockpiles and electrification/renewables themes, but demand risks rise from weaker US and China activity. Nickel sentiment is helped by LME stockpiles at a 14-year low, though no new supply disruption is detailed. | Australia’s foreign minister says expanded cooperation with New Caledonia will include critical mineral mining, explicitly citing future opportunities from nickel mining. A labour mobility program may also expand to cover the French territory, aiming to address labour shortages and promote responsible mining. Nuclear submarine discussions focus on compliance, not metals supply. | Copper prices fell after a Federal Reserve policymaker suggested more U.S. rate hikes because inflation remains “problematic.” The move supported a stronger dollar effect on dollar-priced metals. The article also reported declines in LME aluminium and nickel, consistent with a broader risk-off, tighter-monetary impulse. | Glencore has signed loan documentation and an amended sales agreement to provide up to $22m funding for Pacific Nickel’s Kolosori nickel project in the Solomon Islands. The loan supports pre-production needs, with drawdowns expected imminently, and Glencore to acquire all nickel output for six years. | The Stainless Monthly Metals Index fell sharply, and the article links continued downside in nickel to a surplus shift reported by the INSG. A 112,000-ton surplus, boosted by Indonesia’s higher production (mostly Class 2), pressures stainless demand sentiment despite lower LME inventories and limited benchmark deliverability. | BHP downgraded production guidance for its Escondida mine in northern Chile, forecasting lower copper output for the year through June and reducing its annual nickel production estimate, citing heavy rainfall. Despite weaker Escondida copper, BHP kept overall group copper guidance unchanged. Guidance cuts are typically supply-negative. | BHP cut its FY guidance for Escondida in northern Chile, lowering expected copper output for the year through June to 1.05–1.08 million tons from 1.08–1.18 million. It also trimmed nickel production due partly to heavy rainfall, signaling weaker near-term supply for both metals.

news_summary: IGO agreed to farm into Venus Metals’ Henderson nickel-lithium project in Western Australia, funding exploration through a pre-feasibility study and potentially acquiring up to 70% of the tenements. The deal, including a $1m reimbursement, reinforces near-term development activity for nickel targets and could support sentiment. | IGO will fund exploration for Venus’ Henderson project in WA under a farm-in/JV arrangement, with potential to acquire up to 70% after a pre-feasibility study. The project is nickel- and lithium-bearing, but remains at exploration stage, so near-term nickel price impact is likely limited. | 6K Additive and Siemens Energy signed a long-term supply agreement for Siemens to deliver spent nickel alloy powder (revert streams) to 6K Additive. 6K Additive will upcycle the material into additive-manufacturing-ready nickel alloy powders via its UniMelt microwave plasma system. | The Market Herald released interviews featuring multiple companies, including SPC Nickel reporting positive new assay results from its West Graham nickel-copper drilling in Sudbury and Candente Copper outlining 2023 plans for its Cañariaco copper project in Peru. These updates are supportive for near-term expectations around copper and nickel supply prospects. | The Market Herald publishes interviews featuring multiple companies. Of note, SPC Nickel reports positive assay results from drilling at its West Graham Nickel and Copper project in Sudbury, supporting nickel and copper development sentiment. Candente Copper also outlines plans for its 100% owned Cañariaco Copper Project in Peru.

news_summary: POSCO Holdings plans a $441 million nickel refining plant in Indonesia’s Weda Bay Industrial Park, targeting about 52,000 tons of nickel matte annually. Construction begins this year with production in 2025. The move expands POSCO’s nickel supply chain for secondary battery demand and EV growth. | Nickel 28 reported Q1 2023 performance from its Ramu nickel-cobalt operation, including higher contained nickel production and a large jump in nickel sales versus the prior-year quarter. It also cited improved cash costs and received a US$9.7 million distribution while reducing construction debt. Overall signals supportive near-term nickel fundamentals. | FPX Nickel reports drilling and confirmatory geotechnical results for its Baptiste nickel project and outlines multiple awaruite nickel target advancements at the Klow property in British Columbia. The company also begins environmental assessment work and completes pilot testwork to produce concentrate, aiming to support discussions with EV battery supply partners. | Indonesia plans to stop tax holidays for investments into nickel pig iron (NPI), saying downstreaming should reach higher nickel content and not only intermediate products. The move targets lower-grade smelter expansion while the global nickel market faces major oversupply, with Indonesia output surging and capacity additions from TBP and Merdeka. | Indonesia’s minister says the government will cut tax breaks meant to attract nickel investment, specifically to discourage projects deemed low quality. The policy change is expected to reduce or delay some nickel-capacity development, weighing on near-term nickel supply expectations and pressuring nickel prices lower over coming weeks. | InZinc Mining outlines a fully funded 2023 exploration program at its Indy project, following 2022 drilling that intersected nickel-cobalt-copper and minor copper in oxide material, plus new nickel-cobalt-copper targets and zinc targets. The article is about exploration groundwork and drilling timing, not immediate production effects. | Copper held steady on the LME as investors awaited the Fed’s rate decision, balancing nervous macro sentiment against a tight longer-term supply outlook. On-warrant LME stocks rose to the highest in nearly four months. Aluminium slipped and nickel rose modestly amid broader commodity pressure from tighter policy fears. | EV Nickel reports preliminary bench-scale bioleaching tests on its W4 nickel sulphides, using locally sourced bacteria, achieving nickel extraction of 86.0%–90.5% over 8–12 days. The company says Tank Bioleach could reduce smelter/refiner reliance and acid use, improving prospects for domestic nickel product. | Copper is expected to drift higher but remain rangebound amid weaker-than-expected China demand and falling LME stocks, alongside mine-output disruption risks trimming surplus estimates. Aluminium faces softer sentiment from increased output and rising inventories in China. Nickel is pressured by concerns over rising Indonesian supply and broad nickel-bearing exports. | US Senator Rubio asked multiple US agencies to investigate Ford’s Chinese partnership tied to an Indonesia nickel processing plant, arguing it threatens national security. The probe could create regulatory and legal uncertainty around the venture, potentially delaying nickel processing and pressuring short-term nickel supply expectations. | U.S. Senator Marco Rubio asked the Biden administration to investigate Ford’s Indonesia nickel processing plant in partnership with PT Vale Indonesia and China’s Zhejiang Huayou. Rubio alleges national security concerns linked to China’s Belt and Road strategy. The probe could disrupt expectations for nickel processing timelines and demand/supply balance. | U.S. Senator Marcio Rubio asked the Biden administration to investigate Ford’s plan with Vale Indonesia and China’s Huayou to build a $4.5 billion nickel processing plant in Indonesia. The probe raises potential delays or disruptions to nickel supply development, adding near-term downside risk for nickel markets. | A Reuters poll expects copper to recover slightly but remain rangebound, with low inventories supporting prices while weak China physical demand and recession fears cap gains; aluminium is pressured by higher output and rising inventories; nickel is bearish on concerns of expanding Indonesian supply and growing market surpluses.

news_summary: SMM reports May 4 spot pure nickel premiums versus SHFE 2306 falling, with Jinchuan and NORNICKEL premiums still high in absolute terms. SHFE nickel rose early, tracking overseas nickel futures, but premiums drifted lower and spot trades missed expectations. Nickel briquette demand was slack as prices stayed above nickel sulphate premiums. | Indonesia is cutting tax holidays for investment in low-quality nickel products like NPI, aiming to redirect spending toward higher-value downstream processing and reserve nickel for EV-battery materials. The move may slow incremental supply, but the market already faces major Indonesian oversupply, with INSG projecting a sizable surplus this year. | SMM reports nickel prices declined last week as macro factors and improving supply reduced tightness. Imported pure nickel premiums fell sharply after spot inflows, failing to boost demand, while Jinchuan demand held better. Civil/high-temperature alloy demand was soft, leaving no strong support for nickel. | Copper prices rose after the dollar weakened, but gains were capped by weaker-than-expected China demand signals, including contracting factory activity. Analysts highlighted reduced bullish positioning and bearish forecasts. Aluminium was nearly unchanged, while nickel rose modestly, reflecting limited broader momentum beyond copper. | SMM notes a rebound in industrial metals alongside weaker oil and a steady dollar. SHFE nickel jumped 2.85%, with SHFE copper up slightly and SHFE aluminium down marginally. LME copper gained 1.26% and LME nickel rose 0.65%, while LME aluminium was slightly higher, suggesting mild bullish bias. | Flying Nickel Mining Corp. released additional drilling assay results from its Minago Nickel PGM project in Manitoba, reporting multiple drill intervals with nickel grades (about 0.25% to 1.61% Ni) plus platinum and palladium. The update supports ongoing resource/resource-definition work, with no immediate change to production or market supply flows. | Fortune Nickel and Gold provides an update on exploration drilling plans for its Gowan Township project in Ontario, including recommending and collecting a helicopter-borne airborne TDEM survey to refine targets for future drilling. The company cites nickel prospects and does not report resource or production changes. | CanAlaska signed a definitive agreement to acquire the Mel nickel deposit and adjacent claims in Manitoba, subject to TSX Venture approval. The company positions the asset as part of a district-scale sulphide nickel portfolio near Vale’s Thompson nickel operations. The release frames future nickel demand but provides no immediate supply impact. | CanAlaska Uranium signed an agreement to acquire the Mel nickel deposit and adjacent mineral claims in Manitoba’s Thompson Nickel Belt. The company frames the deal as adding a high-grade nickel resource near Vale’s operations. The announcement is subject to TSX Venture approval, with no near-term output changes described.

It simultaneously raised its copper price forecasts for 2023-2025, implying improved outlook. Overall, the revisions point to mixed metal fundamentals rather than a single immediate directional move. | Citi Research cut its 2023-2024 price forecasts for nickel and aluminium, lowering nickel and multiple aluminium annual averages. The update may affect market sentiment, but it is a projection change rather than a direct near-term supply or demand disruption. | Citi Research lowered its 2023-2024 forecasts for nickel and aluminium, reducing nickel and aluminium price expectations for 2023-2024. It simultaneously raised copper forecasts for 2023-2025, increasing expected copper prices. The note is about research revisions rather than immediate supply-demand shocks. | Power Nickel closed the final tranche of its private placement, raising CAD 516,000 to fund Quebec exploration at its NISK Ni-Cu sulfide project, including planned assays and metallurgical work. The release highlights Critical Mineral incentives and expects multiple near-term results. This can modestly support nickel sentiment, with limited copper spillover. | LME nickel stocks fell 28% since early January to 39,966 tonnes after small withdrawals from Rotterdam, suggesting tighter LME-deliverable supply. The nickel forward curve remains in persistent contango and contracts show liquidity concerns and potential volatility. However, weak demand keeps physical premiums under pressure globally. | Posco will invest $440m to build a nickel smelter in Indonesia’s Halmahera, targeting 52,000 tons per year of nickel matte (70–75% nickel) for EV battery intermediates. Nickel dispersion also slipped. | SMM notes crude oil prices gained over 1% and the U.S. dollar index edged lower. news_summary: SHFE nickel traded rangebound as downstream firms were cautious about restocking after Labour Day. NPI holders stayed slower to ship despite profits. Mild gains in stainless steel spot and HRC aided demand, but #316L fell sharply. Overall tight domestic and abroad nickel spot supply keeps prices supported, so SHFE nickel should remain rangebound. | Brokers flag pressure across industrial raw materials. Citi turns bearish on copper, expecting slower China demand and weaker US/Europe consumption amid high inventories and disappointing orders, trimming the 0–3 month copper forecast. Macquarie expects a large global nickel surplus, with nickel pig iron and sulphate oversupply widening discounts versus LME. | Citi turns bearish on copper, citing softer global PMI readings, weaker China consumption expectations, high/rising inventories in the US and Europe, and continued order disappointment, cutting its near-term copper estimate. Separately, Macquarie expects a large nickel production surplus, with nickel pig iron and sulphate in oversupply and trading at discounts. | Copper steadied but sentiment weakened as China data pointed to softer growth and demand, with April imports down 12.5% and manufacturing activity slipping. LME-approved copper inventories rose 40% since April 18 and a firmer dollar weighed on prices. Nickel fell on rising supplies; aluminium edged up modestly. | Environmental group WALHI and others urge Tesla to stop or limit direct investment and use of Indonesia’s nickel, citing pollution, smelter emissions, and alleged rights violations affecting fishing communities. With Indonesia a major nickel supplier, the scrutiny could increase compliance or disruption risk, weighing on nickel market sentiment.

news_summary: Copper prices rose on a weaker U.S. dollar ahead of U.S. inflation data, but gains were limited by expectations of subdued demand in Q2 and rising supply. Peru’s March copper output jumped 20.4% after mine stoppages. Aluminium and nickel were roughly steady to mixed as traders waited for CPI. | SMM reports China’s refined nickel output at 17,600 mt in April, slightly higher month-on-month, after offsetting shutdown/overhaul effects with production increases in Jiangsu. May output is forecast to rise to 17,900 mt, supported by continued Jiangsu ramp-ups and normal operations in Guangxi, suggesting added supply pressure. | SMM reported that May 10 spot nickel transactions were sluggish. Jinchuan nickel premiums rose slightly, but NORNICKEL premiums fell, with nickel prices moving down. Nickel briquette trades were slack and priced at premiums over nickel sulphate, reflecting weaker near-term demand conditions for nickel. | Macarthur Minerals provides an update on the Lake Giles tenements, where ultramafic komatiite-type rocks and serpentinised olivine/talc-carbonate formations are viewed as having high potential for nickel sulphides. The company plans nickel-targeted drilling, EM surveys, and assaying to refine prospects, indicating improved exploration focus. | Québec Nickel Corp. initiated scoping metallurgical tests at its Ducros project to assess whether the Fortin Sill Zone can produce a saleable Ni-Cu-Co-PGE+Au concentrate. SGS will run grindability, flotation, and separation tests using 150 kg of drill core, with results expected later in Q4 2023.

news_summary: China’s GEM plans a $500 million joint project in Indonesia’s Morowali Industrial Park to produce 20,000 tonnes per year of nickel intermediate product for the new energy sector. Two rigs will mobilize and 25–30 holes are planned, with results pending.

The move follows cases where nickel delivered to traders proved to be stone and earlier irregularities in LME-warranted stocks.

news_summary: Benchmark London copper prices fell in Asian trading as the U.S. dollar firmed to a five-week high and investors worried about weaker China demand ahead of industrial activity data. Rising inventories and a slowdown in global growth pressured sentiment. LME aluminium and nickel also declined. | Copper rebounded after a five-month low as China’s PBOC injected liquidity, signaling support for economic growth and improving base-metal sentiment. The weaker dollar and “bargain hunting” aided prices. Aluminium rose on lower LME on-warrant stocks, while nickel weakened on vulnerability concerns and forecast cuts. | Emerita Resources reported assay results from 21 drill holes at its Iberian Belt West La Infanta deposit, highlighting polymetallic massive sulphide intervals including ~1.4% copper over 4.2m in one hole. The company also received a permit for Infanta Sur to start exploration. No nickel or aluminium impact is indicated. | Nickel Asia reported Q1 net income down 8%, citing foreign exchange losses and a lower weighted average nickel ore price. The move suggests weaker nickel ore pricing during the quarter, which can influence short-term sentiment about nickel supply/demand and producer cost conditions.

The move targets EV-battery demand and rising preferences for lower-emissions aluminium, but no near-term supply or inventory change is cited. | SMM reports that Chinese ports received 2.67 million wmt of nickel ore in April, up 42% month-on-month, while Philippine exports rose to 2.36 million wmt, up 52%. The monsoon disruption eased and mining recovered, with demand picking up at the same time. May shipments are expected to rise further. | China’s nickel ore imports rebounded in March to 2.12 million mt (+42.9% MoM), with laterite ore and higher shipments from the Philippines after monsoon disruptions. Nickel sulphide ore imports also rose. Domestic port inventories increased, and April imports are expected to grow as NPI supply tightens. | A Thomson Reuters Foundation report warns that nickel mining for EV battery supply chains in Indonesia and the Philippines involves environmental damage and alleged human-rights abuses. It also updates plans for the Taylor Brook project with deeper drilling under prior high-grade Ni/Cu intersections. No production or confirmed new resources.

news_summary: Reuters reports copper fell on deteriorating demand expectations in China, alongside rising supplies and climbing LME stocks (up 80% since mid-April). Mine resumptions in Peru and a DRC royalty agreement supporting copper exports eased supply worries, widening the cash discount. Other metals were mixed; aluminum slid and nickel edged up. | Australian Adavale Resources secured a prospecting licence for its Kabanga Jirani nickel project in Tanzania and plans additional drilling, DHEM, and AMT surveys. The project is within the Luhuma trend with historic nickel sulphide intersections, while recent work also defined copper-bearing soil anomalies near an adjacent Kabanga deposit.

However, trader stocks increased due to imported spot inflows tied to long-term orders. Spot prices for warrants were below market sources as futures fell and mills reduced shipments, driving down inventory. Overall, imported inflows may pressure nickel lower. | Crude oil prices rose as expectations shifted on OPEC+ production-cut signals, while the U.S. dollar weakened. Reflecting this risk-on macro backdrop, SHFE and LME base metals increased broadly: SHFE aluminium +3.38%, copper +2.17%, nickel +1.27%; LME aluminium +0.85%, copper +1.31%, nickel +1.29%. | SMM reported that spot nickel premiums fell at month-end on May 26. Jinchuan nickel premiums averaged 10,050 yuan/mt, down 600 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL averaged 6,900 yuan/mt, also down 600 yuan/mt. Nickel briquette prices rose slightly, but transactions were slack amid continued premiums versus nickel sulphate. | Lifezone Metals has started offtake marketing for products from its Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania, targeting EV companies for nickel, cobalt and copper output. The feasibility work continues and no deal is agreed, but progress toward commercialisation and processing plans supports bullish near-term sentiment for nickel. | China’s refined nickel imports fell to 3,204 tonnes in April, the lowest since 2004, with imports from Indonesia and Russia also dropping sharply. Chinese stainless steel mills are shifting toward nickel pig iron (NPI) rather than Class I refined nickel, and EV battery growth may further reduce demand for high-purity nickel. | G7 expanded sanctions on Russia, reducing Russian nickel exports to markets like the Netherlands and the US while shifting flows to other buyers (notably Canada and China). Additional assays are expected, with diamond drilling planned for early June, but no production timing is provided.

The piece also notes rises in Shanghai and LME aluminium and nickel, reflecting supportive demand expectations. | Hyphen Trading, linked to the Reuben brothers, sued Trafigura over claims said to be tied to an alleged nickel fraud case. Trafigura previously booked a $590 million impairment for purported systematic fraud involving nickel cargoes. The dispute suggests wider fallout and heightened risk concerns for nickel buyers and traders.

news_summary: The London Metal Exchange is defending itself in court against lawsuits claiming it unlawfully cancelled nickel trades during a March 8, 2022 price surge that led to a trading suspension. LME says actions were lawful to prevent clearing-house defaults. The dispute may influence market sentiment, but no new nickel fundamentals are cited. | London copper fell after a three-week rally as a firmer U.S. dollar reduced demand and data showed higher China refined copper production and rising LME inventories. SHFE stocks rose less, while aluminium and nickel prices also eased in LME and SHFE trading on the day. | London copper fell as investors assessed tepid near-term demand despite record-level production in China. LME stockpiles rose, offset by tight SHFE supplies and limited downside room cited by some analysts. Aluminium and nickel also declined on Monday, reflecting softer overall sentiment across industrial metals. | Appian Capital Advisory is selling two Brazilian subsidiaries, Atlantic Nickel and Mineração Vale Verde, associated with a nickel-copper mine. The acquisition is funded partly by Glencore and Volkswagen as well as other backers, totaling about $1 billion. The transaction could influence supply expectations but lacks short-term output implications. | Appian Capital Advisory is selling its Brazilian mine subsidiaries Atlantic Nickel and Mineração Vale Verde to ACG Acquisition, funded in part by Glencore and Volkswagen. The assets are described as a nickel-copper mine in Brazil, linking the transaction to expectations for future supply of both metals. | Chile’s President Boric tasked Codelco to lead lithium development while boosting copper output, as copper prices remain near multi-decade lows. Codelco’s internal turmoil after CEO Andre Sougarret’s resignation and uncertainty over whether lithium negotiations will absorb resources cloud near-term copper production prospects. | Platinex announced exploration permits for twelve drill pads and authorization for Phase 1 work on its W2 copper-nickel-PGE project near Ontario’s Ring of Fire. The company frames the permits as enabling efforts to prove Cu-Ni-PGE potential. Assays and drilling results are pending, with no immediate production impact mentioned. | Platinex said Ontario issued exploration permits for 12 drill pads at its W2 copper-nickel-PGE project near the Ring of Fire, enabling Phase 1 drilling. The company also updated sampling and permit steps for its gold projects. No production changes or immediate supply figures were provided. | Resource Mining Corporation begins its maiden reverse-circulation drilling program at the Liparamba nickel project in Tanzania, targeting nickel sulphide mineralisation along a southern corridor. The program is expected to run four to five weeks, with results in the next quarter. The news is exploration-focused with no production figures. | The LME will face a court showdown over voided nickel trades from March 2022, when it annulled about $12 billion in deals after a record nickel price surge. FCA scrutiny preceded the case. Elliott Associates and Jane Street seek damages, while LME argues its actions were lawful and systemic-risk driven.

news_summary: Copper prices fell on concerns about the lack of clear economic stimulus in China, alongside a firmer U.S. dollar and thin trading during a U.S. holiday. Falling Shanghai Futures Exchange-monitored stocks provided limited support. Aluminium and nickel also dropped on the same day’s demand sentiment. | Reuters reports that Elliott Associates and Jane Street Global Trading are suing the LME over the cancellation of nickel trades on March 8, 2023 after nickel’s benchmark price doubled within hours. They seek $472 million in damages, arguing the exchange had less disruptive options to manage margin calls and market disorder. | Copper prices slipped on Tuesday as disappointing disappointment with China’s rate cuts hurt industrial-metal sentiment. The softer U.S. dollar provided some support, but copper still fell on the LME. Aluminium and nickel were also down, reflecting weaker broad risk appetite ahead of upcoming Chinese data and technical levels. | The piece’s headline claims LME trading “chaos” in nickel following the lifting of circuit breakers. Such actions usually reflect stressed market conditions and can amplify volatility, with a risk of short-term bearish price pressure as participants react to unstable liquidity and repricing. | Centaurus Metals agreed with Vale Base Metals to acquire offtake rights for the Jaguar nickel sulphide project in Brazil. Vale will waive its rights in exchange for higher net operating royalties linked to nickel sulphate and nickel concentrate. Centaurus cites rising battery-material demand and expects improved control over offtake options, mainly affecting nickel, with no clear immediate supply impact. | Indonesia’s President Joko Widodo said the country will stop exporting copper concentrates in 2024 once major domestic smelters (Freeport Indonesia and Amman Mineral) begin processing, targeting domestic cathode production. Indonesia recently allowed continued concentrate exports until smelter startups, implying near-term tightening for copper supply flows. | MTB Metals has begun field work to prepare drilling at its Telegraph porphyry copper-gold project in British Columbia’s Golden Triangle, using geophysics, hyperspectral analysis, and on-site XRF to rank targets. The update is exploratory and does not indicate production disruptions or supply changes, implying limited near-term price impact. | Australia’s Alliance Nickel says its NiWest nickel-cobalt project in Western Australia is positioned to meet EV battery demand. The report highlights high-grade resources, improving drilling and planning toward production, and offtake agreements (including Stellantis) that support future nickel sulfate sales, though financing remains a hurdle. | A London High Court hearing revisits the LME’s decision to cancel $12 billion of nickel trades on March 8, 2022 during extreme price volatility tied to a Tsingshan-related squeeze. Plaintiffs claim the cancellations unlawfully protected specific participants; LME cites margin and systemic-risk prevention.

news_summary: Reuters reports London copper is on track for its first weekly fall in four, with the three-month contract down around 0.5% after a firmer dollar and hawkish central-bank signals boosted recession fears. Nickel and aluminium are also flagged for their biggest weekly declines, reflecting broader base-metal pressure. | Copper was set for its first weekly decline in four weeks as the dollar jumped, making dollar-priced metals costlier. LME three-month copper fell, while nickel also dropped and aluminium slid. The moves were driven by hawkish central-bank comments, risk aversion, and weak China consumer stimulus expectations post-COVID lockdown slowdown. | The article’s title says the LME faced a “death spiral,” with up to a dozen firms at risk of default during a period of “nickel chaos.” Counterparty stress and reduced market confidence can worsen liquidity and elevate risk aversion, weighing on short-term nickel pricing expectations. | Exchanges and funds are awaiting a court decision in a $472 million lawsuit against the LME over the cancellation of $12 billion in nickel trades following the March 2022 nickel surge. Outcomes could shape confidence in London’s market governance and crisis actions, keeping nickel sentiment volatile until a ruling in at least a month. | Copper prices are set for a weekly drop as hawkish rate-hike expectations and a stronger U.S. dollar weigh on growth-sensitive commodities. LME stocks keep falling, but price weakness dominates. Aluminium and nickel are also down on the week, indicating broad risk-off pressure across industrial metals. | Kosovo’s Newco Ferronikeli ferronickel plant restarted June 21 after a 20-month closure caused by soaring European energy prices. The facility had been idle since Oct 2021, with partial wage payments during downtime. The restart boosts near-term nickel production and exporter activity, though environmental concerns remain.

news_summary: Copper prices rose as low supplies outweighed tepid demand concerns. The LME cash premium to the three-month contract widened, while on-warrant copper inventories fell to the lowest level since Oct 2021. Chinese SHFE and bonded inventories also declined. Aluminium and nickel rose too, suggesting broader support.

news_summary: Copper rose on Friday amid hopes of Chinese stimulus, but remained set for its biggest quarterly decline since Sept 2022 due to weak Chinese data and expectations of further U.S. rate hikes. Funds support Norwegian battery metals exploration including nickel and copper, potentially strengthening near-term demand expectations for nickel. | The IMF urged Indonesia to phase out mineral export bans and avoid extending restrictions to other commodities, warning that trade uncertainty and unilateral measures can weigh on investment. Indonesia is considering downstream value-addition; the prior nickel ore ban and bauxite ban are central. This guidance could increase expectations of more raw-material supply. | China’s nickel sulfate exports have risen sharply in April and May as traders exploit wider price gaps versus Japan and South Korea. The piece highlights cost-driven competition between China sulfate and ex-China MHP-based production and notes regulatory/geopolitical constraints. Premium nickel sulfate prices show mixed recovery, supporting nickel-positive sentiment. | Copper is set for its first quarterly decline in three quarters as gloomy China demand prospects and worsening factory activity weigh on prices. Inventories in Shanghai rose sharply. The piece also notes aluminium and nickel falling on the same day, pointing to broader bearish sentiment. | Stellantis signed an offtake term sheet with Kuniko to purchase battery-grade nickel and cobalt sulphate tied to Kuniko’s Norwegian exploration projects, committing to acquire 35% of output under a binding nine-year arrangement. Stellantis also plans a EUR 5m equity investment, supporting project advancement. | LG Chem has started mass production of single-crystal high-nickel cathodes for next-generation batteries, with initial shipments planned from July and production expansion at Gumi by 2027. Because these cathodes use high nickel content and are intended for upcoming EV battery formats, the news is supportive for nickel demand. | Stellantis has bought a nearly 20% stake in Norway’s Kuniko, a nickel and cobalt supplier, and agreed on nine years of future production offtake tied to its Norwegian exploration projects. Stellantis will invest about $5.5 million to fund further battery-metals development, reinforcing EV supply-chain demand signals.

news_summary: Indonesia’s anti-graft agency is investigating alleged illegal exports of nickel ore that bypass the country’s export ban, with China reportedly receiving large volumes despite restrictions. Experts cite a big domestic-versus-international price gap and argue the policy failed, suggesting supervision changes or replacing the ban with tariffs. | Wyloo Metals, led by Andrew Forrest, is buying Mincor Resources for $760m and focusing on “clean” nickel sulphides for EV battery supply. It highlights sustainability-driven avoidance of laterite deep-sea tailings and considers a Kwinana processing plant. The moves imply supportive expectations for battery-grade nickel demand and supply. | Wyloo has crossed 90% ownership in Mincor and expects to complete the acquisition soon, attracted by the Cassini nickel sulphide deposit in Western Australia’s Kambalda district. Wyloo says nickel is its main focus, expects to build on Mincor’s mine life, and links resources to planned battery-chemicals processing. | Ardea Resources agreed with Japanese firms (Sumitomo Metal Mining, Mitsubishi, Mitsui) to progress feasibility and final investment planning for the Kalgoorlie Nickel Project Goongarrie Hub. The company projects A$3.18bn capex and long-run output of ~30,000 tonnes nickel annually. It supports Japan’s push for battery-mineral supply. | Wyloo, controlled by Andrew and Nicola Forrest’s investment group, raised its stake in ASX-listed nickel miner Mincor to about 90.87% and will compulsorily acquire remaining shares at $1.40 each. Mincor’s Kambalda project produces nickel sulphides used in battery demand, but no near-term output changes are described. | Nordic Resources pitches its Pulju project in Finland as a major future supplier of battery-grade nickel, citing an updated JORC resource for the Hotinvaara prospect and proximity to other Cu-Ni sulphide deposits. The EU’s Critical Raw Materials Act names nickel as strategic, boosting near-term sentiment for nickel. | Copper fell on the LME alongside other base metals as global PMI data signaled weaker growth and services activity, dimming near-term demand for industrial metals. Citi warned metals consumption will remain under pressure. The stronger dollar added further pressure. Aluminium and nickel also declined. | Wyloo Metals agreed to buy 90.87% of Mincor Resources for A$760m, adding Mincor’s mainly producing nickel sulphide assets in Western Australia. Management frames the move as building an integrated nickel business, including plans for downstream processing for battery cathode materials in Kwinana. The acquisition is broadly supportive for nickel sentiment. | Mink Ventures announced it is issuing shares and warrants to commence an option on the Warren copper-nickel project in Ontario. The drill-ready property has historical Cu and Ni mineralized zones (A, B, C) and plans for a summer diamond program, but no production or definitive new supply is stated.
