news_summary: A report says China’s rapid expansion into Indonesia’s nickel operations has given it dominant control of nickel needed for EV batteries in the U.S. The Inflation Reduction Act’s mineral-sourcing requirements may exclude Indonesia and invite scrutiny of Chinese firms’ involvement, weakening U.S. supply prospects and near-term nickel demand outlook. | London copper rebounded 0.3% as the dollar eased, but gains were capped by uncertainty over Chinese demand and expectations for further U.S. Fed rate hikes. LME aluminium rose 0.6% and nickel edged up slightly, reflecting mixed sentiment across metals tied to broader risk assets. | BofA reduced its nickel price forecast for 2023 to $22,063/t from $23,222/t previously, while keeping the 2024 forecast unchanged at $21,250/t. The downgrade signals softer expected nickel market conditions, which can weigh on near-term nickel price expectations. | Copper edged up but remained in a tight range as investors weighed U.S. Fed rate expectations and a pending jobs report against China demand outlook. China’s property and new-energy activity supported demand, while expanding domestic supply from smelters and rising aluminium output capped gains. Aluminium and nickel also saw modest moves. | Indonesia’s ministry is probing allegations that nickel ore continued to be exported to China despite a ban since 2020. China imported 5.56 million tons between 2020 and May 2023, prompting investigations into misclassification and possible illegal shipments. Any crackdown could tighten nickel ore supply and support nickel prices. | Indonesia is investigating alleged illegal exports of nickel ore worth 14.5 trillion rupiah to China dating back to early 2020. Authorities say 5.3 million mt of ore were illegally shipped, causing estimated losses of royalties and export taxes. Possible enforcement tightening could reduce near-term nickel ore flows to China. | BofA Global Research lowered its commodity price forecasts, including copper and nickel. Copper forecasts for 2023 and 2024 were reduced versus prior estimates, and nickel’s 2023 forecast was also cut, while 2024 stayed unchanged. The revisions point to weaker expected market conditions for these metals. | BofA Global Research lowered its price forecasts for several commodities. Copper forecasts were reduced for 2023 and 2024, and nickel’s 2023 forecast was also cut (2024 unchanged). The changes suggest weaker expected prices, but the impact is mainly forecast-level. | A London court set a July 12 deadline for Prateek Gupta and related firms to file their defence in Trafigura’s nickel cargo fraud case. Trafigura alleged some 25,000 tonnes were not high-grade nickel, with early containers found to contain carbon steel, and booked a 590-million impairment.

news_summary: Copper edged up on the LME as the weak dollar supported USD-priced metals, but sentiment was pressured by weak Chinese economic data. China signaled consumption-support policies that could help later demand. LME aluminium and nickel rose modestly, indicating broadly mixed, low-magnitude moves rather than a strong directional shock. | Indonesia’s anti-corruption agency (KPK) is urged to investigate alleged smuggling of about 5 million tons of unprocessed nickel ore to China, despite a 2020 export ban. Reports cite origins in Sulawesi and North Maluku and focus on potential bribery and enforcement gaps, potentially affecting near-term nickel ore flows and risk. | Copper fell in London as weak China GDP growth raised concerns about consumption and metals demand. Investors stayed cautious ahead of any large stimulus, while aluminium and nickel also declined alongside the broader base-metals selloff. The weaker demand outlook outweighed a slightly weaker U.S. dollar support. | Canadian North Resources reported spring drilling assay results at its Ferguson Lake project, expanding near-surface mineralized zones with intersecting nickel and copper grades (plus cobalt and PGMs). The update supports expectations for further resource definition and potential future supply, providing modest near-term bullish sentiment for Cu and Ni. | Philippine nickel miners via the Nickel Industry Association argue that the global transition to clean energy, smart cities, and China’s infrastructure spending will raise nickel demand. They cite nickel’s use in EV batteries and stainless steel for construction and renewable technologies, but impacts are described as medium-to-long term. | Canadian North Resources reported assays from 6,151 metres of spring drilling at its Ferguson Lake project, expanding near-surface zones with copper and nickel mineralization (plus cobalt and PGM). It is an exploration definition update with no stated production or supply disruption, so near-term price impact is likely limited. | CMOC settled a $2 billion dispute with Congo’s Gecamines over royalties at the Tenke Fungurume mine, where copper exports were suspended for a year. The agreement (including settlement fees and arrears dividends) reduces disruption risk and supports near-term expectations for copper supply continuity, with limited spillover effects beyond copper.

news_summary: Reuters reports copper prices dipped, pressured by weak Chinese demand and a widening spot discount alongside rising inventories, while China refined output is expected to rise as new projects come online. A weak dollar limited losses. Aluminium and nickel saw small gains in China trading, but copper remained under demand pressure. | Reuters reports copper prices fell as China’s demand outlook looked lackluster and inventories increased. Weak spot buying and a slower growth expectation for the power sector weighed on sentiment, while expectations of rising Chinese refined supply added pressure, despite some support from a weaker US dollar. | Copper Lake Resources reports an exploration update at its Marshall Lake copper-zinc-silver VMS property, identifying new drill targets (MT conductor and build-up conductor) and citing encouraging drill intercepts with high copper grades. The company plans additional geophysics and deep drilling in 2023, but no production or supply changes are stated. | Visionary Metals plans a 4,500m drill program and increased claim control at its King Solomon nickel sulfide project in Wyoming, following a prior discovery with reported nickel grades and battery-grade sulfide. It also outlines additional nickel anomalies at Tin Cup, supporting incremental bullish sentiment for nickel exploration. | ICSID ruled Tanzania must pay Indiana Resources over $100 million in damages plus compounded interest and legal costs for the unlawful expropriation of the Ntaka Hill nickel project. The company said it is moving to enforcement as payment continues to rise until Tanzania pays, but no production timeline is given. | Copper prices fell as investors worried China’s stimulus may disappoint and demand remains weak, while strong domestic Chinese refined output hit record levels. The Yangshan import premium eased, signaling lower import appetite. In the same session, aluminium and nickel also declined amid softer overall metal sentiment.

news_summary: Glencore backed its full-year guidance after first-half copper production slipped 4.4% to 488,000 mt, but remained in line with its expectations for mine sequences at Collahuasi and Antamina. Nickel output fell 20%, while zinc declined and cobalt/gold rose; no guidance changes were announced. | Copper and most industrial metals eased after investors were disappointed by China’s modest stimulus aimed at boosting consumption. Reuters reports LME copper down about 0.3% and aluminium down 0.4%, with weekly losses extending. Commentary cited weaker-than-expected activity signals and possible rising supply risk. | Copper and most industrial metal prices edged lower as investors were disappointed by modest Chinese stimulus aimed at consumption. Three-month copper fell to about $8,459/t, with limited Chinese downstream demand and slightly easing spot tightness. Aluminium also dropped on the week, alongside broader base-metal weakness and supply expectations. | BNN Bloomberg’s piece, titled “Nickel’s at a Crossroads as Supply Boom Hits LME, Macquarie Says,” points to expectations that additional nickel supply is pressuring LME balances. That backdrop implies weaker pricing near term as market participants anticipate more supply than demand. | Macquarie warns that additional nickel processing plants in China and Indonesia could increase LME-deliverable nickel supply by roughly 35% versus 2022, adding to an already oversupplied physical market. Nickel prices have fallen to around $20,000/ton, and the analysts call the market a “turning point” likely pressuring prices further. | Glencore reported a spike in trading profits despite lower copper and nickel output. Copper production fell 4% YoY and nickel 20% YoY, with disruptions including a 2022 strike at Raglan. The firm kept full-year guidance unchanged and expects higher second-half volumes, tempering immediate supply concerns.

news_summary: London copper rose 0.2% as investors expected China’s Politburo and broader stimulus to revive growth and boost industrial demand, following disappointment with smaller measures. Near-term demand was still soft, with producer operating rates and import appetite weak. LME aluminium and nickel also edged higher. | Indonesia’s SOEs Minister Erick Thohir said Vale Indonesia must relinquish a larger stake to the state-linked miner MIND ID, aligning with Freeport Indonesia’s move to give Indonesia 51% control. The policy aims to accelerate nickel downstream development, which can influence near-term nickel supply expectations. | Nornickel reported preliminary 1H/2Q 2023 production results. Nickel output fell due to scheduled furnace repairs at Nadezhda Smelter and a grinding mill at Talnakh, plus inter-navigational port timing; copper output also declined, with additional smelting suspensions from Norilsk weather. Trans-Baikal ramp-up partially offset copper in concentrate. | Vale says Q2 copper production rose 41% YoY (18% QoQ) on Salobo III startup and better performance at Sossego, with copper sales increasing similarly. Vale also reported nickel production up 8% YoY. Overall, higher output suggests increased supply that can pressure copper and nickel prices. | Vale said Q2 copper production rose 41% YoY (18% QoQ) on successful start-up of Salobo III and better performance at Sossego, with sales up similarly. Vale also reported higher nickel production (+8% YoY). More supply and sales growth can pressure short-term copper and nickel prices. | Copper prices were little changed in London as China pledged additional policy adjustments to support growth after a weak post-COVID recovery. Analysts cited concerns about slower Chinese demand as a drag. LME copper was flat on the day, while aluminium was down and nickel slightly higher, reflecting mixed sentiment. | Nickel prices have eased from May’s slowdown, traded sideways near a June low, and bounced modestly in early July. However, ING expects a bearish H2 outlook, citing a rising surplus, China’s stagnant consumption, and Indonesia’s expanding supply, offset only partly by energy-transition demand and low LME inventories.

news_summary: The operator of Indonesia’s Morowali industrial park—an emerging nickel production hub—says it is introducing environmental measures as concerns over nickel output grow. Because nickel is central to stainless steel and EV battery supply chains, actions that ease regulatory disruption risk can support near-term nickel pricing. | Aston Minerals raised $11m via a share placement to advance its Canadian Edleston nickel project in Ontario. Funds will support metallurgical testing and infill/resource drilling, with additional flow-through shares offered under Canada’s tax incentive regime. International demand for the placement suggests positive sentiment for future nickel supply development. | Huayou’s subsidiary will build a refining project in Indonesia and plans to buy a majority stake in Perlux Limited. The deal gives Huayou ownership of two ferro-nickel production lines with about 24,000 metric tons nickel-content output annually, expanding upstream supply and weighing on nickel sentiment. | Copper prices rose on London trading after China’s top leaders pledged more supportive policy measures to boost domestic demand, especially in property. The expectation improved sentiment and strengthened the yuan, aiding dollar-priced metals. LME aluminium and nickel also increased on the day alongside copper.

news_summary: London copper gained as the dollar softened after Fitch cut the U.S. top credit rating, making dollar-priced metals cheaper. China’s weak data weighed on demand, but hopes for stimulus supported prices. The operational modernization may boost near-term nickel supply expectations. | Copper prices eased as traders awaited concrete Chinese stimulus measures and weighed weak China demand signals. LME three-month copper fell 0.4%, SHFE copper dropped 1.3%. Aluminium and nickel also declined on the day, reflecting cautious positioning and uncertainty around China’s policy support timeline. | Norilsk Nickel’s 1H 2023 financial results highlight lower realized prices for Pd, Ni, Rh and Cu, and lower nickel output due to scheduled repairs. These factors can affect near-term expectations for nickel and copper pricing. | Minnesota’s Supreme Court remanded NewRange Copper Nickel’s NorthMet project water discharge permit for additional proceedings and documentation. The decision focused on ensuring permitting issues are properly addressed in a public process. Potential implications include further delays or uncertainty for copper-nickel project development, weighing on near-term supply expectations. | Minnesota’s Supreme Court remanded NewRange Copper Nickel’s NorthMet project water discharge permit to the MPCA for further clarification and documentation. The court acknowledged the permit may be protective of state, tribal and federal waters. The company expects additional proceedings to confirm water-quality protection, supporting project progression.

news_summary: Copper rebounded after three weekly declines as China’s central bank pledged to resolve local government debt risks and cut the one-year lending rate, boosting sentiment. Low inventories also supported prices. LME and SHFE showed small gains in aluminium and nickel as well, reflecting broader risk-on demand expectations. | SMM says nickel prices were mainly driven by macro factors but also supported by concerns over Indonesia nickel ore mine investigations, which firmed nickel ore prices. Across seven major ports, inventories rose to 3.637 million wmt, up 266,000 wmt. China cut the one-year lending rate, adding support, while aluminium and nickel rose modestly. | Posco Future M and MC Group will form a joint venture in the Philippines to process MHP into high-purity nickel sulphate for EV battery cathodes, sourcing nickel ore from MC Group’s Palawan mine. The move expands refined nickel supply linked to cathode production and could bolster near-term nickel demand expectations. | Tsingshan Group began commercial production of refined nickel at its Morowali (Indonesia) plant, with ultimate capacity of 50,000 tonnes, according to three sources. news_summary: Talon Metals announced drilling results from the Tamarack Nickel Project: 101.71 meters at 3.04% nickel-equivalent, including 1.94% nickel and 1.84% copper. The work, supported by a Columbia ARPA-E grant, also targets novel processing to lower CO2 footprint and increase nickel/copper recovery, improving future supply outlook. | Palladium One reports highly anomalous nickel (up to 785 ppm) and copper (up to 431 ppm) soil geochemistry between the West Pickle and RJ Zones at its Tyko Ni-Cu-Co project, supported by ultramafic mapping and geophysics. The news may modestly improve sentiment on future Ni/Cu supply, though no production change is indicated. | Palladium One reports highly anomalous nickel and copper (plus cobalt) soil results at its Tyko project, linking the West Pickle and RJ zones via interpreted chonolith/feeder dykes. It also states it earned an 80% interest in the Pezim II block. The news supports exploration potential but is not production-related. | Copper prices rose for a fourth straight session, supported by firmer Chinese refined copper demand (ICSG) and additional Chinese monetary support, alongside a weaker US dollar. Reuters also reported LME aluminium and nickel rising that day, indicating a broader, sentiment-driven lift across industrial metals.

news_summary: Indonesia’s Jokowi and economist Faisal Basri spar over the government’s nickel downstream policy, with Faisal arguing it disproportionately benefits China and Jokowi citing export and tax gains. Neither side announces immediate changes to quotas, tariffs, or production, so near-term nickel price impact is uncertain despite the policy debate. | SHFE 2309 nickel closed higher on Aug 22, but intraday trading and volumes/open interest weakened, suggesting fund outflows. The report links the strong trend mainly to Indonesian nickel ore quota conditions and also cites China’s lower LPR supporting sentiment. Weak spot deals and higher upstream selling point to near-term rangebound trade. | Palladium One announced highly anomalous nickel and copper soil results between the West Pickle and RJ zones at its Tyko project, including values up to 785 ppm Ni and 431 ppm Cu. The company also earned an 80% interest in the Pezim II claim block. No production or supply impact is indicated. | Copper and most base metals rose in London as improved demand prospects from top consumer China—supported by upbeat Chinese copper consumption data and speculation of further government measures—boosted sentiment. A stronger yuan and weaker dollar supported dollar-priced metals. LME aluminium and nickel also increased alongside copper. | Moneta Gold announced it completed transactions to expand its Loveland Nickel property near Timmins, adding claims around the Cominco and Hollinger zones. The release cites historical intercepts and resource grades for nickel and some copper, but provides no development timeline or production affecting near-term metal supply. | Copper rose for a fifth straight session on improved Chinese demand prospects, upbeat consumption data and speculation of further Chinese support measures. Tight LME-monitored stockpiles near a 15-year low also helped. A stronger dollar eased, keeping base metals generally higher, with aluminium and nickel also moving up.

news_summary: London copper edged down 0.1% to $8,443/t as traders stayed cautious ahead of key China and U.S. economic data that could steer interest-rate expectations and demand outlook. LME aluminium rose 0.3% while nickel fell 0.2%, indicating mixed, low-magnitude pressure across industrial metals. | Philippine officials and the Nickel Industry Association urge streamlining mining permitting via a potential “one-stop shop,” cutting approval timelines from 5–10 years to 6 months to 1 year. They cite rising nickel ore demand for EV batteries and stable production, seeking regulatory predictability to accelerate nickel development. | London copper slipped as traders took a cautious stance ahead of key U.S. and China data. A softer dollar and China mortgage easing in Guangzhou limited declines. Copper premiums widened on higher London–Shanghai spreads. Nickel fell and aluminium edged up, reflecting mixed macro-driven sentiment rather than a specific metal supply shock. | Indonesia delayed mining quotas and suspended operations at a key Antam site, tightening local nickel ore availability. Smelters began importing low-grade Philippine limonite ore as a stopgap. Local prices rose 8% this week. Philippine ore prices in China jumped, reflecting tighter Indonesian supplies and boosting near-term nickel sentiment. | Copper prices slipped on concerns about weaker global growth and metals demand, with investors reassessing softer U.S. jobs data. Losses were moderated by signs of support for China’s property sector. Aluminium rose, while nickel eased, reflecting mixed sentiment ahead of further China manufacturing data. | Copper prices fell 0.2% on weaker economic-growth concerns, but losses were moderated by China property-sector support measures, including mortgage easing in Guangzhou. The green transition was cited as underlying support. Aluminium rose while nickel declined, reflecting mixed macro-demand signals across industrial metals. | Abaxx said it aims to launch nickel sulfate futures by year-end after MAS regulatory review. The contract targets EV-relevant nickel sulfate and seeks to bridge the physical market shift away from Class 1 nickel toward intermediates like nickel matte/MHP. Nickel sulfate and its premium were also assessed higher recently.

The improved demand outlook supported broader industrial metals, with LME aluminium and nickel also up around 0.8-1.1% despite a strong U.S. dollar. | Thiess won a AUS$240 million contract to deliver full-scale mining operations for a nickel project in Central Halmahera, Indonesia, for 3.5 years. The deal strengthens regional operating capacity and indicates ongoing nickel production investment, which can be mildly supportive for nickel price expectations despite limited immediate volume detail. | Copper hit a four-week high as China announced measures to support its crisis-hit property market and manufacturing activity expanded. Analysts attributed the rally to improving demand indicators such as new orders in PMIs and expectations of metals restocking as construction resumes. Aluminium and nickel also rose alongside copper. | Aurubis says it may face large losses after discovering discrepancies in its metals inventory tied to manipulated invoices for shipments that never reached its site. The affected inputs include copper and precious metals. The firm has downgraded forecasts and is investigating potential criminal activity, raising near-term risk perception in metals markets. | The piece argues nickel remains weak after the March LME squeeze, citing continued decline in the Stainless MMI, lower volumes versus pre-LME shutdown levels, reduced liquidity and hedging constraints, and risk aversion among market participants. It also highlights reputational damage and lawsuits, expecting fallout to last months or longer.

news_summary: Reuters reports that Abaxx Commodities Exchange is proposing a physically settled nickel sulphate futures contract to create pricing for the expanding EV battery nickel sulphate market. It reviews LME stock depletion of Class I nickel and the lack of deliverable hedging for intermediate products. The piece focuses on a new pricing venue rather than immediate fundamentals. | Shanghai nickel jumped about 5% to near a four-month high as rising ore prices and tighter supply from Indonesia followed delays in issuing mining quotas. Strong September stainless steel production and a stainless steel futures rally boosted sentiment, pushing SHFE nickel contract gains despite a firming dollar headwind. | The Philippines’ Mines and Geosciences Bureau said H1 nickel ore output rose 40% to 16.87 million dmt. It is also expecting additional increases as four more mining projects are set to start between 2023 and 2025, improving nickel ore supply for stainless steel and EV battery feedstock. | Shanghai nickel jumped nearly 4% to a nearly four-month high on supply worries. Reuters attributes the move to higher ore prices and tighter Indonesian nickel ore availability after delayed mining quotas, alongside firmer demand from China’s stainless steel sector and improving sentiment. | Copper prices fell as markets worried about slower demand from China’s property sector and as LME-registered copper inventories rose sharply. A softer US dollar limited losses, but copper still declined. Aluminium also eased, while nickel dropped amid concerns over Indonesia delaying mining quota issuance for nickel supply. | Metals One said initial diamond drilling at its SRH Rana project in Norway confirmed high-grade nickel-copper-cobalt sulphide mineralisation at the Bruvann prospect. Reported intercepts include 1.0%+ nickel with copper grades around 0.14–0.18%. The mineralisation is open along strike, with further drilling planned by the farm-in partner. | Metals One said early diamond drilling at its SRH Rana project in Norway confirmed high-grade nickel-copper-cobalt sulphide mineralisation, including intercepts with up to 1–2% nickel and notable copper values. The company expects further drilling updates. Near-term fundamentals likely remain unchanged, limiting price impact on nickel and copper. | Copper prices retreated 0.9% to $8,425 as traders worried about China demand, particularly slower property activity, and about rising LME warehouse inventories. Losses were limited by a softer dollar. Aluminium also fell. Nickel edged lower, despite separate supply concerns tied to Indonesia mining quota delays. | The Philippines’ DTI said domestic downstream nickel processing is critical for clean-tech ambitions. Trade Secretary Alfredo Pascual urged private firms to invest or partner globally to upgrade nickel-related value chain activities, aiming to secure a stronger role in clean technology supply chains through sustainability and increased processing capacity.

news_summary: A miner died during a shift at the Avebury Nickel Mine near Zeehan. Mine management confirmed the death and said underground operations have ceased temporarily while employees are supported. Although the cause is stated as non work-related, the shutdown may reduce near-term nickel supply expectations. | Macron’s visit to New Caledonia emphasized nickel’s strategic role for France and Europe. Ministers met nickel firms to discuss sector challenges, including rising energy costs and industrial disputes. The energy crunch raises production risks and costs in one of the key nickel supply regions, creating near-term bearish pressure. | Copper prices fell to the lowest in over a week as a firm dollar and weaker yuan weighed on demand sentiment in China. LME three-month copper and SHFE October both dropped to multi-week lows. The piece also shows nickel weakness alongside modest aluminium gains, reflecting mixed but generally soft metal tone. | Indonesia investigated illegal nickel ore practices, including exports without proper IUP qualifications and ore exports after quota usage. China’s yuan weakened and trade data remained soft, weighing sentiment. LME copper dropped 0.4%, with nickel also down while aluminium edged up. | Copper Lake Resources announced an updated NI 43-101 mineral resource for its Norton Lake Ni-Cu-Co-PGE deposit in Ontario, totaling 1.96 Mt Measured & Indicated at 0.67% Ni and 0.66% Cu (plus PGE). The release is a resource estimate, not reserves, implying limited short-term supply impact. | Copper fell to a two-week low as LME inventories jumped 21% to 133,850 tonnes, signalling potential surplus concerns, while China’s August imports were 5% lower year-on-year. A stronger U.S. dollar and easing Chinese premiums added pressure. The release is primarily technical and does not announce production, permits, or operational changes, so near-term price impact on copper/nickel is likely limited. | Russian nickel producer Nornickel did not pay dividends on 2022 results, citing “negative geopolitics” and the need for steadier free cash flow. While free cash flow rose in H1, management said risks remain. Dividend decisions rest with key shareholders; buybacks are not considered, though a share split is. | A federal judge dealt another setback to a proposed copper-nickel mine in Minnesota’s wilderness. The ruling likely delays or undermines future mine development, reducing anticipated supply additions and supporting tighter near-term balances. Twin Metals’ proposed copper-nickel underground project near Ely, Minnesota, faces further delays. Reduced near-term supply expectations for copper and nickel could pressure prices. | A federal judge dismissed Twin Metals’ lawsuit seeking permission to mine land covered by a 20-year federal moratorium. The project targets copper and nickel near Ely, Minnesota. The decision upholds agency actions and extends uncertainty over timelines, reducing expected incremental supply in the near term and weighing on both metals. | A federal judge dismissed Twin Metals’ lawsuit seeking to restore mineral rights for its proposed Minnesota copper-nickel mine, after the Biden administration canceled leases and imposed a long moratorium. Backers cite critical-mineral needs, but opponents say it protects Boundary Waters, limiting supply expectations.

news_summary: Copper rose after China’s Country Garden won creditor approval to extend repayment terms on onshore bonds, easing stress in the property sector that drives large metal demand. Costs and fundamentals remain supportive, lifting prices slightly. | SMM and Indonesia’s nickel miners association (APNI) agreed to cooperate on data systems and to launch an Indonesian nickel industry price index. Aluminium and nickel were also down on the day. | EV Nickel upsized a non-brokered private placement, raising CAD$2.08M to fund continued exploration and advancement of its Shaw Dome nickel project in Ontario, plus general corporate purposes. The offering is subject to TSX Venture approval and is expected to close shortly. | Talon Metals said the US Department of Defense will provide about $20.6 million in cost-share funding via DPA Title III to accelerate drilling and geophysics for high-grade nickel deposits in Minnesota and Michigan. The program targets domestic nickel for defense and US battery supply chains, potentially improving short-term nickel sentiment. | The U.S. Department of Defense agreed with Talon Metals to fund (via DPA Title III) accelerated exploration for high-grade domestic nickel in Michigan and Minnesota. With matching cost-share funding, the program aims to discover and delineate more nickel supply for U.S. batteries and defense supply chains. | Québec Nickel reports drilling results from the Ducros Ni-Co-Cu-PGE project, including multiple long intervals grading around 0.20–0.22% nickel with associated cobalt. The company frames this as a second large Ni-Co ultramafic intrusion discovery this year. No production, permitting, or near-term supply changes are announced. | Amnesty International alleges forced evictions, intimidation, and village destruction tied to expansion of copper and cobalt mining projects in DR Congo’s Kolwezi region. While the DRC supplies most of the world’s cobalt and large copper volumes, the article provides no immediate operational or regulatory disruption, limiting near-term price impact. | The US Defense Department is providing $110 million in support to lithium and nickel miners. While lithium is not tracked here, the funding for nickel producers can improve expectations for near-term nickel supply or production continuity, which tends to be mildly bullish for nickel prices over the coming weeks. | The U.S. Department of Defense signed a deal with Talon Nickel to advance the Tamarack nickel project in Minnesota. The move is intended to strengthen critical-material supply chains and reduce dependence on China and Russia. With limited U.S. operating nickel supply, the announcement is supportive for nickel prices. | A report says nickel mines in Michigan and Minnesota received funding. The financing is intended to support operations and/or development at the mines, linking the update to nickel supply prospects. Because new capacity usually takes time to translate into production, the immediate effect on nickel prices is likely modest. | The U.S. Department of Defense awarded $20.6 million to developers of the proposed Talon nickel mine in Michigan and Minnesota, supporting prospecting work, following earlier DOE funding for ore processing. The article frames nickel as a national security priority amid efforts to bolster domestic critical mineral supplies. | The U.S. Department of Defense awarded $20.6 million to support prospecting for the proposed Talon nickel mine in Minnesota and Michigan. The funding complements prior Department of Energy support for ore processing in North Dakota and aims to identify more high-grade nickel deposits in Upper Michigan. | Indonesia, the world’s largest nickel producer, is importing nickel ore as smelter capacity expands, with shipments rising sharply in 1H 2023. The piece argues the country may have “overplayed its hand” via heavy reliance on export bans and lingering environmental/investment hurdles, potentially weighing on nickel demand and investment sentiment.

news_summary: Copper and other base metals were largely flat in Asia as markets awaited the U.S. Federal Reserve’s Sept. 20 rate decision and guidance. Analysts said the Fed outcome would steer metals and energy. Reported moves were minimal: copper near-flat, aluminium slightly higher on LME, and nickel marginally up. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate around 31,500–32,000 yuan/ton, up modestly and then stabilized. Manufacturers are mostly sold out September spot stocks and expect production cuts, but precursor transactions are weaker and electro-nickel purchasing has slowed. With demand steady and costs high, prices are expected to remain stable next week. | Indonesia’s energy minister Arifin Tasrif warned that, at current nickel ore consumption, Indonesia’s reserves could be depleted in about 15 years. The forecast is tied to high production and growing demand from smelting and EV battery supply chains. Recycling and further exploration are cited as potential ways to extend life. | Canada Nickel closed a US$12 million secured loan facility with Auramet to fund working capital and continue permitting and detailed engineering for its nickel-cobalt sulphide project. The financing supports project progress and potential future nickel supply, but the transaction is not a direct near-term supply or demand shock.

news_summary: Reuters reports copper prices in London rose slightly on Friday but were set for a weekly decline as LME inventories swelled and the U.S. dollar firmed on higher-for-longer Fed expectations. Analysts cite uneven China growth and tighter financial conditions. Nickel is near multi-month lows on surplus data; aluminium is up. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate at 31,949 yuan/ton on Sept 21, up 54 yuan/ton, with spot scarcity and strong purchasing activity supporting prices. The high precursor price is only partly transmitted downstream, but electro-nickel margins remain attractive. Cost support may strengthen after Indonesia ends nickel ore quotas starting in 23 years. | Reuters reports copper is on track for a weekly loss as the US dollar strengthens following Fed signals, while LME-registered copper inventories remain at their highest since May 2022. Reduced risk appetite and a wider near-term discount point to ample supply and an oversupplied market. | Giga Metals announced a positive NI 43-101 pre-feasibility study for the Turnagain nickel-cobalt project in British Columbia. The study outlines large, long-life production of high-grade nickel concentrate and favorable economics under nickel price assumptions, which can support bullish sentiment for nickel development prospects. | Giga Metals announced a positive pre-feasibility study for the Turnagain nickel-cobalt project in Canada, projecting long-life output of nickel/cobalt concentrate with low impurities and favorable costs. Mitsubishi highlighted the project’s low-carbon profile and potential role in growing nickel demand for batteries. The update improves development outlook for nickel supply. | Giga Metals announced a positive pre-feasibility study for the Turnagain nickel-cobalt project in British Columbia, showing production of nickel-cobalt concentrate with positive post-tax economics. The company highlights demand growth for battery-related nickel products and positions the project to attract strategic investors, supporting nickel sentiment.

Nickel prices edged up, with nickel inventories still climbing. Aluminium was flat, with no clear near-term catalyst. | Copper rallied 0.7% and nickel 1.3% on Monday after the U.S. passed a stopgap bill to avoid a government shutdown, boosting risk-on sentiment. Aluminium also rose 0.8%. The weaker dollar supported dollar-priced metals, while higher LME copper inventories and increased Chile supply limited further upside. | Horizonte Minerals updated construction progress for its Araguaia nickel project in Brazil, but final engineering changes and supplier delivery delays increase planned capex by at least 35% and push first production to Q3-2024. The project targets direct shipping ore and Pacific Nickel expects its first ore shipment in November, supporting prospective nickel supply outlooks.

news_summary: Reuters reports London copper slid to its lowest in more than four months, down as much as 0.6% to around $8,005/t, driven by a firmer U.S. dollar after strong U.S. data supporting higher-for-longer rates. Aluminium dipped slightly, nickel rose, and COMEX showed managers increasing net short positions. | Copper prices fell to a four-month low as the dollar strengthened and LME exchange inventories rose sharply. Warehouse stocks are at the highest level since May last year, with expectations of more deliveries widening contango. Physical demand was described as subdued, pressuring nearby copper prices. Aluminium retreated and nickel slipped slightly as broader metal sentiment weakened. | Indonesia says its WTO appeal over the EU dispute (DS592) challenging Indonesia’s nickel ore export ban remains pending because the WTO appellate body has been dysfunctional since 2019. The nickel ore ban is still in effect, keeping restrictions on unprocessed nickel exports as the dispute is revisited. | Vale has partnered with BluestOne under a commercial off-take and non-binding agreement to repurpose refinery waste and extract materials from its Onça Puma nickel mine in Brazil. The arrangement could improve processing/recovery, potentially easing future nickel supply expectations, though timing and magnitude are not specified. | Copper Lake filed an NI 43-101 technical report for its Norton Lake Ni-Cu-Co-Pd-Pt deposit, providing an updated Mineral Resource Estimate. The update includes higher contained copper and nickel figures, but resources are not reserves and there’s no near-term production or supply change, so immediate price impact is limited.

China demand is cited as robust despite property stress. Aluminium edges up; nickel is also higher in SHFE trading. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate trading around 32,100–32,800 yuan/ton, with slight week-on-week increases but weaker demand transmission from high prices. Manufacturers cite tight raw-material supply and high raw-material coefficients supporting prices, while electro-nickel-side purchasing slows. Post-holiday, nickel sulfate is expected to stay stable. | SMM reports that China’s battery-grade nickel sulfate index rose to 32,182 yuan/ton on Oct 9. Spot activity remains weak after holidays, but inquiries persist. Tight raw-material supply and low shipping willingness support costs, while downstream demand continues, leaving potential upside for nickel sulfate prices. | Indonesia’s government says a European company is negotiating a large nickel smelter investment to supply batteries and electric vehicles. Since Indonesia’s 2020 ban on raw nickel ore exports, the policy has driven downstream investments despite an EU WTO dispute. New smelter plans are likely to support nickel supply expectations. | Copper prices fell as renewed jitters over China’s property demand intensified after Country Garden warned of significant challenges meeting offshore debt. The move was reinforced by sharply higher LME warehouse inventories, widening a surplus outlook and weakening cash premiums. Aluminium and nickel also declined amid broader industrial-metals risk sentiment. | Russian-origin aluminium on LME warrants fell to 76% in September from 81% as  cancellations occurred in Gwangyang, with Russian aluminium tonnage dropping to 132,475 from 183,650. The release is exploration-focused with no near-term production or infrastructure impact. | Advance Metals announced “outstanding” rock chip assay results from its Augustus polymetallic project in Arizona, including up to 11.97% copper and economically viable copper grades in ~40% of samples, plus gold and zinc. news_summary: Copper prices edged lower as London inventories rose to the highest since May 2022, with LME copper down and the copper premium signaling resilient Chinese imports. The drop was partly offset by a soft U.S. dollar and hopes for further China stimulus. Nickel and aluminium moved modestly. | Copper rose on Thursday as a weaker U.S. dollar made dollar-priced commodities cheaper and as investors looked for further Chinese stimulus. Chinese infrastructure-debt expectations lifted demand signals, including a higher Yangshan premium. However, gains were capped by sharply higher LME copper inventories, while aluminium stayed flat and nickel rose. | Canada Nickel reported a positive bankable feasibility study for its Crawford nickel sulphide project, highlighting higher after-tax NPV/IRR and production of about 48 ktpa nickel. The update strengthens expectations for future long-life development (permits/financing) and improves sentiment for nickel demand-supply balance over coming months. | Midland Exploration released new assay results from drilling at its Santos nickel-copper discovery on the Tete Nord property. The latest hole intersected mineralized zones with grades including 0.33% Ni and 0.12% Cu over 39.73 metres. Additional drilling is planned after permits in November. | Grizzly Discoveries reports first sample results from work at the Greenwood District targets (Midway, Imperial, Copper Mountain). Results highlight gold and silver, with some base-metal grades including copper (up to 0.475%) alongside lead/zinc. The article is exploration-focused and contains no immediate supply or market-impact details. | The Market Herald releases interview content covering multiple companies. The only clear industrial-metals link is Canada Nickel’s positive bankable feasibility study for its Crawford nickel sulphide project, reporting improved economics versus the preliminary analysis. Better project economics can support expectations for future nickel supply and sentiment.

Reuters notes weaker demand indicators, including falling Chinese copper imports. LME aluminium also fell slightly and nickel eased, reflecting broader industrial-metals softness. | SMM reports the October 16 battery-grade nickel sulfate index at 32,236 yuan/ton, up 36 yuan/ton from the prior day, with the market broadly flat. Supply and demand remain weak and quotations unchanged, indicating muted near-term pressure. | Copper prices in London fell as traders worried about weaker global demand, focusing on China’s property sector and upcoming monthly economic data. The article cites strong supply in copper. LME aluminium and nickel also fell, reflecting softer sentiment and a stronger dollar, which can dampen metal buying. | Bell Copper says the Arizona Geological Survey, with USGS and state funding, is starting a three-year geochemical study of critical minerals at Arizona porphyry copper deposits, using core samples from its Big Sandy project. news_summary: Copper prices opened lower on Monday as Middle East conflict concerns intensified after fears Israel-Hamas could broaden, while LME/SHFE copper stocks rose. Three-month LME copper fell 0.4% and Shanghai December copper dropped 0.8%. Aluminium also dipped slightly; nickel was slightly higher. | The SMM morning note cites stronger-than-expected US retail sales but a higher December Fed hike probability, with a USD spike then retreat and commodity pressure. For nickel, new electrowinning capacity is ramping, imports of nickel plates keep declining, and demand weakens amid reduced 300-series output and lower downstream orders. Prices seen weak but stable this week. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate prices stabilizing around 32,000–33,000 yuan/ton, with wider price ranges. Raw materials remain tight and high, providing strong cost support tied to the LME nickel coefficient, but electro-nickel buyers face losses and procurement stagnates, limiting demand and constraining downside. | Russia’s Nornickel said Q3 nickel output rose 21% quarter-on-quarter to 53,945 tonnes, boosting near-term supply expectations for a major nickel producer. Full-year guidance remains intact. Although Western suppliers avoiding Russia could create longer-term operational constraints, the reported production increase is supportive for nickel. | Norilsk Nickel reported consolidated 9M 2023 production results. Nickel output fell 9% y/y to 145 kt due to scheduled furnace repairs and concentration grinding mill maintenance. Copper output fell 4% y/y to 304 kt mainly from cathode-quality adjustments. Q3 showed q-o-q increases for both metals. | Canada Nickel’s investor presentation emphasizes the Crawford nickel sulphide project’s economics, permitting progress, and potential production scale, including carbon capture-related incentives. It argues medium/long-term nickel supply is constrained outside Indonesia/China and positions Crawford as a new source. The update is likely supportive for nickel sentiment in the short term.

news_summary: Nickel prices fell on Friday and were set for their worst weekly performance in about a month. The move was attributed to expectations of a growing global nickel surplus in 2023. Analysts also cited possible short-covering rallies, but the near-term market tone remains bearish. | Nickel prices reversed earlier losses but remained on track for their worst weekly decline in a month, pressured by forecasts of a widening global nickel surplus in 2023. While some analysts see potential rallies from fund short-covering and supply risks in Indonesia, the immediate outlook is dominated by surplus concerns. | The LME said Guangxi CNGR New Energy Science & Technology has applied to list its primary nickel brand CNGR. The exchange is working to improve nickel liquidity amid low LME-approved warehouse stocks by speeding brand approvals for nickel deliverable against its contracts. | Reuters reports Indonesia’s high-grade 1.7% nickel reserves could be depleted in about six years, prompting calls for reserve management and more processing of lower-grade ore. It also references Indonesia’s prior export ban, delayed NPI export taxes, and Citi’s warning about supply control risk as nickel prices fall on surpluses. | Indonesia’s miners’ association warns high-grade nickel ore reserves may be depleted in roughly six years. The 1.7% nickel ore is mainly used for nickel pig iron (NPI), a feedstock for stainless steel, while lower grades support EV battery-related products. The risk implies potential future nickel supply tightness.

news_summary: Copper prices inched higher as the weaker U.S. dollar made dollar-priced metals cheaper, but gains were capped by renewed concerns over China demand, with home prices falling again and commentary on rising Chinese refined copper output. LME aluminium also edged up, while nickel rose slightly, indicating mixed near-term pressure. | Indonesia’s state holding Mind Id is negotiating to increase control of Vale Indonesia, Indonesia’s major nickel producer, amid rising resource-nationalism and elections. The key risk is whether Vale Indonesia’s mining concession is extended by December 2025; otherwise its major nickel smelting projects with Chinese investors could be jeopardized. | Indonesia’s mining minister said the government will extend its SIMBARA online tracking system to nickel shipments starting next year, covering ore movements from mines to buyers. The policy aims to improve governance after the platform was first used on coal, potentially reducing supply uncertainty for nickel. | Panoramic Resources is undertaking a strategic review that includes potential sale or partnership options for its Savannah nickel project in Western Australia due to weak nickel/battery metal prices. The review aims to reassess capital requirements and funding in the current market environment. Savannah is a nickel-copper-cobalt mine with prior suspensions. | Reuters reports that under a contract, Trafigura will supply nickel in the range of about 20,000 to roughly 40,000 tonnes per annum. The disclosed supply commitment points to additional availability expectations for nickel, which can pressure prices unless demand or other constraints counterbalance. | Trafigura agreed to invest in a planned all-in-one nickel refinery to be established by Korea Zinc via its nickel sulfate subsidiary, KEMCO. The move increases refining/capacity expectations for nickel, which can reduce fears of near-term supply tightness and exert downward pressure on nickel prices. | Trafigura and Korea Zinc signed an KRW185 billion (about USD140 million) deal to build an all-in-one nickel refinery in Ulsan, South Korea, and secure working capital. Trafigura will supply 20,000–40,000 tonnes of nickel feedstock annually under a long-term agreement with off-take rights. | Trafigura signed a $140 million agreement with Korea Zinc to build an all-in-one nickel refinery in Ulsan, using pyro-hydro processing for nickel matte and MHP. Korea Zinc will set up the project via its nickel sulfate unit KEMCO, with Trafigura taking a stake and securing long-term feedstock supply and off-take rights. | Vale Canada and Sumitomo agreed to sell a 14% stake in their Indonesian nickel miner to state miner MIND ID as required for Indonesia to extend Vale Indonesia’s mining permit, due in 2025. The restructuring increases MIND ID’s share while stabilizing operations, supporting expectations for nickel supply continuity. | Reuters reports that an agreement has paved the way for the renewal of mining permits for Vale’s nickel operations in Indonesia beyond 2025. The development lowers uncertainty about future nickel supply from the site, which can be supportive for nickel prices as markets price in less regulatory disruption. | The article says Chinese firms have invested over $14 billion in Indonesia’s nickel sector over the past decade, building smelters and refineries and expanding related infrastructure. It also notes widespread complaints about working conditions, but does not indicate immediate operational stoppages. Net near-term impact on nickel prices appears limited.

Reuters cites pledges for property financing and debt-risk resolution. LME aluminium also advanced and nickel increased, reflecting broader bullish sentiment across industrial metals. | Korea Zinc and Trafigura signed a KRW185bn (USD140m) deal to build an all-in-one nickel refinery in Ulsan via KEMCO, using an integrated pyro-hydro process for nickel matte and MHP. A long-term contract secures nickel feedstock supply of 20,000–40,000 tonnes per year, supporting nickel supply outlook. | Trafigura will invest $140m for a 12.9% stake in Korea Zinc’s KEMCO to build an all-in-one nickel refinery in Ulsan. The plant will process nickel matte and mixed hydroxide precipitate, supported by Trafigura’s long-term feedstock supply of 20,000–40,000 tpa and offtake rights. | Gama Explorations completed a SkyTEM time-domain EM survey over its 625 km2 Tyee nickel-copper project in Quebec, adding 1,187 line km and generating 66 drill targets. Results from pending analyses are expected to refine targeting for sulphide-related nickel-copper occurrences, but there is no production or supply update. | Gama Explorations says it completed a SkyTEM geophysical survey over its Tyee nickel-copper-cobalt project in Quebec. The EM data is expected to improve drill targeting and highlight additional targets after previously discovered nickel-copper and titanium-vanadium showings, with samples pending analysis.

news_summary: Copper prices climbed to two-month highs as supply risks intensified. An indefinite strike at Peru’s Las Bambas mine and maintenance at First Quantum’s Cobre Panama facility—both tied to protests blocking coal—supported LME and Shanghai copper gains. A weaker U.S. dollar further boosted the base-metals complex, lifting aluminium and nickel too. | Ranchero Gold announced it can earn a 100% interest in the Pinchi Lake nickel project in British Columbia under an option agreement with Recharge Resources. news_summary: Copper prices pulled back from two-month highs as a firmer U.S. dollar and renewed growth concerns weighed on sentiment. However, declines were limited by a supportive supply-demand backdrop, including low inventories, supply disruptions, and strong Chinese demand. Aluminium and nickel also fell modestly amid the macro/dollar influence. | The article reviews Indonesia’s dominant nickel role and its raw nickel export ban since 2020. It also highlights EV-battery supply chain shifts and policy moves in the US and EU, including an anti-subsidy investigation targeting Chinese automakers sourcing Indonesian nickel. The implications are trade- and policy-driven, but timing/magnitude are uncertain.

news_summary: Indonesia believes its ban on exporting unprocessed nickel is boosting domestic value-added. The country has invested heavily in nickel smelting capacity, with downstream provinces showing double-digit growth. The ban has increased nickel-related export value substantially, suggesting improved supply concentration and near-term supportive conditions for nickel prices. | Nornickel’s 2023 has been difficult, though not described as a disaster. The article notes trimmed medium-to-long-term estimates and highlights rapid redirection of sales to Asia. By focusing on the company’s nickel revenue and market access rather than a specific disruption, near-term price pressure appears limited. | Aluminium prices fell to their weakest in over three months as speculative funds increased selling, driven by expectations of rising LME inventories ahead of the Third Wednesday prompt. Weak China data and a strong dollar pressured the wider metals complex, with copper and nickel also declining. | ValOre reported assays showing nickel-copper sulfide mineralization in all four 2023 drill holes at its Pedra Branca Salvador target, with grades including ~0.26–0.29% Ni and ~0.10–0.13% Cu over tens of metres. news_summary: Copper prices rose in London as a weaker dollar improved currency buying power and tighter supply prospects next year were reinforced by recent mine closures/disruptions. The piece also cites China stimulus hopes supporting metals demand. Aluminium also gained, while nickel edged lower, suggesting mixed cross-metal performance. | Copper prices reversed earlier gains after U.S. CPI reinforced expectations that the Fed will keep interest rates higher for longer. A weaker outlook for early rate cuts supported the dollar, weighing on growth-sensitive industrial metals. The piece also notes nickel and aluminium price movements alongside copper, while supply disruptions remain supportive.

news_summary: Copper prices fell after China’s Central Economic Work Conference readout disappointed investors by omitting large fiscal stimulus measures, worsening expectations for demand from the world’s top consumer. LME three-month copper and Shanghai January copper both dipped, while nickel also declined and aluminium rose modestly. | Nickel North Exploration announced amended and restated option agreement terms for selling interests in its Hawk Ridge nickel-copper project in Quebec to 1844 Resources. The amendment adjusts cash/deposit timing and share payments for staged option exercises up to 100% ownership. It is an exploration-deal update with no stated production changes. | IGO’s Ivan Vella-led IGO warned investors to expect another write-down on its Western Areas nickel assets after job losses began at the Cosmos nickel project. The company says the $1.3 billion acquisition value may be impaired again, indicating weakening nickel project outlook and potential supply uncertainty. | Aluminium prices fell to their lowest in nearly 15 months as oversupply expectations outweighed demand hopes. LME data showed funds’ net short positions at the highest since May 2020. The firmer U.S. dollar also pressured base metals, with aluminium weaker and copper and nickel also down. | Quebec Nickel Corp. says it has completed 2023 exploration at its Ducros Ni-Cu-PGE project in Quebec, drilling 17,815 metres in 50 holes and advancing a scoping-level metallurgical study. It reports discovery of large nickel-cobalt-bearing ultramafic intrusions and new targets from AI, with environmental baseline work started. | South32 has invoked force majeure at its Colombia ferro-nickel operation, signaling an interruption to production. Such disruptions can lower near-term ferro-nickel availability and tighten nickel supply expectations, which usually weighs on nickel prices over the first days of the event.

news_summary: London copper was steady after a sharp prior-session jump as dovish Fed expectations pressured the dollar near four-month lows. Three-month LME copper was almost flat, while January SHFE copper rose. In the same session, aluminium fell and nickel declined, though the week trend was mixed and copper ended stronger weekly. | The UK announced new sanctions restricting imports of certain Russian-made metals and banning exports of dual-use goods. The restricted list includes copper, nickel and aluminium. Markets reacted with sharp overnight moves: palladium jumped nearly 12% and copper logged its largest intraday gain in 11 months, indicating immediate supply-risk pricing. | BHP warned that Australia’s critical minerals strategy overhaul offers no relief for the struggling nickel industry. BHP said its Nickel West operations face the same pressures that have led to mine closures, with further job losses at its mines. Panoramic Resources also entered administration this week. | Indonesia’s leading political candidates are expected to maintain Jokowi’s nickel ore export restrictions. Since the 2020 ban, foreign investment has flowed into domestic smelting, increasing nickel pig iron output. With Indonesia producing a large share of global nickel, continued export limits could tighten supply dynamics and support nickel prices near term.

news_summary: Nickel 28 reported Q3 results for its 8.56% stake in the Ramu nickel-cobalt operation in Papua New Guinea. news_summary: Copper prices in London were steady near a five-month high as a softer U.S. dollar—driven by expectations for potential Fed interest rate cuts—supported dollar-priced metals. Three-month LME copper was almost unchanged while SHFE copper edged higher. LME aluminium was flat and nickel slightly lower, reflecting mixed but mostly mild macro effects. | An explosion at a Chinese-owned nickel smelter in Indonesia’s Sulawesi killed at least 18 workers and prompted police to order the plant to stop operating pending investigation. The incident is part of Indonesia-China nickel processing expansion, potentially disrupting near-term nickel output and supporting prices. | Copper retreated from a five-month high as investors took profits ahead of year-end, with concerns about Chinese growth/property. Aluminium fell after LME inventories built to six-month highs. Nickel was the largest decliner as LME nickel inventories rose to levels not seen since July 2022.

news_summary: London copper edged higher as China manufacturing improved and investors looked for fresh stimulus, while SHFE copper benefited from low warehouse inventories. Aluminium and nickel were weaker on the firm dollar. Overall, the piece signals supportive demand expectations for copper but mixed impacts for other industrial metals. | Canada Nickel completed a C$34.7m flow-through private placement at C$1.77 per unit, with Agnico Eagle becoming about a 12% shareholder. Management says proceeds will fund continued advancement of its Timmins Nickel District and related initiatives. The deal may mildly improve sentiment on near-term nickel supply prospects. | Canada Nickel’s investor presentation highlights the Crawford nickel sulphide project with detailed economics (NPV/IRR), expected nickel production, and a mid-2025 permitting target. It argues the nickel market is structurally tight outside Indonesia/China and suggests a new North American supply source could emerge, supporting nickel sentiment. | Canadian North Resources reported BHTEM results from deep drillholes at its Ferguson Lake project, indicating the West Zone extends more than 200 metres down-dip with high-grade Cu-Ni-PGM sulphides. The findings support prospects for continued expansion, but they do not imply near-term changes to metal supply. | Canadian North Resources reported borehole geophysical survey results indicating the West Zone at its Ferguson Lake project extends further down-dip by more than 200 metres at depths around 650 to >850 metres. The mineralization is described as Cu-Ni massive sulphides plus PGE zones, but no near-term production or supply changes were announced. | GFNI said it has secured a new agreement with Baosteel for up to 1.5 million WMT of nickel ore for 2024, priced monthly based on prevailing market values. The ore will come from GFNI subsidiaries operating nickel mines in the Philippines, reinforcing nickel ore demand for Asia-Pacific buyers. | Agnico Eagle acquired a 12% minority stake in Canada Nickel via a flow-through offering, supporting the development of the Crawford nickel-cobalt project in Ontario. The deal is an investment in critical minerals, but it does not directly change near-term nickel supply or demand conditions. | Agnico Eagle Mines bought a minority stake in Canada Nickel through a flow-through offering, purchasing 19.6 million units for about $23.1 million. Canada Nickel owns the Crawford nickel-cobalt sulphide project. The deal is framed as investment in critical minerals, but no near-term production change is indicated. | Agnico Eagle completed a $34.7 million investment in Canada Nickel, buying 19.6 million flow-through units at $1.77. Canada Nickel says proceeds will fund work to advance its Timmins Nickel District and the Crawford Nickel Project, supporting development of future nickel supply. | Global Ferronickel said it will sell 1.5 million wet metric tons of nickel ore to Baosteel under renewed purchasing arrangements. The ore is sourced from its mines in Surigao del Norte and Palawan, with monthly pricing tied to prevailing market nickel prices, supporting nickel-related supply expectations in Asia.

news_summary: Copper prices eased as the U.S. dollar firmed on reduced expectations for early, aggressive rate cuts and as Chinese buying slowed with seasonal weak demand and strong domestic output. LME three-month copper and SHFE February fell, alongside a sharp drop in the Yangshan premium. Aluminium also slipped; nickel rose. | Copper fell to a two-week low as the U.S. dollar rose and China’s industrial demand worries intensified after weak December manufacturing PMI data. Expectations of surplus widened cash discounts. The article also shows softer LME prices for aluminium and nickel on the same risk-off, demand- and FX-driven backdrop. | An explosion at a Chinese-owned nickel smelter in Morowali, Sulawesi killed at least 19 workers and injured dozens. Operations were halted by police while investigations continue after a furnace accident involving molten metal residue and a subsequent fire. Workers protested demanding smelter shutdowns and better safety measures.

news_summary: Indonesian nickel miner AKP launches an IPO and after listing will sell 60% to South Korean buyer LX International via Energi Battery Indonesia. LX plans to expand nickel production capacity and supply battery-related offtake. The transaction value is about Rp 1.59 trillion, with AKP near Morowali. | Indonesia’s industry minister said the government may penalize ITSS, which operates a nickel smelter at Morowali’s IMIP, after a deadly explosion in late December 2023. news_summary: Copper rebounded on Friday, supported by a weaker dollar and hopes tied to the Fed’s potential rate cuts, though gains were limited as December minutes suggested rates may stay high longer. Supply tension from low stocks in China supported prices. LME aluminium and nickel also rose modestly. | Copper prices edged up as supply concerns and thin Chinese stocks supported spot premiums, offsetting changes in investor expectations for U.S. Fed rate cuts. LME aluminium was stable and nickel slightly higher, but the main focus was copper’s supply-tightening backdrop and macro-driven risk appetite. | Reuters reports copper at a three-week low as a stronger U.S. dollar and reduced odds of early Fed rate cuts pressured LME prices. It also notes nickel down on persistent surplus and improving LME warehouse stocks, while aluminium declined alongside broader weaker metals sentiment. | Jindal Stainless asked India’s Ministry of Steel for continued zero customs duty on stainless steel scrap, steel scrap and pure nickel, and for a long-term exemption on basic customs duty for ferro nickel and ferro molybdenum. It also sought measures against dumping and fixed tariff values for stainless steel.

China’s unchanged policy rate and softer pre-holiday replenishment weighed, but mine-side disruption could reduce refined output. LME aluminium edged up, while nickel fell slightly amid mixed moves across metals. | First Quantum Minerals will suspend mining at the Ravensthorpe nickel operation in Western Australia for up to three years and cut 30% of staff, while continuing to process ore stockpiles. The decision follows weaker nickel prices and is intended to preserve viability until nickel prices recover. | Samsung SDI invested $18.8m for an 8.9% stake in Canada Nickel, a Toronto-based junior developing Ontario’s Timmins-Cochrane camp/Crawford Nickel sulphide project. The move is intended to secure critical nickel inputs for EV battery production, boosting confidence in future nickel supply demand. | Copper prices fell after the dollar strengthened and Chinese demand worries resurfaced when the central bank left key rates unchanged. Aluminium also declined and nickel retreated. Separately, Nyrstar will suspend zinc smelting in the Netherlands due to high energy costs, potentially tightening Europe’s zinc market but not directly tracked metals. | Samsung SDI will buy an 8.7% stake in Canada Nickel for $18.5 million and obtain 10% of nickel-cobalt output from its Crawford project, plus further production rights for 15 years. The deal aims to secure battery mineral supply in North America, potentially supporting near-term nickel demand expectations. | The article forecasts continued bearish conditions in nickel as China and Indonesia capacity expansions have turned the refined nickel market from short supply to oversupplied. LME and SHFE nickel prices collapsed in 2023, and analysts expect ongoing surpluses in 2024, though less severe than 2023. | First Quantum will cease mining at Western Australia’s Ravensthorpe nickel and cobalt mine as part of a cost reduction plan, cutting more than 300 jobs. The decision follows large operating losses and continued nickel price declines, implying reduced near-term nickel supply and potential price support. | Samsung SDI will acquire an 8.7% stake in Canada Nickel and secure rights to 10% of nickel-cobalt production from the Crawford project in Ontario, with an option for additional output for 15 years. The deal is aimed at stabilizing battery raw-material supply and signals firmer nickel demand. | First Quantum Minerals said it will reduce operations at its Australian nickel mine by suspending mining at the Shoemaker Levy ore body. The move directly lowers near-term nickel output, tightening expected supply and likely providing upward support for nickel prices in the short term. | First Quantum says it is halting operations at an Australian nickel mine, attributing the decision to weak nickel prices. The move signals continued price softness in the nickel market, likely reinforcing bearish near-term expectations for nickel demand and pricing. | First Quantum is suspending production at its Ravensthorpe nickel mine in Australia, citing low nickel prices amid weaker steel and EV demand. It also remains heavily impacted by Panama’s Supreme Court forcing closure of its Cobre Panama copper mine, driving asset sales and refinancing efforts. | First Quantum said it will cut production at its Australian Raventhorpe nickel mine and reduce the workforce by about 30%, citing a significant downturn in nickel prices. The article links the prior price weakness to high supply and low demand, with Raventhorpe already running losses.

news_summary: An AFP dispatch, citing a US NGO report, says a Chinese/French-financed nickel smelting hub at Weda Bay Industrial Park on Halmahera has driven deforestation and air and water pollution, relying on multiple coal plants. The report urges mitigation of emissions and tailings handling, with concerns about Indonesia’s nickel expansion and labor risks. | Copper prices edged up on Friday as the dollar retreated, but Reuters notes the contract is on track for a fourth straight weekly decline amid weak Chinese economic data and concerns over rate cuts. Aluminium and nickel also moved modestly, reflecting broader FX/macro sentiment. | Centaurus Metals received technical approval from Brazil’s National Mining Agency (ANM) for its Mining Lease Application (PAE) for the Jaguar Nickel Sulphide Project. Pending environmental clearance for the mining lease (LI), the company expects to secure permits in H2 2024. Approval reduces permitting risk and supports nickel supply expectations. | Copper prices in London rebounded on Friday, supported by oversold technical signals and lower LME on-warrant copper stocks hitting a four-month low after cancellations. Gains are tempered by cautious trading ahead of Chinese New Year and a firmer dollar. Aluminium and nickel also ticked up alongside copper. | Power Nickel started its 2024 winter drill program at the Nisk nickel sulfide project in Nemaska, Quebec, targeting new zones and continuing drilling at Nisk Main and other targets. The update is aimed at extending high-grade nickel-copper PGM mineralization, which may support near-term nickel sentiment. | Canada Nickel reported corporate updates including extending a US$12 million Auramet loan repayment to Feb. 16, 2024, and entering agreements to acquire additional mining claims in Ontario in exchange for shares and cash. It also notes equity issuances tied to exploration agreements and bank fees for flow-through financing. | Canada Nickel Company says its Crawford nickel sulphide project is “rapidly advancing,” with environmental permitting ongoing and plans to finalize construction in 2025 and start building in 2026. The CEO cites agreed highway and Hydro One re-routing plans and potential milling arrangements, alongside new core investors, supporting bullish sentiment for nickel.

news_summary: Copper opened lower as a steady dollar and softer demand expectations from China weighed on sentiment ahead of U.S. data. Three-month copper on the LME slid 0.2% and Shanghai prices were flat. The story also notes declines in nickel and aluminium, reflecting broader downside pressure in industrial metals. | Copper fell as a firmer dollar ahead of U.S. data and caution over China’s demand outweighed supply concerns. SHFE on-warrant stocks rose for a fifth week and spot trading weakened around early Chinese New Year. Aluminium and nickel also declined, reflecting broader risk sentiment. | The report says sentiment improved as US GDP beat expectations and rate-cut expectations eased. For pure nickel and nickel sulfate, it cites Indonesia policy concerns, stronger electrowinning costs, and a shortage of spot supplies as support. Indonesian RKAB approval disruption is seen as limited so far, implying nickel prices rebound. | SMM’s review highlights weak 2023 demand for pure nickel and strong supply growth from newly released electrowinning capacity, alongside rising LME/warehouse receipts and accumulating deliverable inventories. With pure nickel surplus persisting and imports/clearances falling sharply, nickel prices softened from about 208,000 yuan/ton to ~140,000 yuan by November. | Nornickel expects nickel output to fall again in 2024 to 184,000–194,000 tons after a 5% decline in 2023. The company links the reduction to ongoing geopolitical risks affecting operations and also plans capital repairs at its Nadezhda flash smelting furnace. Reuters reported the forecast. | Copper slid as traders reinforced concerns about China’s near-term demand, citing further stress in the property sector after Evergrande liquidation and weaker profit data. A firmer U.S. dollar and rising SHFE warehouse stocks also pressured prices. Aluminium and nickel also fell on demand-focused sentiment.

It also announced a $100m buyback plan and dividend policy. | Australian shares rose as investors awaited retail sales data and watched U.S. Fed guidance. Mining stocks climbed as metal prices rose. LME aluminium fell, and nickel dropped further on continued supply-glut pressure and a streak of monthly declines, while overall market sentiment stayed cautious. | The piece reports weak performance in nickel and stainless steel. Pure nickel sees declining restocking and weak spot trading sentiment, while NPI transactions remain light near the holiday. Nickel sulfate has a persistent spot shortage that may support its price, but overall sentiment and trading activity suppress nickel. | Premium Nickel Resources reports additional underground drill assays at Selebi North in Botswana, including 2.88% NiEq over 30.45m with Ni and Cu grades, plus 9.55m at 3.94% NiEq. Results suggest mineralization extends below prior intersections. Material applies mainly to nickel and copper, but global price impact is likely minimal. | Copper Lake Resources provides an exploration update for its Marshall Lake VMS copper-zinc-silver project in Ontario, reporting modelled magneto-telluric conductors and planning additional large-loop EM surveying. news_summary: Nickel prices fell for five straight days, with sentiment pressured after Fed comments reduced expectations for early rate cuts. Fundamentals are mixed: inventory is accumulated and spot transactions soften into the Spring Festival, while Indonesian RKAB approval is slowly improving supply. Ferronickel slightly rebounds, but nickel is still reported as trending lower. | Shanghai nickel and LME nickel have been declining week-on-week, with weak spot transactions ahead of the Spring Festival. Inventories are accumulating domestically and abroad. Supply/demand balance looks soft as output rises earlier but production suspensions approach. A stronger dollar and Indonesian election-related uncertainty are expected to keep nickel prices weak. | Sumitomo reduced its Ambatovy nickel output forecast to about 30,000 tonnes for the year to March 31, from roughly 40,000 previously, citing equipment malfunctions and a utility plant glitch that is not yet clearly resolved. The downgrade coincides with weak nickel prices and worsened financial losses. | Copper prices fell to their lowest in nearly two weeks as the dollar climbed and sentiment around China’s property sector worsened. Traders cited weak credit impulse and fading expectations of Fed cuts after strong U.S. jobs data. While mining issues offered some support, aluminium and nickel also edged lower. | Australia-based RMC acquired a 75% stake in Vancouver Mineral Resources, gaining the Mpanda and Mbozi copper-gold projects in Tanzania. The areas have a history of copper mining and are along strike from high-grade Singililwa copper operations. The deal expands RMC’s Ni-Cu exploration base but provides no immediate production impact.
