news_summary: The troubled Avebury nickel mine near Zeehan will close and move to care and maintenance, ending production and reducing employment. The decision is attributed to sustained weak LME nickel prices and expanded Indonesian supply. Receivers cite an inability to sell the asset under current nickel market conditions, implying reduced supply. | Tasmanian Avebury Nickel Mine is returning to care and maintenance after a sustained collapse in nickel prices, leading to over 200 job losses. Local leaders say the closure will harm regional supply chains. Reduced nickel production capacity could support short-term nickel price expectations, despite limited market detail in the article. | Anglo American said Q4 nickel output rose 9% due to better operational stability, while copper production fell 6% as Los Bronces in Chile declined despite higher output from Peru’s Quellaveco. These company-level production changes may influence short-term supply expectations, but no market-moving disruptions are cited. | Anglo American said overall output fell 7% in 4Q, largely due to lower copper output and planned slowdown at iron ore operations. Copper production declined 6% as Los Bronces faced lower grades/harder ore. Nickel production rose 9% on improved stability, partially offsetting metal supply pressures. | Western Australia plans to proceed with a critical minerals trade pact with Indonesia despite concerns that Indonesia’s drive for EV battery production has flooded global markets with cheap nickel. The resulting pricing pressure has led to local mine closures and job losses, highlighting bearish near-term nickel supply expectations. | Canada Nickel’s subsidiary NetZero Metals plans two downstream facilities in Timmins: a large nickel processing plant and a stainless-steel/alloy production unit, using CO2 storage linked to the Crawford nickel project. Feasibility is underway and production is targeted for 2027, implying limited immediate nickel-price impact. | The piece highlights a Canadian miner’s plan to invest about $1 billion in a nickel processing plant aimed at EV demand. Such announcements can affect short-term nickel sentiment by shaping expectations for EV-linked nickel demand and the pace of future processing capacity. | Canada Nickel plans a US$1 billion nickel processing plant in Ontario for EV supply chain needs, targeting 80,000+ tons annually and operations by early 2027, plus an additional US$2 billion stainless steel/alloy facility. The company links its plans to reduced reliance on Indonesia/China amid recent nickel price declines. | A Canadian miner plans a US$1 billion nickel processing plant focused on EV-related supply. The project highlights investment in nickel refining capacity tied to battery demand, which can improve market sentiment and near-term expectations for nickel supply/demand balance. | Canada Nickel plans to build two >$1B processing plants near Timmins, including a nickel processing facility feeding its Crawford nickel sulphide project and a large stainless-steel/alloy plant. Feasibility and design work is underway with targeted 2027 startup, supported by major investors and clean-carbon ambitions. | Canada Nickel’s unit NetZero Metals plans to raise about $1 billion to build an Ontario nickel-processing plant, aiming for 80,000 tonnes annually, with production starting in 2027. It also develops a nickel mine in Ontario. The move targets “clean” nickel supply for North America, where battery demand is expected to rise. | Canada Nickel Company’s subsidiary NetZero Metals plans to develop two facilities in Timmins—a nickel processing plant and a stainless-steel/alloy production facility—using carbon storage from its Crawford Nickel project. The project is in selection and feasibility stages, with production targeted for 2027, implying little immediate effect on nickel prices. | Vulcan Minerals reports highly anomalous soil geochemical results at its Red Cross Lake nickel-copper-cobalt project, including peak nickel of 3,936 ppm and elevated copper values, with drilling and permitting planned for 2024. The release is exploration-focused and does not indicate any immediate marketable supply changes. | Vulcan Minerals reports strong soil geochemical anomalies at its Red Cross Lake nickel-copper-cobalt project in Newfoundland, including very high nickel and associated copper values, plus plans to initiate permitting and drilling in 2024. The release is exploration-focused with no confirmed resource or production impact, making near-term price effects limited. | Canada Nickel plans to build two facilities in Timmins, Ontario: a nickel processing plant and a stainless steel/alloys plant to support the EV battery supply chain. The company targets zero-carbon production using its carbon storage tech, with operations expected in 2027. Ontario also aims to make Timmins a nickel hub. | Canada Nickel seeks government funding and partners to build a large nickel processing facility in northeastern Ontario, targeting EV battery and stainless-steel supply-chain needs. The company cites a lack of new processing capacity amid rising projected nickel demand. Governments indicate willingness to explore proposals; project timelines extend to 2027. | Canada Nickel says it is seeking government funding and partners to build a large nickel processing facility and a steel plant in Timmins, Ontario, aimed at closing a gap in EV supply chains. The company is not yet in feasibility and expects production only from 2027, constraining immediate price effects.

news_summary: Canada Nickel plans to build two processing facilities near Timmins, including a large nickel-processing plant partly aimed at electric-vehicle feedstock demand. The roughly $1 billion project depends on uncertain funding but management expects substantial government support via critical-mineral tax credits and funds. Approval of the Crawford mine is targeted next year. | Shanghai non-ferrous metals review reports January 2024 nickel sulfate production at 35,200 mt, up 13.9% MoM on recovery in downstream precursor demand. For February, production is forecast to fall to 29,000 mt amid Spring Festival-related holiday dynamics and expected plant shutdowns. | Prismo Metals reports that a heliborne ZTEM survey identified a new deep conductive drill target at its Hot Breccia copper project in Arizona. The company cites historic copper/zinc indications and expects permits in Q1 2024 to drill. The update is copper-focused with no immediate market-wide impact described. | Norilsk Nickel revised its 2024 capital expenditure outlook down to $3–3.2 billion from about $3.6 billion, citing clarified technical parameters and payment procedure optimization. The CFO said the board approved the earlier higher budget, indicating a shift toward reduced spending by the company amid 2024 planning. | Canada Nickel Company, via NetZero Metals, plans to invest around $1bn to build a nickel processing facility in Timmins, with capacity above 80,000t/year. A second stainless/alloy plant is also planned. Both are scheduled to start in 2027, limiting immediate price impact. | NetZero Metals, a subsidiary of Canada Nickel Company, intends to build a nickel processing facility and a stainless-steel and alloy production facility in Ontario’s Timmins Nickel District. The projects are still in site-selection and feasibility stages, with nickel production targeted for 2027, and focus on low-carbon output using the Crawford project’s carbon storage.

news_summary: Glencore outlined plans to sell its stake in Koniambo Nickel SAS after concluding the project remains unprofitable amid weak nickel prices, which have fallen nearly half since the start of last year. Glencore has funded the operation for over ten years and cannot justify continued losses. | Copper was little changed in thin holiday trading as China’s Lunar New Year holidays weighed on sentiment. Traders awaited U.S. January CPI for cues on Fed rate cut timing, which could move the dollar and support dollar-priced metals. LME aluminium was up slightly, nickel flat. | Australia’s junior nickel explorers are upbeat despite recent job losses and mine closures amid a nickel price slump and fast-rising Indonesia supply. However, they expect the nickel surplus to flip to deficit, cite tightening sulphide concentrate terms, and reference possible WA royalties relief and federal tax-credit support. | Power Nickel reported fall drilling results at its Nisk Main nickel-sulfide PGM project near Nemaska, Quebec, including intersections with ~0.62–0.71% Ni and associated copper grades. The company says the program expands its resource footprint and plans further winter drilling; no near-term production or offtake changes are stated. | Power Nickel reports successful fall drilling extending resource mineralization at its Nisk project using ambient noise technology, expanding its resource footprint. It plans winter drilling to test four ANT targets. The news is focused on exploration and future resource growth, with no immediate production or supply/inventory impact. | Glencore plans to quit its struggling nickel mine in New Caledonia, described as a blow for France. The decision signals potential supply disruption and weaker near-term nickel output, which is likely to affect nickel market expectations over the next days to weeks. | Copper prices extended gains as LME-registered warehouse inventories fell to the lowest level since September, with outflows of 850 tons. Investors awaited U.S. inflation data for clues on Fed rate cuts, improving near-term sentiment. Aluminium and nickel also rose modestly alongside copper. | SRQ Resources reports further characterisation of Lac Brulé’s ultramafic complex, finding nickel- and copper-bearing sulphides (pyrrhotite, chalcopyrite, cobalt-rich pentlandite) with drill intervals reporting up to ~0.79% Ni and ~0.64% Cu, and outlines a 2024 exploration program. No production changes are announced. | Nickel 28 reports operating updates from its 8.56% interest in the Ramu nickel-cobalt operation in Papua New Guinea. Q4 nickel production fell 8% due to unplanned earthquake outages, while full-year output stayed near nameplate capacity. The company also notes future work, including a planned 30-day September shutdown. | Nickel 28 updates Ramu’s Q4 and full-year 2023 results, including nickel production/sales and higher MHP availability. It cites falling LME nickel and cobalt prices in 2023 and notes planned maintenance in 2024. As a largely retrospective operational release, it’s unlikely to materially shift near-term nickel prices. | Premium Nickel Resources reports additional underground drill assay results at its Selebi North mine in Botswana, highlighting long intersections with strong nickel and copper grades (NiEq-based). The company plans further down-plunge drilling to support a future NI 43-101 Mineral Resource Estimation, with no stated production or shipment changes. | China and Indonesia, producing about 70% of global nickel supply, are set to cut output by at least 100,000 tonnes this year to limit losses from a continuing nickel price slide. Analysts suggest deeper reductions may be needed to eliminate surplus and lift prices after previous cuts failed. | Alaska Energy Metals reported a major increase in the Eureka deposit resource at its Nikolai nickel project, with total contained nickel rising to about 8.1 billion lbs (including large indicated and inferred categories) and grades around 0.22–0.21% nickel plus copper. The higher-grade core could improve economics, but production is not yet impacted. | Glencore will stop further funding for its Koniambo nickel project, having invested billions since 2013, citing persistent losses and operational issues. The article places this in a wider context of weaker nickel prices, high-cost curtailments, and a still-tight supply outlook dominated by growing Indonesian output and a reported surplus. | Glencore said it will move its Koniambo nickel mine in New Caledonia to “care and maintenance” and look for a new partner after a decade of losses. The decision follows government discussions on cost relief and potential relaxation of nickel export rules amid weak nickel prices.

news_summary: DRC state miner Gecamines says it intends to acquire ERG’s copper and cobalt assets in the DRC, with funding to buy three assets. The transfer could affect near-term copper supply expectations and production resumption prospects for ERG’s Boss Mining, where copper output is cited. | Glencore is selling its 49% stake in the Koniambo nickel operation, citing high operating costs and unfavorable market conditions. The move comes amid a nickel glut, demand disappointments for EV-related metal, and sizable but insufficient supply curbs from China and Indonesia, keeping prices under pressure. | BHP said it will record a $2.5 billion non-cash impairment charge related to its Western Australia nickel business. The miner linked the charge to industry challenges and continued pressure on nickel prices, highlighting weak demand/profitability conditions that likely sustain bearish sentiment for nickel in the near term. | BHP said it will book $2.5 billion of non-cash impairments for its Western Australia Nickel business and $3.2 billion related to the Samarco dam failure. It also lowered nickel price assumptions due to a worsening short- and medium-term outlook, citing higher capital costs from inflation. | BHP said it will record about $2.5 billion in impairment charges for its Western Australian nickel operations and increase Samarco dam-related provisions. The miner plans to mothball the Kambalda concentrator in June and may suspend Nickel West. Nickel prices have fallen sharply since early 2023. | BHP has written down the value of its nickel business by $5.4 billion pre-tax, warning that full suspension could be possible. Western Australia is urging federal action, including potential royalty relief, to protect about 3,000 jobs and key growth projects as some nickel miners have already shut.

news_summary: Australia’s nickel industry faces severe pressure after the nickel price plunged as Indonesia sharply expanded supply. BHP has written down Nickel West and considers care-and-maintenance. Government funding and temporary royalty rebates aim to protect jobs, but oversupply dynamics from Indonesia are highlighted as weighing on nickel prices. | BHP reported an 86% fall in profits attributed to nickel-related impacts and ongoing losses tied to Brazil’s Samacro dam disaster. The dam failure raises operational and cost risk for nickel output, which can disrupt supply expectations in the near term and weigh on nickel sentiment. | Copper prices inched lower as a stronger U.S. dollar pressured dollar-priced commodities, while traders assessed China demand after the Lunar New Year. China’s 25-bp cut to the 5-year mortgage reference rate aims to stimulate credit and the property market. LME aluminium fell and nickel edged lower. | BHP reported a sharp 86% fall in first-half net income, driven by a $2.5 billion impairment on Australian nickel assets after an oversupply shock. Indonesia’s higher production ballooned global supply and pressured nickel benchmarks lower. BHP said nickel could stay over-supplied for years, with potential mothballing. | BHP’s CEO faced heavy media attention on nickel despite the metal being a small share of revenue. The coverage reflects a broader nickel-sector crisis, with mines shutting and jobs being lost, prompting federal government discussions on financial support. The news implies continuing stress in nickel market fundamentals. | BHP says its nickel business is unlikely to be profitable until 2030 and is weighing mothballing Nickel West, putting jobs at risk. Management links the outlook to a sharp Indonesia-led nickel supply surge at lower costs, particularly hurting class-one nickel, implying persistent imbalance until the end of the decade. | BHP reported an 86% plunge in half-year profit, including large writedowns of its Western Australian nickel assets. The company said the nickel market weakened due to a surge in Indonesian exports and changing refining pathways for low-quality nickel used in batteries. The piece also references related legal costs in Brazil. | BHP reported an 86% slump in first-half net income after impairing $2.5 billion of Australian nickel assets amid an oversupplied nickel market. Indonesia’s fast production ramp crushed prices and closed Australian projects. BHP warned nickel remains loss-making and may shut Nickel West without stronger incentives. | Copper rose modestly on Tuesday after China cut mortgage rates, boosting expectations for construction and demand from the world’s largest metals consumer. Gains were capped as investors awaited further policy support and mining-company earnings. Aluminium eased on higher LME inventories, while nickel edged higher. | Fathom Nickel Inc. announced it closed the second and final tranche of its private placement, raising about C$1.19M (total offering C$4.57M). Proceeds will fund exploration and development of its nickel sulphide projects in Saskatchewan. The release does not mention production or supply changes. | SMM says the battery-grade nickel sulfate index is 27,222 yuan/ton, up 259 yuan/ton from the prior working day. It cites strong demand after the Spring Festival, still-active inquiries, tight spot supply from incomplete salt-plant restarts, and higher MHP raw-material coefficients supporting costs. | Horizonte Minerals says its Araguaia nickel project capital requirement has jumped to about $1bn, with cost-to-complete rising 87% from the prior budget. The company is engaging shareholders and lenders to secure full funding and restructure debt, which could increase delays or uncertainty around nickel supply. | Canada Nickel reports assay results from initial infill drilling in the Bannockburn “B Zone,” with multiple intervals grading 0.27–0.29% nickel and higher-grade sections of 0.3%+ nickel, including 16.5m at 0.54%. The company plans further drilling and an initial resource later in 2024. | BNN Bloomberg reports that aluminum and nickel jumped after the U.S. signaled major sanctions on Russia. Such sanctions can disrupt Russian metal supply and logistics, tightening availability in the near term and improving price expectations for aluminium and nickel over the coming days. | Aluminium and nickel prices rose after the US signaled major sanctions on Russia. The market reaction suggests traders expected reduced Russian output and/or exports, tightening near-term supply for both metals and lifting prices in the following sessions. | The Biden administration supports Australia’s push to classify “clean” nickel under higher environmental and labour standards. US officials and industry are urged to persuade global pricing agencies to distinguish this from “dirty” nickel linked to cheaper, higher-supply imports that have flooded markets and pressured Australian miners. | BHP CEO Mike Henry says nickel’s downturn is being driven by a boom in Indonesian supply that is expected to keep prices soft for some time, potentially to the end of the decade. BHP also flagged possible closure of Nickel West after nickel impairment, reinforcing bearish near-term nickel sentiment.

news_summary: Copper prices opened the week lower as the dollar firmed and higher inventories in top consumer China weighed sentiment. Three-month LME copper fell 0.4% and Shanghai March copper slid 0.3%. The piece attributes pressure to upcoming U.S., Japan, and Europe economic data influencing rates. | Fortescue founder Andrew Forrest urged the LME to classify nickel contracts as “clean” and “dirty” to give buyers better choice amid a collapse in nickel prices after cheaper Indonesian supply surged. He argues Australian nickel is higher-standard, supporting calls for a green premium and reducing reputational and compliance risk for EV batteries. | Andrew Forrest’s Wyloo Metals plans to shortly shut down West Australian nickel mines bought last year. The decision follows months of weaker global nickel prices blamed on Indonesia’s rapid nickel-industry expansion. The move highlights deteriorating profitability and near-term uncertainty for nickel supply and sentiment. | Indonesia’s mining ministry approved mining quotas (RKAB) covering 145 million tonnes of nickel ore and 14 million tonnes of bauxite, following earlier delays. The ministry is working on further RKAB approvals, currently focusing on nickel and tin, which may lift expected near-term supply for nickel and aluminium feedstock. | Aluminium fell to a one-month low after Shanghai inventories jumped and the U.S. did not impose sanctions on Russian metal, easing supply-risk fears that had supported prices last week. LME aluminium weakened despite earlier expectations of a sanctions package, while nickel also softened on the same news backdrop. | Eramet’s CEO says Indonesia’s low-cost nickel producers could squeeze out global rivals, with Indonesia possibly exceeding 75% of “highest class” pure nickel in five years. The piece links prior Indonesia-led oversupply and demand headwinds (stainless steel and EV battery shifts) to continued nickel price weakness. | Core Nickel received conditional approval for a C$207,300 (nonrepayable) grant from Manitoba’s mineral development fund to support Q1 drilling on its Halfway Lake nickel exploration targets and also obtained a multi-year exploration work permit valid to 2026. The news is nickel-specific and supportive of exploration momentum. | Premium Nickel Resources reported additional underground drilling assay results from the Selebi North (SNUG) nickel-copper-cobalt sulphide deposit in Botswana. The highlight is SNUG-23-067 with 110.75m at 2.56% NiEq (including intervals up to 4.40% NiEq). Drilling and BHEM surveys continue to extend mineralization down plunge. | Fortescue’s Andrew Forrest urged the LME to classify nickel contracts as “clean” and “dirty” to give buyers choice and enable a green premium for lower-carbon Australian nickel. The push follows price weakness after more Indonesian nickel supply entered the market and comes alongside job losses and mine maintenance. | US/EU sanctions tied to alleged Russia developments and EU asset freezes are expected to disrupt Russian metal supply. With Russian nickel a major share of global and LME deliverable inventory, and with RKAB/Indonesia nickel-ore approval delays plus tight nickel-salt and ferronickel supply, nickel prices jumped sharply. | SMM reports Shanghai nickel falling on Feb 26, with weaker intraday activity and declining volume/open interest, suggesting longs are cautious and bulls are closing. Macro sentiment is said to be largely priced in, while faster Indonesia RKAB review reduces expectations of tight nickel ore supply. Outlook is weak nickel price fluctuation. | Nickel pig iron/ferronickel prices increased, with SMM 8–12% high-nickel pig iron averaging 945 yuan per nickel point. The report links gains to Indonesia’s RKAB policy effects lifting nickel ore prices and higher input costs, alongside expected February production declines. Demand is weakening, but RKAB approval shortfalls provide emotional support. | The UK, US and EU added measures to penalize Russia, but they avoided direct sanctions on Russia-origin aluminium and nickel. US imports are already minimal due to high tariffs, while Europe could be more affected: Russian shares in EU aluminium and nickel imports have fallen, and new logistics issues may tighten supply. | The UK/US/EU imposed new measures against Russia, including attempts to directly sanction Russian aluminium and nickel. US exposure is limited, but EU imports of Russian aluminium (~10% of ingot imports) have fallen and EU nickel sourcing has declined. Alternative supply faces duties and shipping disruptions, tightening availability. | Australia plans 50% royalty relief for Western Australian nickel producers when quarterly average nickel prices fall below $20,000/t. The March 2024–18 month rebate is repayable later. It follows nickel’s >40% fall amid Indonesia supply and is meant to support struggling miners and critical-minerals financing. | Glencore reports lower 2023 metals EBITDA driven by weaker market prices for cobalt, nickel, and zinc. The piece cites nickel price declines on LME and oversupply from Indonesian MHP. It also notes higher copper and nickel operating costs (including less by-product credits), but offers no clear near-term trigger for price direction. | US announced new Russia sanctions affecting 500+ entities, but not the base metals industry. Aluminium and nickel had risen beforehand on expectations sanctions would restrict supplies. After the announcement, aluminium fell 1.1% and nickel gains faded (1.2% down to 0.3%). Nickel remains oversupplied and aluminium outlook weakens with China demand concerns. | The US announced Russia sanctions affecting over 500 entities but excluding the base metals industry. Aluminium and nickel had initially risen on expectations sanctions would restrict supplies. After the announcement, aluminium fell 1.1% as gains were unwound, while nickel dropped toward 0.3%, with nickel described as oversupplied. | Lifezone Metals reported progress on its two-phased Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania, including definitive feasibility study work, high locked-cycle nickel recovery to concentrate (with strong copper recoveries) and completion of key process testwork. It also received the environmental impact assessment certificate for the Kahama hydromet refinery, supporting potential future nickel/copper supply. | Kinterra’s portfolio company Cannon Resources expanded the nickel sulphide resource at its Fisher East project, increasing JORC-indicated/inferred resources 75% and doubling the sulphide resource in under six months. The project targets IRA-compliant nickel for EV batteries, with further metallurgical work and permitting planned.

news_summary: Andrew Forrest and Tesla-backed efforts seek temporary Australian production tax credits to rescue the struggling nickel industry. The proposal, modelled via AMEC and assessed by economist Chris Richardson, would involve about a 10% nickel production tax credit, far exceeding prior Labor support levels for car makers. | Copper prices edged down as a firm U.S. dollar pressured greenback-priced commodities and Chinese exchange warehouses saw inventory spikes. SHFE copper and LME copper both declined, with SHFE inventories more than doubling in two weeks. Aluminium and nickel also fell on the day, reflecting softer near-term sentiment. | Indonesia’s government delayed issuance of nickel ore production quotas (RKAB), slowing mining and tightening ore availability. Smelters curtailed output due to thin stocks, while Indonesian nickel ore prices rose about 14% in February and nickel pig iron hit a near three-month peak, boosting near-term nickel price expectations. | Copper prices resumed falling on a stronger U.S. dollar and worsening China property-sector news, with a liquidation petition against Country Garden hurting demand expectations. LME three-month copper slipped and SHFE inventories rose, indicating weak post–Lunar New Year consumption. Aluminium and nickel also fell on the same day’s risk backdrop. | Treasurer Jim Chalmers will push G20 incentives for environmentally sustainable critical minerals, citing concerns over Australia’s nickel industry amid rising Indonesian competition. He argues current market structures don’t reward improved ESG output and signals possible differentiated trading or carbon-tariff approaches that could support higher-standards nickel producers. | On Feb 28, SMM8–12% high-nickel pig iron averaged 960 yuan/nickel point, up 10 yuan from the prior session. The rise is attributed to Indonesia’s RKAB policy supporting nickel ore prices, lifting ferronickel costs, and expectations of production cuts, despite weaker stainless demand.

news_summary: France agreed to convert €320m of prior loans into debt “quasi-equity” for Eramet’s SLN and plans further aid tied to reforms to lower nickel production costs. Koniambo is in care-and-maintenance and Prony gets a €140m loan, alongside an emergency jobs plan—reducing near-term supply risk for nickel. | Singapore’s MAS charged former Envysion Wealth/Hui Xun Asset Management executives in connection with a cited billion-dollar nickel investment fraud case involving alleged false promises of nickel purchases. The allegations center on investor money raised for nickel trading/returns, but no concrete data is provided on real nickel flows affecting near-term prices. | Global nickel producers including BHP and Andrew Forrest are urging the LME to introduce a green price premium and formally distinguish “dirty” from sustainably produced (“green”) nickel. Indonesia’s low-cost supply has driven a slump, while PT CNGR’s bid for a ‘good delivery brand’ could reshape nickel pricing via ESG-linked differentials on the LME. | Granite Creek Copper announced a non-brokered private placement raising about $1.5 million to fund general corporate purposes and advancement of strategic initiatives. The company focuses on copper projects in Yukon and also holds a Star copper-nickel-PGM project. The financing is not tied to near-term production changes or supply disruption. | France will convert and provide financing tied to a March “nickel pact” to rescue New Caledonia’s struggling nickel producers, including Eramet’s SLN and loans for Prony. Koniambo production has stopped, but aid and cost-reduction reforms aim to prevent further disruption amid tough Indonesian/Chinese competition.

news_summary: Canada says Western countries may consider tariffs on nickel from Indonesia and China over concerns about market manipulation. Indonesia’s share of nickel supply has surged, contributing to a major global oversupply and an 80% price collapse. Producers face mine closures and surplus pressure, implying near-term bearish sentiment for nickel. | The Pentagon discussed with Australia’s resources minister options to co-invest with the Australian government in an Australian nickel project after a collapse in nickel prices. The decline has triggered closures and write-downs of projects, reflecting a glut. Potential funding support could ease downside sentiment for nickel. | The LME’s rejection of a separate index for “green premium” nickel is described as a setback for Australian producers such as BHP and Wyloo Metals. Without a differentiated pricing mechanism, their ability to earn higher premiums over Indonesian nickel suppliers is weakened, reinforcing broader bearish sentiment after nickel’s price collapse. | Experts argue Indonesia may need to adjust its nickel downstream strategy as EV makers increasingly consider lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries, which use iron cathodes rather than nickel. The shift is linked to nickel price fluctuations, with forecasts of LFP gaining market share by 2025. | Experts say Indonesia may need to adjust its nickel downstream strategy as EV makers consider alternatives. The report cites expectations that momentum will build behind lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries rather than nickel-based chemistries, potentially weakening nickel demand outlook and weighing on prices. | Australia’s Resources Minister met US Defence officials to discuss co-investing in an Australian nickel project amid weakening nickel prices that have forced some operations into reduced output or care and maintenance. The talks aim to preserve the nickel supply chain, alongside prior discussions on investment incentives and infrastructure support. | SMM reports China’s February refined nickel production at 24,600 tonnes, down ~3% from January due to fewer Spring Festival working days but up 42.2% year-on-year. March output is forecast to rise to ~25,000 tonnes, though higher raw-material prices may constrain some outsourced production. | EV Nickel upsized its previously announced private placement of flow-through shares to fund Canadian exploration expenses for its Shaw Dome nickel project in Ontario. A strategic investor increased participation to maintain its pro-rata stake. The announcement supports sentiment toward the company’s nickel resource pipeline but not immediate supply volumes.

news_summary: Aluminium prices rose toward six-week highs as traders priced in seasonally stronger demand, with LME three-month aluminium around $2,270/t. However, China’s ample supply and rising inventories could limit upside. A firmer U.S. dollar after inflation data pressured industrial metals, though nickel was slightly higher. | Spot copper concentrate TC/RCs in cif Asia-Pacific plunged to $12.70/t in early March from $19.80/t a week earlier, the lowest since tracking began. Sources link the decline to unexpected mine supply disruptions (e.g., Cobre Panama) and persistent concentrate deficits, squeezing smelter feedstock economics and raising risks of cuts. | Indonesia’s tech and fintech M&A is highlighted, but the piece also notes active deals in critical minerals. Antam sold stakes to CATL and Australia’s Nickel Industries bought into an Indonesian nickel project, reinforcing ongoing investor focus on Indonesia’s nickel supply for EV battery demand.

news_summary: Stantec was selected to provide environmental consulting for Canada Nickel’s Crawford Nickel Project in Ontario, including preparing the Impact Statement and related studies under Canada’s Impact Assessment Act. The mine is expected to produce about 50,000 tons of nickel annually, but the news is about approvals, not immediate supply changes. | Australia’s Avebury Nickel Mine near Zeehan is preparing to re-enter care and maintenance, with many workers told they will not return. The shutdown is attributed to a 50% decline in global nickel prices, blamed on cheap low-quality Indonesian imports, reinforcing bearish sentiment for near-term nickel. | LME nickel has surged from below $16,000/t to above $18,000/t as traders increased buying to cover large short positions. The move was supported by higher Chinese nickel sulphate prices, delays to Indonesian ore licences, and class 2 supply cuts, though class 1 oversupply keeps downside risk.

news_summary: Indonesia’s mining ministry has issued nickel ore production quotas totaling 152.62 million metric tons so far this year, alongside tin quotas. The government has approved production for 191 miners and is still processing more than 500 proposals, indicating ongoing supply management in a key nickel-producing country. | Copper fell on the LME as investors took profits after a rally and the dollar strengthened ahead of the Fed decision. The article cites weaker near-term China buying intentions. It also notes losses in nickel and aluminium, reflecting broader base-metal sentiment plus easing supply disruption signals from Indonesia. | Shanghai nickel fell during the March 19 session, with declining volume and open interest suggesting longer positions were closing. The key catalyst was Indonesia’s completion of nickel ore quota approvals for 107 companies, easing expectations of tight ore supply, plus signs intermediate production tightness may ease in April. Nickel is expected to stay weak. | Canadian North Resources reported a 172% increase in NI 43-101 indicated mineral resources at its Ferguson Lake project, including copper (66.1 Mt, 1,093 Mlb) and nickel (678 Mlb), with cobalt and PGM values also stated. It signals exploration potential, but provides no immediate production or supply disruption for prices. | BHP cut roughly a quarter of contractor staff at its West Musgrave nickel and copper project in Western Australia, citing the impacts of a global nickel price crisis. The company is also reviewing capital spend and business plans after shutting Nickel West, amid losses tied to nickel oversupply and a weaker outlook. | Shanghai Non ferrous Metals reports Ni 1.5% ore prices rebounded to about $46/wmt CIF, up $0.6. The rise is linked to Surigao’s rainy season reducing Philippines-to-China exports, lowering spot transactions, and coinciding with lower Chinese port inventories and weaker weekly arrivals. | BHP has reduced contractors and deferred a 1000-bed camp plan linked to rebuilding infrastructure at its Kalgoorlie nickel smelter, with ageing assets at risk of being mothballed. The company seeks to clarify the outlook for its loss-making nickel division, creating near-term uncertainty for nickel supply.

news_summary: The Financial Review brief reports that BHP is cutting contractors at its Kalgoorlie nickel smelter. Alongside other unrelated items, this personnel change is the only element directly tied to industrial metals, potentially indicating disrupted or reduced nickel production capacity in the near term. | Shanghai nickel prices fell about 2% as fears of tight supply from Indonesia eased. A mining ministry official said Indonesia is accelerating the approval process for issuing mining production quotas, with progress already reported on nickel ore. Tin also declined, while LME nickel showed a smaller rebound. | Susan McDonald urges adding copper to Australia’s critical minerals list to protect supply chains and unlock financing and international investment. She cites nickel disruption after delays to show potential job risks. The piece notes copper prices rising on mine/smelter supply concerns, while processing fees have fallen. | SMM analysis says nickel prices rose in February, supported by a weaker US dollar, favorable domestic policies, and tighter supply from slower Indonesian ore quota approvals. For March, sentiment on ore quotas is easing while demand recovers. However, rising inventories are expected to pressure nickel prices in late March. | Talon Metals reported drill results from hole 23TK0447 at its Tamarack project, outside the current resource area, including 12.7m grading 6.04% Ni and 2.68% Cu (7.5% NiEq) and additional disseminated sulphides. Wet intermediate supply shrank due to slower Indonesia ramp-ups and scrap availability remained weak, while downstream inventory drawdowns intensified the mismatch. Prices rose faster than raw materials and profits recovered, but further upside may be limited. | Indonesia, the dominant player in nickel supply, plans to keep LME nickel prices below $18,000/mt. The government moved quickly to address quota-approval delays that had raised deficit fears. Still, the stated pricing cap and low-cost production could pressure nickel toward lower levels despite a recent rebound.

news_summary: China’s February nickel pig iron imports fell MoM, with Indonesia-linked volumes down 14–17% MoM, attributed to slow Indonesian nickel mine RKAB approvals and the Chinese New Year holiday. Despite YOY growth, the delay suggests near-term tighter nickel supply, while recovery is expected as approvals accelerate. | Yle reports that Russian nickel continues to move by rail into Finland and flows to the Nornickel Harjavalta smelter despite checkpoint closures, because nickel is not covered by EU sanctions. The report also notes that freight strikes have not halted these shipments, suggesting steady near-term nickel supply to the EU. | Indonesia’s government approved 152.62mn tonnes of nickel mining quotas for 2024 (RKAB). Industry sources cited limitations and said approved ore quotas appear far below 2024 demand and may not be fully mined. Given nickel ore demand projections and constraints on ore supply to smelters, near-term nickel availability looks tighter. | BHP reduced contractor headcount at its Kalgoorlie nickel smelter and halted a new precipitation system, citing the current rout in nickel prices and an industry surplus—especially from Indonesia and China. The firm is also assessing whether to deactivate nickel operations if prices do not improve. | Lifezone Metals says Tanzania issued a licence for its Kahama multi-metal processing facility, allowing refining of nickel and copper (and cobalt) from the Kabanga Nickel Project using hydromet technology. The refinery is planned within a Special Economic Zone with tax benefits, supporting improved in-country beneficiation prospects. | The article argues the nickel market is facing continued pressure as Indonesia’s nickel laterite mines increase output and add new supply. This supply influx is described as flooding the market, implying weaker near-term balance and downward price pressure for nickel.

news_summary: EV Minerals reported historic drill targets and selected intercepts from its EV Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Quebec, including multi-metre grades of nickel and copper and plans for follow-up drilling after assay results. The update may modestly support market sentiment for nickel and copper supply potential, but it is exploratory with no immediate production impact. | EV Minerals reports compelling North Zone drill targets and historic assay intersections at its EV Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Quebec. Multiple intervals include >1% nickel and meaningful copper grades, supporting potential resource expansion via infill drilling. The company plans follow-up work and metallurgical evaluation, but no near-term production change is stated. | France set a March-end deadline to finalize an agreement to restore New Caledonia’s nickel industry, including €200m/year in subsidies for energy and investment in electricity production. The plan targets cost reductions versus Indonesian competition and seeks more European supply, easing bankruptcy/supply disruption risks. | Refined nickel imports stay depressed as Russian nickel inflows decline, with difficulties signing long-term Russian contracts. Indonesia’s supplies rise, but refined nickel exports fell in February due to Spring Festival logistics disruptions and increased nickel sulfate prices, which led some producers to prioritize sulfate output over refined nickel. | Archer Exploration reported winter 2024 drilling results from its Grasset nickel sulphide project in Quebec, including intersections of high nickel (e.g., 2.67% Ni) with some copper content. The company frames results as extending mineralization at depth but provides no production, shipment, or inventory changes. | Tanzania awarded Lifezone Metals licences to operate a multi-metal hydromet facility at its Kabanga project, including production of nickel and copper (and cobalt). The Kahama Refinery licence follows SEZ and mining licence steps, leveraging existing Buzwagi infrastructure and aiming for local production with lower transport and operating costs.

news_summary: SMM’s morning nickel comment reports SHFE nickel weakening and expects continued mild decline. It attributes softer raw-material tightness to faster nickel-ore approvals and ramping Indonesian intermediate output, while downstream spot buying recovered only because prices fell sharply. Overall, it forecasts weak nickel price fluctuations. | The article argues nickel prices are likely to drop further in 2024. It cites forecasts of a continued global surplus supported by higher Indonesian production and expanding China refining capacity, which previously drove nickel to multi-year lows. Import clearances declined.

news_summary: Morgan Stanley’s commodity update highlights a strengthening bullish narrative for copper, expecting supply disruptions to drive a large deficit that persists into 2025, supported by AI/data-centre demand. It also prefers nickel for near-term upside based on rapid supply-side response to low prices and ongoing supply tightness. | The article examines how Indonesia can make its nickel downstreaming and mining more environmentally and economically sustainable. It highlights that export-ban disruption and oversupply have pressured nickel prices, and discusses potential measures like limiting smelter expansion, improving data and emissions controls, and using incentives more effectively. | London-traded base metals rose on April 4, led by copper hitting the highest level since January 2023. Gains were linked to a softer dollar, expectations of lower U.S. rates after Powell’s remarks, stronger-than-expected Chinese manufacturing data, and planned Chinese smelter output cuts amid raw-material shortages. | The article reviews Indonesia’s nickel downstreaming push—aiming for many new smelters—and ongoing controversies including oversupply-driven nickel price declines and the 2021 export-ban dispute. It suggests limiting expansion, reassessing incentives, and improving sustainability. Overall, the tone implies continued supply pressure and weak near-term nickel pricing. | Indonesian coal producer Harum Energy bought a 51% stake in Blue Sparking Energy, which operates nickel processing/refining and an HPAL project in North Maluku. Harum also previously increased its stake in nickel smelter Westron Metal. The moves reflect ongoing diversification into nickel production capacity. | Prospector Metals updated its Devon nickel project in Ontario, contracting a VTEM airborne survey and citing recent field sample results with up to 0.53% Ni and 1.125% Cu (plus PGE/Co trace). The company is also progressing other projects, but no production or concrete supply impacts are described. | Mosaic Minerals reported remaining drill results from its Gaboury project, confirming nickel potential over more than 4.5 km and identifying Ni-Cu magmatic mineralization potential. The geology also shows zinc, cobalt, copper and gold. The company plans further drilling and surveys, but no immediate production or supply-market change is provided. | Mosaic announced drilling results at its Gaboury Nickel project in Quebec, estimating intersection thickness at ~70% of the core interval and concluding the property has potential for magmatic Ni-Cu mineralization. Management plans more intensive drilling and surveys across magnetic anomalies, supporting expectations for additional nickel-bearing zones.

news_summary: Japan, the US, and the Philippines plan to cooperate to strengthen the nickel supply chain, with a joint statement expected after the April 11 Washington summit. The Philippines is cited as a leading nickel producer, while China’s dominance in processing/refining is a key concern. EV and fuel-cell demand is expected to keep rising.

news_summary: SHFE nickel rose for five straight days to around 133,450 yuan/mt by Apr 3. Slow Indonesian nickel ore approvals have left little new quota for two weeks, keeping ore premiums high and supply tight. Markets also watch potential effects from US/Japan/Philippines supply cooperation and macro expectations for June Fed cuts. | London copper slipped 0.6% as investors weighed uncertainty over fewer-than-expected U.S. Fed rate cuts, after strong jobs data reduced near-term cut expectations. In contrast, Shanghai copper rose 2.1% to a record high. LME aluminium and nickel fell, while SHFE aluminium and nickel increased. | Chalice Mining is seeking environmental approval to develop a mine based on Tim Goyder’s well-known nickel-copper discovery. If approved, it would reduce permitting uncertainty and improve expectations for future nickel and copper supply, which can provide a mild supportive effect on prices in the coming weeks. | SHFE nickel strengthened for five straight sessions, reaching 133,450 yuan/mt by April 3. The key driver is tighter Indonesian nickel ore supply as quota approvals are slower than expected, keeping ore premiums elevated. Market also weighs US/Japan/Philippines nickel cooperation after April 11 and Fed rate-cut timing. | Core Nickel announced it completed a 2024 winter drill program at its Halfway Lake nickel project in Manitoba, reporting visual sulfide mineralization in all three drillholes and expanded testing of the W62 Zone framework. Assay results are still pending, and no production, supply, or market structure changes were stated. | SMM reports China’s nickel pig iron output fell 2.82% m/m in March, while ferronickel output declined due to slower Indonesia RKAB approvals, rainy-season disruptions in the Philippines, tighter nickel ore supply, and ongoing profit inversion leading some plants to stop. April output is expected to keep declining, supporting nickel demand/supply balance. | Australian miner Andrew Forrest urges China and other buyers to demand higher environmental standards for nickel supply from Indonesia, after Indonesian processing faced criticism. The push aligns with LME changes to report low-carbon nickel trading and follows prior nickel price declines tied to heavy Indonesian supply. | Nickel prices reversed after peaking around March 13, falling about 5.56% month over month. Stainless market sentiment softened as nickel retraced and distributors described Q1 as slow. LME nickel inventories ballooned to the highest since May 2022, reinforcing a bearish near-term outlook amid still-oversupplied conditions.

news_summary: China’s refined nickel output was 24,900 mt in March, slightly higher MoM and up strongly YoY, but down versus January due to maintenance shutdowns. SMM forecasts April refined nickel production around 25,900 mt, about +4% MoM, as suspended plants restart and new capacity ramps up. | China’s nickel sulphate output rose sharply in March on resumed wet-process recycling and demand recovery, keeping supply comparatively tight. With NCM chain destocking easing and EV auto sales improving, smelters raised operating rates. April output is expected to increase but still face demand support, sustaining higher nickel sulphate prices. | Aluminium prices surged to a 14-month peak as hedge funds bought the sector on optimism for the global economy. Better-than-expected manufacturing expansion in China and the U.S. boosted demand expectations. LME copper neared a 14-month high, while nickel also gained, reflecting broad bullish fund flows. | SMM reports the average price of SMM8–12% high-nickel pig iron rose slightly to 931.5 yuan per nickel point. High nickel ore costs kept Indonesian ferronickel output relatively steady, while some China producers paused. Post-holiday sentiment improved with higher stainless steel disk prices. | Fastmarkets reports sharp month-on-month swings in Shanghai bonded stocks: aluminium jumped over 150% as sellers held back expecting better future arbitrage terms; nickel bonded inventories fell due to exports; copper bonded inventories rose as prolonged arbitrage losses reduced imports and reflected smelter routings/maintenance-driven delivery changes. | Nickel premiums in Europe (briquettes, uncut cathode and 4×4) were unchanged week-on-week, supported by some Indonesian material inflows but with quiet trading. In China, nickel full plate premiums were also steady as import arbitrage losses widened and overseas demand remained sluggish, keeping prices range-bound.

Support came from supply-side disruptions and improving China manufacturing. Aluminium edged up slightly; nickel and tin fell on the session. | Canada Nickel says it has begun FEED for the Crawford nickel sulphide project, with engineering led by Ausenco. Management expects FEED completion before a mid-2025 construction decision and first production by year-end 2027. The update supports improving nickel project development outlook and potential supply expectations. | Flying Nickel announced an updated Mineral Resource Estimate for its Minago nickel-PGM project, reporting a 41.95% increase in in-pit measured & indicated nickel and an inaugural platinum and palladium resource. The estimate uses nickel/Pt/Pd price assumptions for pit optimization but gives no near-term production or inventory changes. | Canada Nickel says it has started front-end engineering design for its Crawford nickel sulphide project, led by Ausenco, while continuing financing and permitting. news_summary: China’s primary aluminium output rose 7.4% in March, reaching 3.59 million tonnes, as rising aluminium prices and stronger demand improved industry profits. Contracts on SHFE and LME increased in March, with supply uncertainty in Yunnan adding support. Copper and nickel output also hit records. | The overnight report links higher base metal prices to new US/UK sanctions on Russian imports of aluminium, copper and nickel. It describes a risk-off tape for equities and safe havens, but notes base metals rose in response to the sanctions, supporting related miners’ share performance. | Fathom Nickel reports results from its Q1 drilling at the Albert Lake project, highlighting drillhole AL24077 with 0.42% Ni over 2.01 m and ~10% nickel tenor in ultramafic host rock. BHEM and geochemical anomalies suggest further potential along strike, awaiting additional drilling and assay updates. | The US and UK announced sanctions blocking Russian aluminium from LME/CME delivery and banning imports, with similar restrictions for Russian copper and nickel. Aluminium prices jumped immediately, as non-Russian deliverable supply was expected to tighten. RUSAL said output continues, but deliverable warrants are dominated by Russia. | Washington and London prohibited metal exchanges from accepting new Russian-produced aluminium, copper, and nickel and barred imports into the U.S. and Britain. The change removes delivery of Russian Class 1 nickel from Global Commodities Exchange, following recent sanctions, tightening supply and supporting higher prices for these metals. | Fathom Nickel reported Q1 drill results from its Albert Lake Project in Saskatchewan, including 0.42% Ni over 2.01 meters and a very high nickel tenor (~10%) in Rottenstone-like ultramafic rock. The company said assays for Gochager Lake are due next month, supporting bullish nickel sentiment. | The podcast with Nickel 28’s CEO focuses on nickel market developments, arguing that miners are struggling because Indonesia—holding about 70% of global supply—has created oversupply and keeps prices under pressure. It also touches on shifting demand narratives for nickel amid EVs, AI, and the energy grid.

Copper’s average realized price fell 19% year on year and nickel’s fell 33%, despite higher volumes of copper and iron ore. Vale’s net profit and adjusted EBITDA both missed analyst expectations. | Vale reported weaker Q1 earnings, attributing the decline to lower iron ore, nickel and copper prices versus the prior year. The implication is that buyers’ supply channels may shift away from Russia and toward alternative origins, potentially increasing available nickel supply and limiting upside pressure on prices in the near term. | BHP’s $39-billion bid for Anglo American to create the world’s biggest copper producer is likely to boost near-term copper price sentiment, given Anglo’s ~760,000 tonnes/year of copper output and the prospect of supply consolidation. news_summary: Copper prices edged higher, nearing the $10,000/ton resistance, supported by investment flows into funds. LME three-month copper was up about 1%, and SHFE June copper gained about 1.5%. Aluminium and nickel also rose on both LME and SHFE, reflecting broad base-metals sentiment. | Copper prices hit a two-year high, breaking above $10,000/mt on Friday as index funds and ETFs attracted retail money and momentum-based buying overcame resistance. LME copper rose ~1.1% and Shanghai June copper ~1.7%. Aluminium and nickel also edged higher, reflecting supportive broad metal sentiment. | BHP made a $60 billion bid to acquire Anglo American, arguing it would combine copper (and greater nickel exposure) assets to become a top copper producer. Anglo American rejected the offer, saying it undervalued prospects and created demerger and execution risks, likely limiting any immediate market expectations for copper supply changes. | BHP’s $39bn bid for Anglo American and potential mining consolidation put copper in focus, with the companies together controlling about 10% of global copper. The expert links demand growth to electrification and renewables, and argues consolidation could improve miners’ pricing power, supporting copper bullish expectations. | SMM reports stable high-nickel pig iron prices at 956 yuan/nickel point as Indonesia’s Ramadan/Eid disruptions and suspended RKAB approvals keep ferronickel raw materials tight. Production ramp-up is slow and some smelters remain shut. Demand improves near May, with stainless mills replenishing inventories, supporting short-term stability and strength. | SMM reports the average spot price of 8–12% high-nickel pig iron rose slightly and remains supported. Indonesia faces holiday disruptions and slower approvals, smelter raw-material availability stays tight, and production ramp-up is slow, while domestic inventories are limited. Demand for stainless-steel raw material reserves improves, supporting stable, strong prices short term. | SMM reports the April 23 average price of 8–12% high-nickel pig iron rose to 956 yuan/nickel point (+4.5 yuan). Supply is constrained by Indonesia holiday effects, stalled RKAB approvals, tight smelter raw materials, and slow production ramp-up. Demand improves as stainless steel reserve buying lifts transactions, supporting short-term strength.

news_summary: Stellantis is reported to be in talks with Vale and Huayou Cobalt about investing in an Indonesian nickel smelter/HPAL facility to produce battery-grade nickel. The discussions aim to secure supply for Stellantis’ EV expansion, potentially affecting near-term expectations for nickel availability and demand. | Anglo American’s strategy update said it is exploring options to offload its steelmaking coal, nickel, and platinum businesses to become a simpler organisation. The move follows the prior rejection of BHP’s raised takeover offer. The report does not specify timing or production impacts, making near-term nickel effects uncertain. | Anglo American said it is reviewing options that could include demerging or selling its steelmaking coal, nickel, diamonds and platinum units amid a hostile takeover bid from BHP. It will explore options for its nickel mines, but the announcement does not indicate immediate changes to nickel production. | Copper prices hit their highest level since April 2022, rising on base-metal sector fund inflows tied to expectations of U.S. rate cuts, supply disruptions, and green-energy demand. Despite weaker-than-expected China Q2 consumption due to high prices, LME copper and SHFE copper still advanced, while aluminium rose slightly and nickel eased. | Anglo American announced a strategic review that could include selling or demerging parts of its nickel business as it fends off BHP’s takeover bid. It plans to explore options for its nickel mines, but provides no clear, immediate production changes, reducing direct short-term price pressure on nickel. | Copper prices hit their highest since April 2022 on the back of technical signals drawing fresh fund buying, despite reports of weak physical demand in China. LME three-month copper rose modestly. Aluminium edged up despite higher warehouse inventories, while nickel slipped slightly. | Indonesia’s outgoing government added five state-supported industrial parks for nickel processing, including HPAL plants targeting 120,000 tons of nickel in MHP per year, alongside plans for nickel and cobalt sulphate for EV batteries. The incoming administration pledged to continue the downstream policy, boosting nickel-related investment expectations. | SMM reports May 14 prices for 8–12% high-nickel pig iron around 968.5 yuan per nickel point, up slightly. Indonesia’s nickel ore approval and smelter raw-material supply remain constrained, while domestic smelter restarts are limited by costs. With tight inventory and cost support, ferronickel is expected to stay stable and strong short term, despite softer stainless demand. | SMM reported battery-grade nickel sulfate prices edging up on May 14, with spot still tight but downstream procurement activity weakening. Supply-demand conditions are described as weak and market activity low. Higher post-holiday nickel prices pushed nickel salt raw material costs up, keeping salt mill pricing sentiment supported despite loss thresholds. | Anglo American announced a major restructuring, exiting diamonds, platinum and coking coal and aiming to become a copper-focused company after pressure from shareholders and a BHP takeover bid. It also plans to sell or shutter its relatively small nickel business in Brazil. Investor sentiment shifts toward copper prominence as shares fell.

news_summary: Copper prices eased from a record high as a firmer U.S. dollar weighed on LME pricing and traders paused after a strong rally. The report points to weaker Chinese import premiums and higher SHFE stocks. LME aluminium and nickel also fell, indicating broader metal softness despite copper’s earlier momentum. | Indonesia’s investment minister says the government proposed to Tesla’s Elon Musk building an EV battery and cathode-precursor factory in Indonesia. The proposal is framed around Indonesia’s large nickel reserves and its ambition to raise nickel supply share. The potential downstream battery investment supports near-term nickel demand expectations. | Copper prices edged higher near record levels, but gains were tempered by subdued physical demand. Chinese import premiums fell to zero and SHFE inventories remained elevated, suggesting fundamentals lag speculative momentum. The piece also notes small moves in aluminium and nickel, indicating mixed broad metal sentiment rather than a clear driver. | Core Nickel reports 2024 winter drill results from its Halfway Lake project, finding wide packages of nickel-bearing sulfide mineralization in ultramafic rocks, with nickel tenors up to ~17% in assays. The company plans additional VTEM and aeromagnetic work to refine targets for potential economic nickel deposits. | Nickel Pig Iron Daily Review reports SMM 8–12% high nickel pig iron averaging 977.5 yuan/nickel point, up on the day. Indonesia’s nickel ore approvals lag, raw material shortages persist, and smelter output remains weak with low operating rates. Demand from steel mills may rise near the procurement cycle, supporting near-term stability. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate prices rising to around 32,004 yuan/ton. Limited nickel-salt supply, low finished-product inventories, and high raw-material coefficients deepen profit inversion at salt plants, pushing them to raise prices strongly. Downstream demand is weaker but forced to accept higher prices due to precursor constraints and replenishment needs. | The report says Indonesian nickel ore premiums were stable and Philippine laterite ore transactions stayed active, with freight to China still high. It notes China’s nickel ore port inventories rose 210,000 wmt WoW, suggesting more supply entering ports, while NPI smelters showed little added buying interest, keeping prices steady. | SMM data show refined nickel inventories in China’s bonded zone fell to 4,160 mt last week, and the publication expects further declines. It attributes the tighter bonded supply to riots in overseas nickel raw material areas and rising nickel prices, while domestic social stocks rose slightly on weak downstream demand. | Shanghai Non ferrous Metals reports nickel sulphate prices rebounded to 31,000–31,500 yuan/mt. MHP supply remains tight due to downstream smelter losses and continued overseas hydrometallurgy project shutdown concerns, keeping the MHP discount elevated. Weaker orders and inventory pressure cap gains, but cost support should keep prices 31,500–32,000 yuan/mt. | Copper has surged, but the article warns the rally may be vulnerable. It cites heavy long speculative positioning, negative Chinese demand signals (Yangshan premium), strong contango, and higher-for-longer rates supporting the USD. It also mentions Cobré Panama closure/resumption uncertainty affecting supply tightness and possible downside correction risk. | Copper prices eased from Monday’s record high as investors questioned whether the rally—supported by speculative short covering—can be sustained amid subdued physical demand. China demand softness and refinery output rising also weighed. The piece also flags aluminium up on lower LME warehouse stocks and nickel down on the day. | DMCI Holdings says it is exploring new copper (and gold) mining assets and possibly an additional open-pit coal mine. It also notes nickel projects are currently not operational due to pending permits, with some expected by year-end. DMCI recently agreed to acquire Cemex Philippines.

news_summary: Copper prices trended lower as a steady U.S. dollar weighed on demand and China’s physical market weakened after record highs. Higher SHFE warehouse inventories reinforced bearish sentiment. LME aluminium and nickel also edged down, reflecting broader risk-off moves in industrial metals as markets awaited Fed minutes. | New Caledonia’s second week of deadly riots has included road blockades and an airport shutdown, prompting a 12-day French state of emergency. Because New Caledonia is a major nickel producer, the disruptions raise short-term supply risk and can support higher nickel prices over coming days. | Resources Minister Madeleine King warned that BHP’s underinvestment in ageing infrastructure could force thousands of workers into hardship, with BHP flagging it may close its Western Australian nickel operations. She linked the situation to Alcoa’s recent alumina refinery shutdown at Kwinana, citing similar consequences for supply. | Violent unrest in French Pacific New Caledonia—home to 20–30% of global nickel reserves—has raised concerns of shipping and production disruptions. Nickel prices on the LME jumped sharply, with the article citing supply-risk fears, sanctions on Russian nickel, and broader critical-material shortage outlooks. | Prospect Ridge Resources announced new high-grade copper-bearing quartz vein showings on its Holy Grail property near the Copper Ridge zone, including samples up to ~14.8% Cu. The company plans to initiate drill holes on Copper Ridge in summer 2024. The release is exploration-focused and does not affect near-term supply materially. | Rising nickel futures have pushed Chinese battery and precursor makers to increase electrolytic nickel output, including potential additional capacity in May. EV sales growth has slowed, but producers are shifting from nickel sulfate to refined nickel as arbitrage widens. Nickel prices have been trending higher, near recent highs. | Alaska Energy Metals secured an option to acquire the Bambino nickel–copper property in western Quebec and has begun a fully funded exploration program, including soil sampling and a VTEM survey. The geology targets magmatic sulfide Ni+Cu±PGE mineralization, which may support bullish sentiment for nickel and copper. | Alaska Energy Metals secured an option to acquire the Bambino nickel-copper-PGE property in western Quebec and will begin exploration with soil sampling and a VTEM survey. The geology targets magmatic sulfide Ni+Cu±PGE hosted in gabbro sills. The announcement is exploration-stage, implying limited immediate supply effects. | Riots in New Caledonia, a major nickel producer, have halted mining and damaged SLN infrastructure, forcing mines to idle and the processing plant to run at minimum capacity. The disruption supports higher LME nickel prices by reducing output and potentially eroding a global surplus, despite some offset from growing Indonesian supply. | New Caledonia produces about 7% of global nickel, but the article cites near-term disruption risks: metallurgical plants teetering on collapse, Glencore stopping financing for a loss-making mine, and Goro reducing output after a tailings dam leak. With a global nickel glut already weighing on prices, disruptions add bearish sentiment. | Class 1 Nickel announced an updated mineral resource estimate for the Alexo North nickel sulphide deposit, with indicated resources rising to 42,600 tonnes at 0.92% Ni and a higher contained nickel figure versus the 2020 estimate. The company plans further drilling to upgrade and expand resources across the project. | Class 1 Nickel announced an updated NI 43-101 mineral resource estimate for the Alexo North nickel sulphide deposit, combining new and historical drilling data. The update significantly raises indicated resources versus the 2020 estimate and supports continued exploration at the Alexo-Dundonald Nickel Project. This is generally supportive for nickel sentiment. | Civil unrest in New Caledonia has halted mining and damaged/curbed output at Societe Le Nickel’s operations, while Koniambo idled its mine and processing plant. The disruption supported a rally in London Metal Exchange nickel prices to nine-month highs as analysts warned reduced supply could erode a global surplus. | SMM reports nickel pig iron prices (SMM8–12% high-nickel) holding steady but under pressure from heavier upstream sentiment. Limited Indonesian ore approvals and persistent raw-material shortages keep smelter output weak and operating rates low. Demand from steel mills may rise near the procurement cycle, supporting stable-to-strong ferronickel in the short term. | Nickel prices continued to ease in the morning, with the spot market difficult to trade. Jinchuan premium was 600-800 yuan/ton (avg 700), while the Russian nickel premium ranged -600 to -300 yuan/ton (avg -450). Downstream demand did not rise materially, keeping sentiment cautious despite recent-high levels. | Glencore and Rusal extended their long-term aluminium supply contract into 2025 despite previously low traded volumes. The extension is enabled because Rusal is not directly targeted by Western sanctions. The report also notes an LME ban on Russian aluminium, copper and nickel from April 13, reinforcing near-term supply-tight sentiment. | Deadly unrest in New Caledonia has raised concerns about nickel supply disruptions from a region holding 20–30% of global reserves. LME nickel prices jumped from about $18,510/t in early May to above $21,275/t, with broader worries of future shortages for battery and industrial materials. | Nickel prices surged after political riots in French-ruled New Caledonia, where the country is a top global nickel producer. The unrest led to deaths, airport closures, curfews, and troop reinforcements, raising fears of supply and logistics disruption. Nickel jumped about 5% then added further gains to a multi-month high.

news_summary: Violent unrest in France’s Pacific territory of New Caledonia has followed constitutional voting changes and a 12-day state of emergency. New Caledonia supplies roughly 20–30% of world nickel reserves and most local exports, raising fears of disrupted production. The article says global nickel prices have surged. | Copper prices fell after hawkish Fed minutes supported a firmer U.S. dollar, making dollar-priced commodities more expensive. The drop was reinforced by weakening Chinese demand as earlier price gains reduced end-user consumption and lifted inventories. LME and SHFE aluminium and nickel also declined, reflecting broad risk-off pressure. | Riots in New Caledonia have halted nickel mining operations and damaged/limited processing at SLN, reducing output to minimum levels. As New Caledonia is a major nickel supplier, analysts say disrupted production could erode a global surplus and supported a rally in LME nickel prices, though prices later eased. | Nornickel’s board recommends not paying dividends for FY2023, citing sanctions and geopolitical challenges that hit revenue and EBITDA and reduced free cash flow. The company already paid interim dividends in 9M 2023 and says it will not increase debt for dividends. Nornickel is a major nickel and copper producer. | Copper on the LME fell for a third straight session due to profit-taking and consumers shunning near-record prices. It notes elevated China inventories and a refined market surplus in March. The piece also shows aluminium and nickel easing on the session, alongside caution ahead of U.S. jobs data. | Indonesia approved 204 nickel RKAB work plans totaling 220.7mn t, but participants expect production approvals may not meet 2024 ore demand of about 220mn t. Supply concerns include lower approval rates outside Morowali and New Caledonia unrest disrupting nickel value-chain operations and transportation. Nickel prices have risen on tighter availability. | May 23 SMM battery-grade nickel sulfate averaged higher (index 32,389 yuan/ton). The report attributes the rebound to limited nickel-salt supply, low finished-product inventories, and higher raw-material costs from elevated nickel prices. Downstream buying continued despite weaker demand due to replenishment needs and warehouse-building constraints. | SMM’s May 23 nickel spot review says prices fell sharply: nickel beans dropped to 149,000–149,800 yuan/ton (down 5,050 yuan/ton). Nickel sulfate traded about 2,582 yuan/ton below beans, narrowing the beans–sulfate spread. Jinchuan and Russian premiums also shifted, reflecting bearish market sentiment. | SMM reports that SMM 8–12% high-nickel pig iron averaged 978.5 yuan per nickel point, up slightly. Higher Indonesian ore approvals vs March are still far below demand; Indonesian smelter output remains weak, and China supply is low. Weak stainless steel demand limits acceptance, but cost-driven tight circulation supports a stable-to-slightly-firm near-term outlook. | French President Macron visited Nouméa amid clashes between separatists and loyalists in New Caledonia, hinting the state of emergency could be lifted. The unrest is tied to New Caledonia’s nickel-dependent economy and an industry downturn, with stalled rescue talks. Disruption risks likely weigh on nickel prices soon.

news_summary: Copper prices were nearly unchanged as investors reassessed the rally that recently pushed LME prices to record highs, supported by fund buying and a U.S. Comex short squeeze but offset by dampened Chinese physical demand. LME aluminium and nickel fell slightly, indicating limited immediate directional impact beyond copper’s cooling momentum. | Talon Mining begins nickel exploration in Michigan’s Upper Peninsula, backed by about $20 million in U.S. federal funding. The company acquired a large land package and cites prior findings of up to 7.4% nickel, aiming to drill once targets are identified. It frames results for domestic EV-related supply chains. | Reuters reports BHP is racing to secure Anglo American’s copper assets in Chile and Peru, with Anglo investors seeking higher cash/price or a simpler deal structure. The copper mines targeted are expected to produce ~532,000 tons this year, but the dispute is about valuation and execution details, not immediate output changes. | Macron postponed voting reform in New Caledonia after yielding to rioters, in a territory described as nickel-rich. The unrest could disrupt local mining or logistics feeding nickel production, tightening near-term supply expectations. That scenario is generally supportive for nickel prices over the next days to four weeks.

news_summary: Copper prices rose as expectations of stronger Chinese demand improved, supported by new property-sector measures and an IMF upgrade to China’s growth outlook. Gains were capped by a steady/firm U.S. dollar and higher Chinese inventories. Aluminium and nickel also ticked up on the broader metals sentiment. | SPC Nickel reported high-grade channel sampling from its West Graham Ni-Cu project in Sudbury, including results of 0.94% Ni and 0.44% Cu over 17 metres and 0.62% Ni and 0.31% Cu over 38 metres. The company says results correlate with prior drilling and support development potential, but no immediate production impact is stated. | Copper Lake Resources reported winter drilling results from its Marshall Lake copper-zinc-silver VMS property in Ontario, highlighting high-grade copper and zinc intercepts and a persistent EM conductor with the deposit remaining open at depth. The update supports potential resource expansion but does not indicate near-term production. | SGS says it was contracted by The Metals Company to test and optimize onshore processing flowsheets producing high-purity nickel sulfate from seafloor polymetallic nodules. Bench-scale work also targets cobalt sulfate and mentions copper cathode production from nickel matte, supporting future supply expectations for nickel battery chemicals. | LME approved the first Indonesian refined-nickel brand (“DX-zwdx”) for delivery, adding a new source of Class I nickel. With Russia’s Norilsk constrained by sanctions and LME stocks rising to a two-year high, the shift toward Indonesian/Chinese deliverable supply is likely to weigh on nickel prices.

It also mentions Rio Tinto keeping a New Zealand aluminium smelter running with new electricity supply agreements, but overall the tone is bearish for industrial metals. | Copper rebounded on the LME, but gains were capped by profit-taking after May 20 record highs and by weaker physical demand in China. China’s May manufacturing PMI fell below 50. LME aluminium eased, nickel edged up slightly. | Amaroq Minerals reported updated results from South Greenland exploration, confirming a copper-molybdenum porphyry system and identifying 24 additional copper targets for its 2024 field season. news_summary: London copper rose 1.1% on supportive China and US data: China’s private manufacturing survey showed the fastest activity growth in about two years, and US inflation stabilization kept the Fed’s planned rate cuts on track. A tighter China copper import discount suggested improved physical demand, lifting copper and supporting aluminium and nickel. | Cobalt Blue Holdings describes virtual tour coverage of pilot-scale feedstock testing at its technology centre for a planned cobalt-nickel refinery in Australia. The company reports high cobalt extraction recovery and ongoing optimisation toward producing cobalt and nickel sulphate. Policy support for critical minerals is cited, but commercial timelines are not provided. | Nickel 28 Capital Corp. announced it received a management cease trade order from the Ontario Securities Commission after a voluntary application tied to delays in filing audited annual financial statements. The order restricts certain insiders from trading the company’s securities but does not affect non-insider shareholders’ trading. No production impacts are stated. | A Conference Board of Canada outlook says Sudbury’s economy is being weighed by soft nickel prices. It links the fall in nickel to worries about electric-car demand and, importantly, rising Indonesian supply, noting nickel prices are about half of March 2022 levels. Lower prices have hurt Vale and Glencore profits.

The piece also alleges continued nickel ore flows to Chinese processors despite Indonesia’s 2020 export ban. These points could influence near-term nickel supply expectations, but no direct immediate price driver is quantified. | Copper prices fell after a strong rally as weak physical demand in China weighed on the market. Aluminium and nickel also eased, suggesting broadly bearish sentiment in base metals. | Copper prices near a one-month low as real demand fails to match strong electrification-driven expectations.
