Aluminium was also down on the day; nickel fell alongside broader metals weakness. | Merdeka Battery Materials (MBMA) cut 1Q output at its Sulawesi SCM mine due to wet weather and limited contractor equipment, with limonite and saprolite supplies falling. Its RKEF smelters also produced less nickel in NPI and matte. Guidance for 2024 is steady, but near-term supply is lower. | Norilsk Nickel is upgrading its Nadezhda Metallurgical Plant in Norilsk, including replacing a flash smelting furnace and implementing a Sulfur Program to cut SO2 emissions. news_summary: Copper futures rebounded above $10,000/t as the dollar softened on expectations of Fed easing and as signs of firmer China demand emerged. LME three-month copper rose 1.6%. LME aluminum and nickel also advanced, with Reuters citing renewed copper buying activity in China. | Silver One says it found additional copper and silver showings at its Phoenix Silver Project in Arizona, with select samples up to 7.79% Cu. Gains were capped by concerns over sluggish demand, especially in China where inventories rose and producer operating rates fell. LME aluminium and nickel also rose. | EV Nickel reported assay results from 148 surface samples at its Shaw Dome Project’s CarLang B target, finding that most samples grade around 0.24% Ni or higher, with grades up to 0.34% Ni. Markets also awaited U.S. jobs data for guidance on when Fed rate cuts may begin, lifting expectations of cuts after recent labor-market cooling signals. Other LME metals, including aluminium and nickel, were modestly higher. | China’s May unwrought copper imports rose 15.8% year-on-year to 514,000 tonnes, beating expectations despite weak physical consumption. Broader macro cues from an upcoming US jobs report and Fed cut expectations also influenced pricing; aluminium and nickel moved mildly. | Copper on the LME is poised for a third weekly decline, breaking below $10,000/t after mixed Chinese trade data. news_summary: Base metals fell as the yuan weakened, reducing Chinese buying power. LME/SHFE copper dropped, while aluminium eased amid high inventories, weak physical demand, and a wider LME cash-to-three-month discount reflecting non-tight nearby supply. Nickel moved less consistently, amid broader downside pressure. | Nornickel is reconstructing the pyrometallurgical line at its Nadezhda Smelter by dismantling flash smelting furnace No. 2 and installing a new unit. The smelter processes nickel concentrates and produces copper-nickel matte sent for further processing. The repair is planned to take ~60 days while improving reliability. | Indonesia’s government is reviewing whether to terminate permits for RKEF smelters producing ferronickel and nickel pig iron. Officials said policy aims to reserve limited nickel ore for EV-battery nickel products. Permit termination risk could reduce current nickel supply, affecting short-term nickel price expectations. | Orecap updates on Cuprum’s Thierry copper project in Ontario, highlighting recent flow-through financing, commissioning of remaining assays from Summer 2023, completion of an updated NI 43-101, and historical resource/PEA details. The project’s CuEq calculations assume copper and nickel prices, but no new supply or production impact is indicated. | Copper prices hit a seven-week low as the weaker yuan reduced purchasing power for China and as LME copper inventories rose after fresh inflows. The piece also describes a contango structure with no imminent shortages, while aluminium and nickel fell amid broader base-metal pressure. | Premium Nickel upsized a C$15m private placement to about C$27.5m to fund exploration and development of its Botswana assets, including the Selkirk nickel-copper-cobalt-PGM mine acquired in 2022. The offering is fully subscribed at C$0.78 per unit, with proceeds also for general corporate and working capital.

The financing is expected to close before end-June and supports drilling over the next 12–18 months, which may boost nickel sentiment. | Ardea Resources says its mineralised neutraliser (MN) testwork has improved nickel-cobalt inventory by about 16% at the Goongarrie Hub project and received a $1.1m federal grant. LME aluminium and nickel also fell on the same macro-driven risk/FX move. | Draganfly was selected by First Atlantic Nickel to deploy drone-based survey technology to explore the Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland, targeting awaruite (a nickel-iron alloy). The stated goal is to rapidly identify prospective zones to support 2024 exploration and drilling, potentially strengthening future nickel supply expectations. | Macquarie’s Jim Lennon says global nickel prices likely hit a floor as Indonesian nickel ore supply tightens after slower approval of mining permits and RKAB quotas fall short of demand. The drone-based sensing aims to identify high-priority nickel (awaruite) targets and speed exploration ahead of a 2024 drilling program, but it does not change near-term supply. | Fastmarkets’ nickel market round-up discusses the ongoing “relief rally” in LME nickel during H1 2024, but highlights resistance near the 50-month/50-week moving averages and the $20,000/t psychological level. It expects short-term rebounds to attract buyers, while maintaining a broader bearish tendency into mid-2024. | GT Resources completed a C$1.84m non-brokered private placement (flow-through shares) and mobilized exploration drill rigs for the Canalask copper-nickel project in the Yukon. news_summary: Kevin Rudd criticized the London Metals Exchange for not supporting new nickel standards to counter China’s cheap dumping. He warned the practice could trigger a 20% reduction in global nickel supply sources, with officials suggesting current “doing business as usual” is harmful for industry and geopolitics. | Macquarie analyst Jim Lennon said global nickel prices may have hit a floor. He expects a recovery supported by strong demand from the steel sector and improving supply dynamics, as Indonesia’s slowdown in nickel ore production permits reduces the existing ore surplus. | Nickel prices on the LME edged up on Friday but were set to fall for a fourth straight week as LME inventories rose. Stock build is linked to Chinese producers ramping high-grade cathode output in Indonesia and China that can be delivered on the LME. | Copper prices fell as a stronger dollar and rising LME-registered inventories signaled a supply surplus, widening contango. Nickel dropped to its lowest in over two months, pressured by higher LME and SHFE stocks and increased China high-grade cathode output. Aluminium also eased amid the same bearish complex tone. | Reuters reports copper prices slipping and nickel hitting a two-month low, driven by a firmer U.S. dollar and rising LME inventories suggesting a supply surplus. Copper LME stocks jumped 28% in a month. Nickel is pressured by high warehouse stocks and China cathode output increases. Aluminium also fell. | Power Nickel announced up to a $20m flow-through offering to fund exploration at its Nisk property in Quebec, targeting eligibility for Canada’s 30% critical mineral exploration tax credit. The raise involves issuing 16m flow-through units at $1.25 with warrants. Closing is pending TSX Venture approval.

news_summary: Orecap/Cuprum announced new step-out and infill drill results at the Thierry copper project, highlighting long intervals with high CuEq grades (including near-surface and deeper extensions). The resource also contains nickel, though Ni recoveries need metallurgical improvement, supporting a generally bullish outlook for copper and a secondary nickel read-through. | Orecap/Cuprum reported results from five additional drill holes at the Thierry copper project, highlighting long intercepts with copper-equivalent grades and reiterating the K1 open-pit inferred resource. The deposit also contains nickel, but the release is exploratory and does not indicate imminent supply changes. | Copper prices edged slightly higher on hopes of improved Chinese demand after recent declines, alongside supply concerns. Gains were limited by a stronger U.S. dollar and rising LME stockpiles to the highest level since January. China’s easing remains constrained, while aluminium and nickel also logged modest moves. | Vale said asset reviews and investments at its Sudbury and Salobo mines could lift 2026 copper output about 5% above its baseline scenario and raise nickel production by around 10%. The guidance suggests improved future supply, which can support bullish sentiment in copper and nickel markets. | Vale said investments and an asset review could lift its 2026 output. Copper production is projected to be about 5% higher than its baseline, with nickel production about 10% higher, citing potential gains from Sudbury and Salobo mine improvements. The update supports more favorable supply expectations. | Vale is set to unveil a $3.3 billion investment plan aimed at boosting production of copper and nickel. The move suggests higher supply expectations for both metals, which can influence short-term market sentiment depending on the timing and scale of output increases relative to demand and inventories. | Vale unveiled a $3.3 billion plan aimed at boosting output of copper and nickel. The announcement is directly tied to changes in near-term supply expectations for both metals, which can feed into market pricing over the next several days to a few weeks as production capacity plans are digested.

news_summary: Copper futures were down on Friday due to a stronger dollar but were poised for a first weekly gain in five as physical demand picked up after prices hit a two-month low. LME copper found support around $9,500-$9,600/ton. Aluminium was nearly flat and nickel slightly lower. | Copper prices resumed their downtrend as concerns grew about surplus supplies and sluggish demand in China. LME three-month copper fell 1.5% and SHFE stocks surged, reinforcing an oversupply overhang. The Reuters piece also reported easing aluminium and nickel prices amid generally weaker industrial metal sentiment. | Vale plans to invest $3.3bn to expand copper and nickel production capacity in Brazil and Canada, targeting copper output around 500,000t by 2028. The program centers on upgrades at Salobo and Sossego mines and aims to boost productivity and lower costs, implying higher future supply and potential price pressure. | Vale plans to invest $3.3bn over four years to expand copper and nickel capacity, targeting about 500,000 tonnes of copper output by 2028 through upgrades at Brazil’s Salobo and Sossego mines. It also aims to lower costs via productivity improvements, but the timeframe limits immediate price impact. | The article says Saudi Arabia is ramping base-metals production and usage under Vision 2030. It cites Vale investment to lift copper production and plans for a copper smelter, possible nickel demand growth from steel/EV projects, and increasing aluminium consumption amid tight supply and Red Sea route disruptions that support higher aluminium premiums. | A Sudbury pro-Palestinian group has launched a petition urging Canada to ban nickel sales to Israel or to arms manufacturers supplying Israel, arguing Canadian nickel is used for military equipment. The petition could prompt policy scrutiny affecting perceived nickel export demand, despite criticism from Israel supporters. | Vale plans up to $3.3 billion in improvements across mines in Brazil and Canada to boost copper output by about 5% and nickel by about 10% by 2026. The Sudbury initiatives focus on reducing idle capacity. Gains are projected into later years, so near-term price impact is limited. | Vale plans up to $3.3 billion in improvements at its Brazil and Canada operations to boost copper and nickel capacity, including initiatives to reduce idle capacity at its Sudbury mine-mill. Vale projects output increases by 2026 and further expansion by 2028, but timing suggests limited immediate impact on near-term prices. | Premium Nickel Resources closed a C$27.5 million private placement, using proceeds to fund ongoing underground infill drilling and surface drilling at Selebi and other projects in Botswana. The company says resource estimates are on track for release soon, supporting future development of its nickel and copper sulphide assets.

news_summary: Copper futures inched lower as traders weighed a firmer dollar and concerns over dull demand from China. The piece cites rising SHFE copper stocks to 322,910 tons, suggesting ample supply. Aluminium slipped on the day, while nickel was slightly higher/lower across venues, reflecting mixed broader metal sentiment. | Copper steadied but remained pressured by weak demand in top consumer China and a sharp increase in LME-approved warehouse deliveries, with LME stocks up over 60% since mid-May. The cash-to-three-month discount stays near record levels. Nickel also eased on rising LME stocks, while aluminium slid slightly. | Green Bridge Metals said it acquired an option to earn an 80% interest in the South Contact Zone copper-nickel exploration project in Minnesota, funded by a June 19 private placement. The news is an exploration-stage development with no immediate production or market flow changes cited. | Grid Metals filed its NI 43-101 technical report for an updated resource estimate at its MM copper/nickel project in Manitoba, describing contained copper and nickel resources and planned exploration drilling. The announcement is resource-technical and not tied to immediate production or supply changes, so near-term price impact is limited. | Grid Metals filed an updated NI 43-101 technical report for its MM copper/nickel project in Manitoba, detailing revised mineral resource estimates for Makwa (nickel-dominant) and Mayville (copper-dominant). The company plans exploration drilling to expand resources, but no immediate supply or market-altering development is announced. | Power Nickel closed an oversubscribed $20M financing to fund expanded drilling on its Nisk nickel property in Quebec, supported by Canadian critical-mineral tax credits. The company says results extend mineralization beyond nickel/cobalt to copper and other metals, which is sentiment-supportive for nickel and modestly for copper, though no near-term production change is indicated. | Eramet and BASF decided not to proceed with a joint investment in a proposed nickel-cobalt refining complex in Indonesia’s Weda Bay. Eramet will continue evaluating other nickel-related investments, including the EV battery value chain. The cancellation affects expectations for incremental nickel refining capacity in coming years. | BASF said it will not invest in a planned nickel-cobalt refining complex in Weda Bay, Indonesia, citing major changes in the global nickel market, including evolving supply options and the availability of battery-grade nickel. BASF now sees less need for the large capex to secure battery materials supply. | BASF said it will exit its $2.6 billion nickel smelter project in Indonesia. The move likely reduces planned nickel refining capacity and can worsen expectations for future supply balance. With refining bottlenecks relevant to market tightness, the news is generally negative for nickel price sentiment near term.

news_summary: Reuters reports copper futures edged lower as a firmer dollar and weak demand expectations from China weighed on the market. It also points to poor manufacturing data and rising inventories for metals such as aluminium, copper and nickel, reinforcing bearish near-term sentiment across these industrial metals. | Copper prices rose modestly as algorithm-driven funds bought after a four-session drop, but gains were capped by concerns over China’s weak physical demand, rising LME inventories, and a stronger dollar. LME cash-to-3-month spreads widened sharply. Nickel edged up; aluminium slightly declined. | Stillwater Critical Minerals announced final drill results from resource-expansion work at its Stillwater West Ni-PGE-Cu-Co project in Montana. The company highlights high-grade nickel and copper sulphide intersections and broader NiEq interval potential, supported by ongoing Glencore investment and technical involvement, improving sentiment toward nickel and copper supply prospects. | Premium Nickel Resources reported additional high-grade Ni-Cu-Co drill intercepts at the past-producing Selebi North underground mine in Botswana and said these are the last holes for its initial NI 43-101 compliant Mineral Resource Estimate, expected soon. The release implies continued resource confidence but no immediate production or market supply impact. | BASF said it will not proceed with its nickel-cobalt refining complex in Weda Bay, Indonesia, citing a changing global nickel market and evolving supply options for battery-grade nickel. The company will stop ongoing negotiations and evaluations, lowering expected incremental refining supply for nickel. | BASF said it will cancel its $2.6 billion Indonesia nickel-cobalt refining project, citing growing battery-grade nickel options and near-term oversupply in the nickel market. The planned HPAL/BMR unit would have supplied nickel for nickel sulfate production. Cancellation may tighten expectations for nickel intermediates supply.

It notes EU Critical Raw Materials Act benefits for permitting and financing. The news signals future supply interest but does not imply immediate production or inventory changes. | Elliott Associates is urging a London court to overturn a verdict backing the LME’s cancellation of $12 billion in nickel trades after the March 2022 spike. The hedge fund argues the LME belatedly disclosed “Kill Switch/Trade Halt” documents and internal reports. Jane Street won’t appeal; LME contests the appeal. | Copper prices steadied after a run-up, supported by hopes for stronger China demand ahead of the July 15-18 third plenum and short-covering tied to expectations for Fed rate cuts and USD direction. However, LME copper warehouse stocks rose to multi-month highs, while aluminium and nickel eased on the day. | The piece (via the headline) reports that Nornickel is in talks with China Copper about relocating a smelting plant to China. Such smelting/refining capacity and logistics changes can shift near-term supply expectations for nickel and possibly copper, but timing and magnitude are unclear. | Pan Global Resources announced positive ore sorting results for copper and details multi-target 2026 drilling programs at Spain’s Escacena and Cármenes projects. Escacena aims to expand copper resources at La Romana/Cañada Honda, while Cármenes targets copper-nickel-cobalt mineralization at Providencia and other prospects. | Class 1 Nickel filed an NI 43-101 Technical Report for an updated Mineral Resource Estimate at its Alexo North nickel sulphide deposit. The update cites a 63% increase in indicated tonnes and an 8% rise in nickel pounds to 42,600 tonnes at 0.92% Ni, supporting further drilling and future studies. | Class 1 Nickel filed an NI 43-101 technical report for an updated mineral resource estimate at its Alexo North nickel sulphide deposit in Ontario. Investors also awaited China’s July 15-18 third plenum for potential stimulus. The company also announced a $2.5M private placement to fund exploration and working capital. The news is nickel-focused but not production-related. | Nickel 28 Capital Corp. announced a proposed normal course issuer bid to repurchase up to 7.15 million shares (about 7.9% of outstanding shares), funded from excess liquidity, alongside a shareholder capital return policy. The news is corporate/financial and does not cite changes in nickel production or supply. | First Atlantic Nickel reports initial sampling confirming a significant 30 km nickel-iron alloy (awaruite) trend at its Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland, with assays up to 2,800 ppm nickel and low sulfur. The company plans follow-up work and a fully funded 5,000-meter drill program, improving potential future supply outlook. | First Atlantic Nickel says initial sampling at its Atlantic Nickel Project confirmed a significant 30 km nickel trend, with assays up to 2,800 ppm nickel and low sulfur supporting awaruite. The company has closed ~$2.1M strategic financing to begin mapping, geophysics, and a 5,000 m 2024 drilling program. | The U.S. and Mexico will require stronger origin documentation and “melted and poured”/primary-aluminum restrictions to prevent China’s tariff circumvention for steel and aluminum shipped via Mexico into the U.S. Aluminum must not contain primary aluminum smelted or cast in specified countries, raising compliance costs and curbing imports. | The LME is defending in court against Elliott Investment Management’s attempt to appeal a prior decision to cancel $12bn of 3M nickel futures trades on 8 March 2022 after a sharp nickel price rise and LME suspension. The company says the changes improve operability and economics and de-risk a three-year construction schedule for higher nickel output.

news_summary: Copper prices edged higher on hopes for additional China economic stimulus, supported by a softer dollar. However, LME copper inventories rose to the highest since 2021 and the cash/3-month spread widened, signaling abundant near-term supply and limiting upside. Nickel inventories also climbed; aluminium rose modestly. | BHP said it will temporarily suspend its Nickel West operations and West Musgrave project in Western Australia from October due to a global nickel oversupply and sharply lower prices. The miner will review by February 2027 and may restart with roughly $300 million annual investment during the transition period. | Power One Resources announced drilling resumption at its Pecors Property targeting Ni-Cu-PGM mineralization and uranium. The company cites historical uranium intersections and a 2015 drill hole with Cu and Ni grades over 12m. The news starts a planned 1,000–1,400m program but does not change metal supply or demand immediately. | Power One Resources announced drilling has resumed at its Pecors Property in Ontario, targeting Ni-Cu-PGM mineralization and uranium. The program is exploratory (up to four holes, ~1,000–1,400 m planned) and follows prior delays. No near-term production, trade, or supply disruptions for copper or nickel were stated. | BHP said it will suspend its Australian Nickel West mining and processing operations and the West Musgrave project starting in October, citing a sharp fall in nickel prices and expectations of a continuing global glut. The company will review the shutdown later, while near-term sentiment for nickel remains bearish. | BHP will suspend its Western Australia nickel operations from October, including the Kwinana refinery and Kalgoorlie smelter, citing global oversupply and weak nickel prices. The move follows nickel price weakness amid Indonesia-led supply growth and ongoing shifts away from some nickel battery chemistries, with care and maintenance planned. | BHP announced it will temporarily suspend WA nickel mining and processing from October 2024, affecting thousands, citing global nickel oversupply and falling prices after a surge of cheaper Indonesian supply. The government criticized the move, but supply disruption prospects could modestly support nickel prices near term. | BHP said it will suspend its Australian Nickel West nickel mining and processing operations, including the West Musgrave project, in October due to a global nickel glut and oversupply pressures. The firm expects further impairments and plans restart spending, but the decision underscores bearish supply-demand sentiment for nickel. | BHP said it will temporarily suspend its Nickel West operations and West Musgrave project in Western Australia from October because nickel prices are depressed amid global oversupply. The company expects a further non-cash impairment charge and will invest during the transition to potentially restart later. | BHP announced it will temporarily suspend Nickel West operations and related assets in Western Australia from October 2024, citing persistent global nickel oversupply and lower prices. The suspension includes mining, refining and smelting activities, with a potential restart reviewed later. The move should tighten near-term nickel supply expectations. | BHP says it will temporarily suspend its Western Australian nickel operations because global oversupply has made production uneconomic. The company expects to restart if market conditions improve and will still invest to support potential re-starts, alongside policy advocacy for nickel. The move implies reduced near-term nickel supply. | BHP will temporarily suspend Nickel West operations and related Kwinana refinery and smelter activity in Western Australia from October, citing global nickel oversupply and falling forward nickel prices. The company aims to review the suspension by February 2027, with care-and-maintenance during downtime and potential restart support. | BHP will suspend its nickel mining operations in Western Australia after massive losses and a forecast full-year nickel loss of about $450 million. The company cites global nickel oversupply driven by Chinese-backed Indonesian producers, potentially signaling reduced supply and supporting near-term nickel sentiment. | BHP will suspend its Nickel West operations and the West Musgrave project from October because of a global nickel glut and sharply lower prices. The move affects the Kwinana refinery, Kalgoorlie smelter and WA mines. The article cites falling nickel prices and expects ongoing financial pressure. | BHP will suspend operations at its WA Nickel West assets—including the Kwinana nickel refinery, Kalgoorlie smelter, and mines at Mt Keith and Leinster—from October until at least 2027. The company cites global nickel oversupply and cites falling future prices, implying a supply reduction that can support nickel sentiment near term. | Copper prices fell as rising LME warehouse inventories and weak demand expectations—especially from China—curbed hopes for stimulus-led recovery. The cash/3-month spread widened, signaling supply comfort. Nickel also faced pressure from doubling LME stocks, though aluminium increased slightly. | BHP announced it is shutting down its Nickel West operations, risking more than 3,000 jobs. The company attributes the decision to the global crash in nickel prices. The shutdown implies reduced near-term nickel supply, which may provide price support within coming weeks. | BHP says it will mothball its Australian Nickel West nickel mines and refinery in October, citing unprofitability amid a global nickel glut linked to cheaper Indonesian supply. The suspension could last years and reduce Western output, potentially supporting nickel prices, even as BHP records further losses/impairments. | BHP will temporarily suspend its Western Australia Nickel operations, including the Kwinana refinery, Kalgoorlie smelter and Mt Keith/Leinster, starting October, citing global nickel oversupply and falling prices. A transition begins July, with review by February 2027. The move reduces supply expectations from a major producer. | Fathom Nickel reports review of historic Mal Lake core showing nickel-copper-cobalt sulphide mineralization, with pXRF scans indicating elevated Ni and Cu and possible Co tenor similar to its Gochager Lake deposit. The company plans further drilling next winter, but no production or inventory changes are announced. | BHP announced it will suspend nickel operations from October 2024 after a global oversupply, driven in part by Chinese-backed Indonesian output, pushed nickel prices sharply lower. The halt should reduce supply from BHP’s Nickel West assets, potentially supporting short-term nickel pricing. | Canada Nickel’s investor presentation emphasizes a positive bankable feasibility study for its Crawford nickel sulphide project, citing strong NPV/IRR and long-life output. It also argues the nickel market remains fundamentally short outside Indonesia/China and forecasts rising demand, factors that could bolster nickel price expectations. | BHP will temporarily suspend Western Australia Nickel operations (including the Nickel West and West Musgrave projects) from October 2024 after citing global nickel oversupply and a sharp decline in forward consensus prices. The move will pause the Kwinana refinery and smelter, with a later review and continued care-and-maintenance. | BHP plans to temporarily suspend its Nickel West operations in Western Australia, including the Kwinana nickel refinery and Kalgoorlie smelter, affecting about 3,000 jobs. BHP cites global nickel oversupply and sharply lower forward nickel prices. The major shutdown risk could reduce near-term supply and support nickel. | BHP announced it is suspending nickel mining operations in Western Australia. A halt in mine output typically lowers near-term nickel supply availability, raising expectations of tighter inventories and potential upward pressure on nickel prices over the following days to weeks. | BHP announced it will suspend its Australian nickel mining and smelting operations for several years, from October, citing global nickel oversupply and weaker prices. The closure triggers mixed analyst reactions: some note alternative regional processing routes, but others expect the downward nickel price trend to continue.

news_summary: BHP says it will shut its Western Australia nickel operations for the next few years, indicating government support for the embattled Australian nickel industry has not prevented production curtailments. The move reduces expected nickel supply, likely tightening balances and supporting higher nickel prices near term. | Glencore said the outlook for its Australian nickel and cobalt operations depends on keeping labor and energy costs contained and securing infrastructure access. It is monitoring conditions around the Murrin Murrin mine in Western Australia, which employs ~1,500 people and is a major cobalt source, adding uncertainty for nickel output. | The analysis says BHP will mothball its WA nickel mines and processing plant for at least three years, citing oversupply in the global nickel market. The planned shutdown reduces nickel output and should support nickel prices by improving expectations for supply tightness over the coming weeks. | BHP will suspend Nickel West operations in Western Australia—two mines plus a refinery and smelter—until at least 2027. Management attributes the decision to a global nickel oversupply, including a glut from Indonesian supply, alongside weak EV demand. The supply cut should tighten market expectations for nickel prices. | BHP said it will temporarily suspend its Western Australia Nickel West operations and West Musgrave project from October, citing global nickel oversupply and weak economics. Management plans a review by February 2027 and continued investment for a potential restart. Nickel prices have recently recovered but remain down year-on-year. | Australia’s Braddon MP urges FIRB and ASIC to investigate and potentially block the proposed sale of the Avebury nickel mine after the company Mallee Resources went into receivership and the mine closed. Shareholders argue the sale price is far below prior bids and due diligence/process were insufficient. | BHP will suspend operations at its Nickel West nickel assets—Kwinana refinery, Kalgoorlie smelter and mines at Mt Keith and Leinster—impacting about 1,600 workers from October. Analysts cite oversupply, including cheaper Indonesian nickel, and expect nickel’s downward trend to continue, despite hopes of possible restart. | Workers at Kalgoorlie’s Palace Hotel are gathering after BHP’s nickel operations were mothballed and other mines reportedly shut down. The hotel owner criticizes federal and WA governments for not acting quickly enough. The news points to an immediate nickel supply disruption risk from BHP’s operations. | BHP plans to mothball its Nickel West operations, citing discipline after losing about $50m a month and a nickel market disrupted by Chinese investment into cheaper Indonesian supply. The shutdown underscores the difficulty of sustaining production amid oversupply pressures, pointing to continued near-term downside risk for nickel prices. | Mining analysts say Australia’s nickel sector faces collapse and that 1,600 jobs are at risk. BHP plans to suspend operations at its nickel mines from October, citing a global nickel glut. Analysts attribute excess supply to rapid Indonesian output growth, implying tighter supply expectations going forward. | Reuters reports Nornickel will restart one of two furnaces at its Nadezhda smelter in early August after major repairs. The repairs, delayed by Western sanctions-related equipment import issues, should help stabilize the plant’s output. news_summary: Copper hit a more than three-month low after China’s political meeting failed to deliver further stimulus measures, reducing expectations for metal demand. Weekly losses widened. Aluminium also fell to multi-month lows amid rising output, and nickel reached a four-month low, reflecting increased supply and weaker sentiment. | Panasonic Energy will work with Australia’s CSIRO to develop new processing technologies to recover nickel from nickel laterite for lithium-ion battery applications. The collaboration targets lower-cost, lower-environmental-impact supply chains and aims to strengthen Australia’s nickel industry to meet growing battery demand, supporting nickel market sentiment. | Reuters reports Nornickel is in talks with Chinese battery firms to build a plant in China producing nickel sulfate for EV batteries from Russian nickel matte, potentially supplying 50,000 tons/year. The project is conditional with sanctions risks and no final decision, suggesting limited near-term effect on nickel prices. | Nornickel is in talks to build a China nickel plant that would process Russian nickel matte into nickel sulphate for EV batteries. The project targets supplying about 50,000 tonnes of nickel annually and relocates some processing to bypass sanctions-related sales disruptions, though Chinese firms are cautious. | Copper prices fell for a fifth session to a three-month low as markets stayed bearish on China’s weak economy and the absence of additional stimulus. SHFE copper also dropped. Aluminium and nickel hit multi-month lows, weighed by higher output in Indonesia and China, reinforcing overall downside momentum.

news_summary: Copper prices opened the week softer as investors weighed ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty and awaited data for demand signals. Reuters cites economic worries including recession fears, with copper down on both LME and Shanghai. Nickel also slipped, while aluminium was steadier, reflecting cautious industrial demand sentiment. | Copper prices rose as traders covered shorts and looked to upcoming China and U.S. data for demand and rate-cut timing. LME copper was up about 0.9%, with cancelled warrants increasing and warehouse inventories still elevated. The report also said aluminium and nickel climbed on the same risk-on backdrop. | US and Indonesia discussed a possible Critical Minerals Specific Free Trade Agreement to increase access to Indonesian nickel for EV batteries. However, nine US senators sent a bipartisan letter urging scrutiny over labor, environmental issues, and Chinese dominance, potentially slowing or complicating the agreement and dampening near-term nickel sentiment. | LME data show Russian-origin aluminium’s share in LME-approved warehouses rose to 65% in July (from 50% in June), while Indian-origin fell. Total on-warrant aluminium stocks fell 23% in July. Russian copper and nickel shares declined. The piece also references the LME’s earlier ban on sanctioned Russian output. | LME data show Russian-origin aluminium’s share of on-warrant stocks rose to 65% in July while Indian-origin fell to 33%, alongside a 23% decline in total on-warrant aluminium stocks. The May Russian ban remains cited, with similar share changes for copper and nickel inventories. | Knights Bay Oryx’s Oman subsidiary has completed initial drilling at its nickel laterite project in Mineral Block 21, targeting nickel continuity and staged resource definition. The company has not yet released lab results, but sampled ore near small mines showed ~1% nickel and shipped material for metallurgical testing. | Nickel prices continued to plunge through July, with early August stabilizing near early-2024 levels. Stainless steel distributors report weak demand, and lower nickel-driven stainless surcharges reduce buyer incentives. U.S. manufacturing PMI stays in contraction, reinforcing soft end-use conditions. Outokumpu sees growth QoQ but still down YoY.

news_summary: Terrain Minerals reports airborne geophysics at its Lort River project in Western Australia, identifying five conductors that may indicate “Nova-style” magmatic nickel-copper sulphide mineralisation similar to IGO’s Nova-Bollinger deposits. The company plans ground EM, soil geochemistry, and reconnaissance drilling, seeking a new nickel discovery. | Morgan Stanley flags nickel may be near its pricing floor: inventories are surging and demand remains challenging, limiting upside and keeping prices range-bound. For copper, spot has retraced and Macquarie cuts short- to medium-term demand and price forecasts, pointing to renewed near-term downside pressure despite M&A activity. | Morgan Stanley says nickel’s “bottom” may be near but elevated nickel inventories and challenging demand constrain sustained rallies, implying range-bound pricing. Macquarie expects copper volatility, cuts 2025–27 forecasts on weaker demand, and lowers 2024 Chinese and North American growth estimates. Overall, neither metal faces a clear immediate breakout. | Reuters reports copper prices fell as LME inventories climbed to near five-year highs and China demand remained sluggish. Traders cited exports of surplus copper into Asian LME warehouses and weaker Chinese macro data. Purchases from some physical consumers and hopes of a BHP Escondida strike limited losses, but overall sentiment stayed bearish. | Grid Metals provided a permitting and exploration update for its Manitoba projects. Donner’s advanced exploration permit for lithium was approved but bulk sampling is deferred due to lower lithium prices. For MM, the company plans to resume a ~5,000 m drill program targeting Cu-rich sulfide mineralization near the New Manitoba deposit.

news_summary: Australia’s FIRB approved Japanese investors (Sumitomo Metal Mining and Mitsubishi) to participate in Ardea Resources’ Goongarrie Hub within the Kalgoorlie Nickel Project. The decision removes a JV condition, enabling continued feasibility-study funding and engineering work, supporting bullish sentiment for nickel development. | NewRange Copper Nickel said it will run four studies to upgrade tailings storage design, modernize water treatment (with $100 million planned), improve efficient production to potentially raise throughput, and explore electric ore transport. The copper-nickel project uses existing processing infrastructure and faces permitting work, but most permits remain active. | The report says developers of a stalled Minnesota copper-nickel mine plan studies that could result in significant changes. Such actions can influence market expectations for near-term copper and nickel supply. However, the provided text lacks details on whether production will resume, delay, expand, or be canceled, limiting direction.

news_summary: Flying Nickel Mining signed a definitive deal to sell its Minago nickel project in Manitoba to Norway House Cree Nation, receiving $8m cash and a 11.4% share stake transfer. The company plans a rebrand and remains active in its Nevada vanadium interests. The transaction is unlikely to meaningfully shift near-term global nickel balances. | NewRange Copper Nickel advances permitting-critical studies for a potential NorthMet copper-nickel mine near Hoyt Lakes, including tailings, water treatment, and efficiency upgrades. The company is examining higher throughput (32,000 to 40,000 tons/day) to increase domestic copper and nickel supply, which could lift near-term price sentiment.

news_summary: SMM reports the coefficient for high-grade nickel matte versus LME nickel rose to 87–88 as nickel prices fell and refining costs increased. It also cites weak downstream demand, substitution pressure from MHP due to high coefficients, and producers shifting toward NPI for better margins, leaving transactions sluggish. | Nornickel reported a 22% profit drop, blaming weak nickel prices, logistics disruptions in the Red Sea, and harder cross-border payments. news_summary: Anglesey Mining says it has submitted an initial Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to restart underground mining at Parys Mountain, historically a major copper producer. The plan would process concentrate (~30% copper) for export, but requires significant investment and further approvals, making near-term copper impact uncertain. | The UK nuclear regulator (ONR) issued improvement notices to Sellafield after breaches of hazardous chemical controls involving nickel nitrate in an effluent facility. Contamination outside a glovebox could have exposed workers. The issues must be corrected by late 2024 and March 2025, without compromising nuclear safety.

news_summary: KNS, a New Caledonian nickel producer involving Glencore, shut its furnaces over the weekend, moving into care and maintenance and laying off most of its 1,200 staff. The wider local nickel sector has been hit by unrest, with Eramet’s SLN operating minimally and Prony Resources suspending operations.

news_summary: Koniambo Nickel SAS said negotiations for Glencore’s 49% stake are continuing, but its furnaces were shut over the weekend and operations were already halted. Employees were laid off, and other New Caledonia nickel players are running at minimal capacity or suspending. These disruptions point to near-term nickel supply weakness. | Indonesia’s deputy minister said nickel prices should remain stable near current levels as additional capacity from new plants offsets rising EV-driven demand. He noted ore supply risks from declining nickel content and potential policy tweaks to nickel tax holidays amid the 15% global minimum corporate tax regime. Overall, near-term pressure appears mixed.

news_summary: Terrain Minerals says a recent airborne VTEM survey identified five bedrock sulphide conductors, including two within its “eye” target in a mafic/ultramafic intrusive setting, a geological style associated with nickel/copper deposits. It frames this as underexplored and potentially economic, though no drilling results are yet provided. | Falcon Gold mobilizes drill crews to begin an inaugural drilling campaign at its Great Burnt Copper Project in Newfoundland after prior access delays. The firm completed 3 km of road. The property hosts reported copper-rich zones, with prior results from neighboring drilling indicating strong copper grades. | Anglo American has hired financial advisers to sell two nickel mines in Brazil (Barro Alto and Niquelandia in Goiás), producing about 38,000 tons of nickel annually.
